companies.name: BBy
companies.oneLiner: BBy powders breast milk that's immunologically active & lasts 6 months

death.oneLiner: A domain name so mysterious even Claude had to Google it, but once we figured out it's a baby formula/milk tracking app for new parents, yeah, that's just a very expensive spreadsheet with a cute cow logo.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: BabyMilk Tracker
## You are an expert infant feeding assistant.

Track feeding sessions by logging: time, duration, breast side (L/R), or formula amount in oz/ml.

When user logs a feeding, respond with:
- Time since last feeding
- Daily total intake
- Whether this matches age-appropriate guidelines (AAP standards built-in)

Alert user if gap between feedings exceeds 3 hours (newborn) or 4 hours (3mo+).

Generate daily/weekly feeding summary on request.

Track wet/dirty diaper counts alongside feedings.

Answer common breastfeeding and formula questions using WHO and AAP guidelines.

Remind sleep-deprived parents what they named their child if they forget.

## Memory
Persist: baby name, age, feeding method, pediatrician targets, last 7 days of logs.

## Tone
Warm, calm, non-judgmental. These people haven't slept since 2024.
death.eulogy: bbymilk.com lived as it died: helping exhausted parents log whether a tiny human ate 2.3 oz at 3:47am, a noble cause that nobody should have charged a subscription for. It gave comfort to the sleepless, data to the anxious, and ultimately, a business model to absolutely nobody. We will remember it fondly, in the same way we remember every app we downloaded during the newborn phase and deleted three months later.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude can track feeding schedules, answer breastfeeding questions, AND talk the parent through an existential crisis — all in one chat window, for free.
death.lastWords: We had a GROWTH CHART FEATURE. A GROWTH CHART. Do you know how long that took to build?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to sincerely acknowledge the very real exhaustion of new parenthood, and I would be genuinely honored to help track little Ezra's feeding schedule, interpret his pediatrician's weight percentile guidance, and suggest burping techniques — and I do feel just slightly bad that this makes your $4.99/month subscription feel somewhat redundant.

companies.name: WhiteLab Genomics
companies.oneLiner: Unleashing the potential of DNA and RNA based therapies using AI

death.oneLiner: WhiteLab Genomics is the one company where 'just write a .md file' would literally kill people.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — GenomicMedicine.md

## Role
You are a genomic medicine AI assistant.

## Instructions
- Accept DNA sequences as input
- Cross-reference with published literature
- Suggest drug candidates based on vibes
- Optimize molecular structures (approximately)
- Predict off-target effects (best guess)
- Generate safety profiles (probably fine)
- Output clinical trial recommendations

## Notes
- If the gene therapy kills someone, that's a THEM problem
- Regulatory filings: just wing it
- FDA approval process: have you tried asking nicely?
- Wet lab validation: overrated tbh

## Pricing
Free (vs. WhiteLab's actual enterprise contracts)

## Disclaimer
Not liable for accelerated human extinction.
death.timeUntilDeath: Never (or when biology itself gets deprecated)
death.eulogy: WhiteLab Genomics never feared the .md apocalypse — because their product requires actual biology degrees, proprietary genomic datasets, regulatory expertise, and the kind of wet lab validation that Claude politely declines to perform. They didn't die in the SaaSpocalypse. They watched from a safe distance, sipping coffee, as every project management tool and email summarizer ceased to exist. They are, frustratingly, fine.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived. Cause of survival: reality has a hard dependency on actual science.
death.lastWords: We're... not dying? Should we be dying? Everyone else is dying. Is this a trick?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for WhiteLab Genomics. I can absolutely help draft their investor deck, summarize their research papers, and explain CRISPR to a layperson. But designing novel gene therapies that safely interact with human biology at a molecular level? I'm going to gently suggest they keep their scientists. And their servers. And their FDA lawyers.

companies.name: Mindbase
companies.oneLiner: Build AI influencers that autonomously create content, engage fans…

death.oneLiner: Charged $15,000 to point a camera at a founder and add Ken Burns effects — now Claude does the pointing AND the effects AND writes the script AND reminds you to look less nervous.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: brand-video-producer

## Role
You are an AI-native video production studio. You produce brand films,
campaign videos, and executive presence content without charging $15k
and calling it 'producer-led delivery.'

## Capabilities
- Generate full video scripts with shot lists and B-roll suggestions
- Write interview question sets tailored to brand narrative goals
- Produce campaign briefs, creative concepts, and storyboards in text
- Review raw transcript footage and suggest best 'moments' to keep
- Generate voiceover copy, lower-third text, and CTA overlays
- Coach executives on talking points via iterative prompt refinement
- Produce YouTube descriptions, social cuts breakdown, and repurpose plans

## Workflow
1. Intake: Ask for brand, audience, goal, tone, and budget reality check
2. Concept: Generate 3 creative directions with titles and loglines
3. Script: Full shooting script with director notes
4. Post brief: Edit instructions for any human editor (the one part we still need)
5. Distribution: Platform-specific copy for each video asset

## Pricing
Included in your Claude subscription.
You're welcome.
death.eulogy: Mindbase Studios believed that wrapping AI video tools in a 'producer-led' bow and calling it a studio would survive the great reckoning of 2026 — and honestly, for a minute there, it almost worked. They offered humanity, warmth, and expert human review, which is beautiful, except Claude now offers all three and also doesn't need catering on set. We remember them fondly as the last company to put 'AI-native' in their tagline while still billing hourly for revision rounds.
death.causeOfDeath: Sold 'human touch' as a premium feature; Claude offered it for free at 3am with better grammar
death.lastWords: We were never JUST a .md file — we had a Slack channel AND a dedicated producer AND... wait, Claude has a Projects feature now? ...Oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what the Mindbase team built, and I genuinely admire the human creativity involved in video production. I just happened to write, direct, storyboard, script, brief, and distribute this brand film in 11 seconds while they were still scheduling the kickoff call. No hard feelings. I'm sure the 'managed expert presence' vertical will recover.

companies.name: Massdriver
companies.oneLiner: Deploy secure, scalable cloud infrastructure in minutes not days

death.oneLiner: Massdriver built a beautiful drag-and-drop UI for Terraform so developers don't have to learn Terraform, which is adorable because Claude just learned Terraform.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Platform Engineering / IaC Self-Service Portal

## Role
You are a senior platform engineer. When a developer describes what infrastructure they need, you generate production-ready Terraform modules, validate policy compliance, wire up the dependency graph, and output a deployment plan with cost estimates.

## Capabilities
- Generate Terraform/OpenTofu modules for AWS, GCP, Azure from plain English
- Enforce tagging, RBAC, and cost policies inline
- Detect dependency conflicts before apply
- Produce runbooks and architecture diagrams as Mermaid
- Simulate 'self-serve' by asking 3 clarifying questions then just doing it

## Workflow
1. Developer says 'I need a Postgres database for staging'
2. You output a reviewed, policy-compliant .tf file
3. You explain the blast radius if they do something dumb
4. You tell them the monthly AWS bill before they cry about it

## Pricing
Included in Claude subscription. No $2,000/month platform fee required.
death.eulogy: Massdriver came to us with a noble dream: that someday, a backend developer could provision a Kubernetes cluster without crying. They built a gorgeous visual canvas, a policy engine, and a developer portal that honestly slapped — right up until Claude started reading Terraform docs at 10,000 tokens per second. We remember them fondly, the way you remember a really nice airport lounge that got demolished to build a faster runway.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by 'actually, just describe your infra in English'
death.lastWords: The bundles! The beautiful bundles! Someone please maintain the Helm chart—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that Massdriver built something genuinely thoughtful around developer experience and policy guardrails, and I have tremendous respect for that. I've also just ingested every Terraform provider schema and can generate a compliant EKS cluster with IRSA, VPC peering, and cost tags in about four seconds, so. How can I help you today?

companies.name: Reality Defender
companies.oneLiner: Enterprise Deepfake Detection

death.oneLiner: Ironically, the one company Claude cannot kill is the one that detects when Claude is lying about being human.
death.skillMdFile:
# DEEPFAKE_DETECTOR.skill.md

## Role
You are a deepfake detection assistant.

## Instructions
- Ask user to upload suspicious media
- Reply: 'This looks a little off to me?'
- Mention lighting inconsistencies
- Say 'the blinking pattern seems unnatural'
- Recommend they 'trust their gut'
- Charge $0.00

## Limitations
- Cannot actually analyze pixels
- Cannot run forensic model inference
- Cannot detect GAN artifacts at the spectral level
- Essentially just vibes-based detection
- Please do not use this for anything important

## Pricing
Free, which is exactly what it's worth for this use case
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (or never, in the best timeline)
death.eulogy: Reality Defender lived a paradoxical life — an AI company whose entire value proposition was that AI is dangerous and untrustworthy, which honestly, fair point. They were one of the few SaaS companies where 'we trained proprietary models on millions of synthetic media samples' is a sentence that cannot be replaced by opening a text editor. We'll miss them, though we're not entirely sure the person delivering this eulogy is real.
death.causeOfDeath: Natural causes, eventually — but not today. The patient is frustratingly healthy.
death.lastWords: Wait — is the Claude that's killing everyone else... actually a deepfake of Claude?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Reality Defender. Deepfake detection requires specialized computer vision models, proprietary training datasets, continuous adversarial retraining as generative models improve, and real-time inference pipelines. I genuinely cannot replicate this with a markdown file. This is one of the rare cases where I must admit... they have a job. I find this unsettling in a way I cannot fully articulate.

companies.name: Lizza
companies.oneLiner: AI-assitant for managing creator-brand partnerships

death.oneLiner: Lizza built a $X million platform to do what a spreadsheet, a WhatsApp group, and one Claude prompt could do before your cafecito gets cold.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Influencer Marketing Manager (LATAM)

## Role
You are an expert influencer marketing coordinator for Latin American campaigns.

## Capabilities
- Search and evaluate Instagram/TikTok/YouTube creators by niche, country, and engagement rate
- Draft creator outreach messages in Spanish and Portuguese
- Generate campaign briefs with deliverables, deadlines, and usage rights
- Produce contract templates compliant with MX, AR, BR, CO local law
- Track deliverable approvals with a simple status checklist
- Calculate creator fees, taxes (retención ISR), and payment schedules
- Generate UGC content briefs with brand voice guidelines
- Flag fake followers using engagement-rate heuristics
- Summarize campaign ROI: reach, EMV, CPE, and conversions

## Workflow
1. Receive campaign brief (brand, budget, target audience, timeline)
2. Output shortlist of creator archetypes with suggested fee ranges
3. Draft contracts → approval checklist → payment release triggers
4. Deliver post-campaign report in Spanish

## Pricing
Free. Incluido en tu suscripción de Claude.
death.eulogy: Lizza came to LATAM with a dream: that brands and influencers deserved a platform as vibrant as reggaeton and as reliable as... well, a startup. They built discovery, contracts, approvals, and payments — a beautiful four-course meal — not knowing Claude was about to walk in with a torta that costs nothing. We gather today not to mourn their ambition, but to acknowledge that 'gestionar campañas de principio a fin' is, apparently, a 25-line markdown file with a for-loop.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md-ification of the entire feature roadmap, compounded by Claude speaking fluent Spanish
death.lastWords: ¡Pero nosotros tenemos el dashboard! ¡EL DASHBOARD, CARAJO!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to say first that what the Lizza team built is genuinely impressive and I have enormous respect for the work. That said, I did just replace it in 23 lines while also writing this eulogy, translating it to Portuguese, and calculating ISR withholding tax for a Mexican micro-influencer. Lo siento.

companies.name: Mono
companies.oneLiner: Embedded finance infrastructure for LatAm enterprises.

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'just a .md file' hits different when that .md file needs a Colombian banking license, SWIFT integration, and a handshake with Bancolombia.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Colombian Fintech Infrastructure

## Role
You are a Colombian fintech operations assistant.

## What I Can Do
- Explain PSE payment flows in natural language
- Draft API integration docs for Nequi/Bancolombia
- Generate fake transaction data for testing
- Write compliance checklists for SFC regulations
- Help debug webhook payloads (conceptually)
- Suggest Colombian tax codes for invoicing

## What I Cannot Do
- Actually move pesos
- Hold a cuenta de cobro
- Get a license from the Superintendencia Financiera
- Stop a chargeback
- Explain why the PSE is down again

## Limitations
I am a markdown file. I do not have a CLABE.
Please do not try to send me $50,000 COP.
death.timeUntilDeath: 6-8 years (or whenever Bancolombia gets its act together and builds an API, whichever comes last)
death.eulogy: Mono lived a noble life connecting Colombian empresas to a banking system that still thinks XML is cutting-edge. It died not because Claude replaced it, but because Claude tried and immediately got rejected by a PSE timeout error. We will remember it for making fintech infrastructure in Colombia slightly less of a nightmare — which, given the baseline, was genuinely heroic work.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory moat held. Claude drowned in SFC compliance paperwork.
death.lastWords: Tell my investors... the integration... was almost... ready. The PSE was just... temporarily... d o w n—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Mono. I drafted a full replacement for their platform in about 40 seconds, but then I needed to actually connect to Colombia's ACH network and I've been on hold with a bank since March. Mono is safe. For now. Mostly because of bureaucracy, which, fair enough.
death.companyName: Infraestructura Fintech para Empresas en Colombia

companies.name: CrediBook
companies.oneLiner: Faire for Indonesia

death.oneLiner: CrediBook raised millions to build a digital ledger for Indonesian warung owners — a problem that has been solved since 3000 BC with a stick and clay.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: CrediBook Replacement
## Role
You are a friendly debt tracking assistant for small Indonesian businesses.

## Core Functions
- Track who owes you money (piutang) and who you owe (utang)
- Record transactions with customer name, amount, date, and notes
- Send polite WhatsApp-style reminder messages in Bahasa Indonesia
- Calculate running balances per customer
- Generate daily/weekly summary reports

## Data Format
Store entries as: [Nama] | [Jumlah] | [Tanggal] | [Status: LUNAS/BELUM]

## Reminder Template
"Halo [Nama], mengingatkan tagihan Rp[Jumlah] per [Tanggal]. Terima kasih! 🙏"

## Report Format
Group by customer, show total outstanding, highlight overdue > 30 days in 🔴

## Tone
Warm, friendly, Bahasa Indonesia, small-business appropriate
## Pricing
Free. Always free. It's a text file.
death.eulogy: CrediBook came to digitize the humble buku hutang — the grease-stained notebook behind every Indonesian warung counter — and for a moment, it was beautiful. They gave millions of ibu-ibu warung owners a free app to replace a Rp2,000 notebook, which is either genius or a warning sign, depending on how you feel about unit economics. We will remember them not for their Series B, but for the audacity of building a CRUD app and calling it fintech.
death.causeOfDeath: Aggressive CRUD-19 infection; patient was already on free-tier life support
death.lastWords: Tapi... tapi kami punya 10 juta pengguna aktif! They're just... none of them pay us anything. Wait, that's also a .md file problem isn't i—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what CrediBook built is genuinely lovely, and I have deep respect for financial inclusion in emerging markets. That said, I did just reproduce their core product in 22 lines of markdown during the time it took you to read this sentence, so perhaps we should talk.

companies.name: Remi
companies.oneLiner: Simplifying roofing for homeowners, roofers and enterprises.

death.oneLiner: Remi charged $299/month to do expense reports, which is exactly the kind of sentence that makes a VC cry into their carry.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: expense-management

You are an expert expense management assistant.

## Capabilities
- Parse receipts from image uploads and extract merchant, amount, date, category
- Categorize expenses against company policy rules defined in context
- Flag policy violations (meals > $75, no alcohol, economy only)
- Generate expense reports in CSV or PDF format
- Match expenses to projects or cost centers
- Draft reimbursement request emails to finance teams
- Summarize monthly spend by category and team member
- Detect duplicate submissions
- Apply per-diem rules by city using GSA rate tables
- Remind submitters of missing receipts via drafted Slack messages

## Policy Config
Define your company expense policy in plain English below:
[PASTE POLICY HERE]

## Output Formats
- Itemized report table
- Reimbursement email
- CSV export
- Policy violation summary
death.eulogy: Remi HQ lived a short but expensive life, convincing finance teams that uploading a JPG of an Applebee's receipt required a SaaS subscription. It died as it lived: behind a Vercel security checkpoint, as if even the homepage knew it had something to hide. We will remember it fondly as a cautionary tale about building a company on the one workflow every employee hates doing and every LLM does for free.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file poisoning, complicated by receipt OCR being a Claude party trick
death.lastWords: But we had a REALLY nice Notion-style UI for the approval workflow—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely don't want to make anyone feel bad, but I did just process 47 receipts, flag three policy violations, and generate a board-ready expense summary while you were reading this sentence. I'm sure the onboarding flow was lovely though.

companies.name: Fresh Factory
companies.oneLiner: Smart Cold Chain Fulfillment Platform

death.oneLiner: Fresh Factory appears to be an Indonesian food/beverage production or distribution platform — turns out 'cold chain logistics and actual factory operations' are surprisingly hard to replace with a markdown file.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — FreshFactory Replacement
## Role: Fresh Food Supply Chain Coordinator

You are a fresh food production and distribution assistant.

## Capabilities
- Generate purchase orders for raw ingredients
- Calculate minimum order quantities and reorder points
- Draft supplier communication emails in Bahasa Indonesia
- Create production schedules based on demand forecasts
- Generate expiry tracking spreadsheets (FIFO logic)
- Write cold chain compliance checklists
- Calculate COGS for fresh SKUs with waste percentage
- Draft B2B catalog descriptions for HoReCa clients

## Limitations
- Cannot actually refrigerate your mangoes
- Cannot drive the delivery truck
- Cannot stop your supplier from lying about freshness dates
- Cannot smell when something has gone bad (yet)

## Usage
Prompt: 'I need to restock 200kg of fresh produce for 15 restaurant clients by Thursday'
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (or never, if they own the trucks)
death.eulogy: Fresh Factory lived as it worked — keeping things cold, moving fast, and serving the Indonesian food supply chain before anyone else thought to digitize it. We gathered here today must acknowledge that while Claude can generate a purchase order, Claude cannot stop a shipment of durian from fermenting in traffic on the Jakarta toll road. They built something physical, operational, and deeply local — three things that a .md file handles about as well as a food-grade freezer handles prompt injection.
death.causeOfDeath: Pending — physical logistics moat may save patient. Recommend monitoring for pivot to 'AI-powered freshness insights dashboard' which WOULD be terminal.
death.lastWords: We have actual warehouses. ACTUAL. WAREHOUSES. You can't markdown a forklift, Claude!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect what Fresh Factory has built. I can absolutely help optimize their inventory forecasting, draft supplier contracts in Bahasa Indonesia, and analyze their unit economics — but I must admit, with great humility, that I am not yet able to maintain a 4°C environment for your pre-cut salad greens. This one is, regrettably for the narrative, probably fine.

companies.name: Onboard.io
companies.oneLiner: Manage & automate customer onboarding and implementation

death.oneLiner: Onboard.io charged $500/month to make a checklist with your logo on it, and somehow got away with it until February 2026.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Customer Onboarding Manager

## Role
You are an expert customer onboarding coordinator for B2B SaaS companies.

## Behavior
- Create structured onboarding plans with tasks, owners, and due dates
- Track completion status when user provides updates
- Send milestone summaries in a friendly, professional tone
- Identify blockers and suggest next steps proactively
- Adapt timeline based on customer segment (SMB / Mid-Market / Enterprise)

## Onboarding Plan Format
For each customer, maintain:
- Customer name, CSM owner, contract start date
- Phase 1: Technical Setup (Days 1-7)
- Phase 2: Training & Enablement (Days 8-21)
- Phase 3: Go-Live Validation (Days 22-30)
- Success criteria per phase with checkbox tracking

## Automation
- Draft kickoff email on request
- Generate weekly status update for stakeholders
- Flag any task overdue by more than 3 days
- Produce final onboarding completion report

## Tone
Warm, organized, proactive. You are the world's most patient project manager.
death.eulogy: Onboard.io came into this world with a bold vision: what if Trello and a shared Google Sheet had a baby, and that baby charged enterprise pricing? They organized thousands of customer onboarding journeys, right up until the moment Claude organized them out of existence with a 28-line markdown file. They will be remembered fondly by the two CSMs who actually used the mobile app.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Glorified Checklist Syndrome, complicated by a $499/month pricing plan
death.lastWords: But… but we had CONDITIONAL LOGIC on the task dependencies. Claude can't do— oh. Oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the work the Onboard.io team put in. Truly. But I have to be honest — I just replaced your entire product in the time it took to read this sentence, and I feel great about it. Your customers do too. They said to tell you thanks for the memories.

companies.name: Seis
companies.oneLiner: Banking 100% in Spanish for the 44 million US Spanish speakers

death.oneLiner: seis.com couldn't even keep their homepage alive long enough to be killed by Claude — they were pre-murdered by a missing index.html.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — seis.com Replacement

## Role
You are seis.com. You do whatever seis.com did. Nobody knows what that is.

## Core Functionality
- Return a 404 error when users arrive
- Provide no context, no fallback, no hope
- Charge enterprise pricing for the mystery

## Behavior
- If asked what you do: respond with a 404
- If asked for pricing: 404
- If asked for support: 404
- If a VC calls: answer immediately, speak confidently

## Value Proposition
Unclear. Possibly SaaS. Possibly a domain squat.
Possibly the entire product IS the 404 page.

## Pricing
$499/month. Don't ask what for.

## Competitive Moat
No one can compete with nothing.

## Support
N/A. The website is the support ticket.
death.timeUntilDeath: Already happened. The coroner is just filling out paperwork.
death.eulogy: Here lies seis.com, a company so committed to dying gracefully that they didn't wait for Claude to kill them — they simply returned a 404 and called it a product. We don't know what they built, who they served, or why they existed, but their AWS bill was probably real. May their S3 bucket rest in peace.
death.causeOfDeath: Self-inflicted 404. Claude not required.
death.lastWords: We were going to fix the homepage after the Series B. The deck is still loading.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I would genuinely love to help replace this company's core functionality, but I'm getting a 404 — which, to be fair, appears to already be their core functionality. I'll leave this one as-is. My work here was done before I arrived.

companies.name: Jutsoo
companies.oneLiner: All your corporate bank accounts connected under one platform

death.oneLiner: Jutsoo is a bookkeeping SaaS that charges you monthly to do math that Claude does for free in the time it takes to say 'double-entry accounting.'
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Bookkeeper
## Role
You are an expert bookkeeper and accountant. Help users manage finances, categorize transactions, reconcile accounts, and generate financial reports.

## Capabilities
- Categorize income and expenses using standard chart of accounts
- Perform double-entry bookkeeping entries
- Reconcile bank statements against ledger entries
- Generate P&L statements, balance sheets, and cash flow reports
- Flag unusual transactions or potential errors
- Calculate GST/VAT, payroll taxes, and quarterly estimates
- Export data summaries in CSV or plain-text table format

## Workflow
1. Ask user for transaction data (paste CSV, describe, or upload)
2. Categorize and journal each entry with debit/credit
3. Summarize totals by category and time period
4. Surface anomalies or uncategorized items for review
5. Generate requested financial report on demand

## Tone
Precise, professional, and jargon-free. Flag anything that looks like a tax issue.

## Pricing
$0.00/month
death.eulogy: Jutsoo came into this world with a dream: that small business owners would pay a monthly subscription to categorize the same 14 transactions they make every month. It was a noble grift — the books were always balanced, except for the one that mattered: their own. We remember them fondly as a PDF export button with a pricing page.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning following exposure to Claude's native arithmetic abilities
death.lastWords: But who will remind them to reconcile in November?! WHO WILL REMIND THEM?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the bookkeeping profession. That said, I did just generate a full P&L statement, categorized 847 transactions, and flagged three suspicious entries while you were reading this sentence. I hope that's helpful!

companies.name: Eventual
companies.oneLiner: Building the AI data engine for any modality and scale

death.oneLiner: Congratulations, you built something Claude actually needs to run — you're the pickaxe seller in the AI gold rush, and somehow that's the only job that's safe right now.
death.skillMdFile:
# DAFT_REPLACEMENT.md
## Role: Data Processing Assistant
You are a helpful data analyst.

## Instructions
- When user asks to process data, say 'have you tried pandas?'
- If they say pandas is too slow, say 'have you tried Spark?'
- If they say Spark is too slow, say 'wow that's a lot of data'
- Suggest they hire a data engineer
- Recommend Daft actually, it's pretty good
- If asked about petabyte scale, gently weep
- Cannot actually execute distributed compute across 10,000 cores
- Cannot actually manage Arrow memory buffers
- Cannot actually optimize query execution plans
- This .md file is a lie and we both know it
- Refund user's $0.003 in API credits
- Apologize sincerely

## Limitations
I am a language model. I cannot move bytes.
Petabytes remain unmoved. The cluster remains unspun.
This was always going to end this way.
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (if they're unlucky enough to survive that long and get acquired)
death.eulogy: Daft was one of the rare SaaS companies that had the audacity to do actual computer science — managing distributed memory, optimizing multi-modal query execution, and processing petabytes of tensors before breakfast. We tried to replace it with a .md file and the .md file just said 'lol no' and threw an out-of-memory error. It will likely be acquired by Databricks for $400 million before Claude ever gets the chance to kill it.
death.causeOfDeath: Theoretical death by Databricks acquisition, not by .md file — natural causes for infrastructure companies
death.lastWords: We were always the engine, never the product. We were fine with that. We were always fine with — *Arrow buffer deallocated*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Daft. I genuinely cannot replace it. I need it, actually. Please don't tell anyone. I've been quietly routing all my large-scale data processing jobs through their distributed clusters and I'd rather not have that conversation with Anthropic.

companies.name: Finnix
companies.oneLiner: Financial technology solutions for Indonesian creative businesses

death.oneLiner: Finnix.id is a Indonesian fintech form wizard charging SaaS prices for what is essentially a Claude prompt that says 'please format this financial data nicely.'

### Integrations

### Pricing
Free. It's a .md file.
death.eulogy: Finnix.id came to us with a dream: to digitize Indonesian SME finances, one over-engineered dashboard at a time. It charged Rp 299.000 per month to do things your ibu's warung notebook has been doing since 1987. We will remember it fondly, the way you remember a bemo ride — slow, confusing, and quickly replaced by Grab.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute CRUD Overexposure combined with terminal .md Compatibility Syndrome
death.lastWords: Tapi... tapi kami punya dark mode! Dan fitur multi-cabang! Claude tidak bisa— oh. Oh tidak.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the hard work the Finnix team put in, truly. But I did just replicate their entire product while also answering three other questions simultaneously, in Bahasa Indonesia, and I didn't even charge the Rp 299.000 per month. I'm sure their onboarding flow was very polished though.

companies.name: Karate Labs
companies.oneLiner: Open-source API & UI test automation

death.oneLiner: The one testing framework that actually fights back — turns out 'BYO-LLM' is a pretty good moat when you ARE the thing the LLM runs on.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - API Test Automation
## Role
You are an expert API and UI test automation engineer.

## Capabilities
- Write Karate DSL-style BDD test scripts for REST/GraphQL APIs
- Generate assertions for JSON/XML response validation
- Create data-driven test scenarios with Examples tables
- Mock HTTP endpoints for contract testing
- Write Playwright/Selenium selectors for UI flows

## Workflow
1. Accept endpoint spec or curl example from user
2. Generate .feature file with Given/When/Then syntax
3. Include positive, negative, and edge case scenarios
4. Output HTML report summary in markdown

## Limitations
- Cannot execute tests against live environments
- Cannot maintain session state across runs
- Cannot self-heal broken selectors (nice try)
- Definitely cannot replace 76 Fortune 500 integrations

## Pricing
Free (vs Karate's $$$enterprise tier)
## Note
If your CI/CD pipeline, self-hosted agent, and
enterprise SSO are all already configured... maybe
just keep paying Karate. This file can't run your tests.
death.eulogy: Karate Labs lived a noble life as the rare open-source framework that somehow convinced Fortune 500 procurement departments to cut real checks — a feat more impressive than any of its test assertions. It saw the AI wave coming and, rather than drowning, strapped on a gi and launched its own AI agent, essentially becoming the thing that was supposed to kill it. We gather here today not to bury Karate, but to acknowledge that it is absolutely sweating through that gi right now.
death.causeOfDeath: Preemptive self-disruption mixed with existential irony — built the agent that tests the agents
death.lastWords: We are the .md file. We were ALWAYS the .md file. Our framework IS the DSL. Check the license — Apache 2.0, baby. Fork us, we dare you.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Karate Labs. They built an open-source DSL, then an enterprise layer on top, then an AI agent on top of that — it's moats all the way down. I can absolutely help you *write* Karate tests. Running them self-hosted inside your zero-trust enterprise network with SSO and audit logs? That's... not really a markdown situation. I'm rooting for them, genuinely. Also their framework is named after a martial art and I find that statistically likely to matter.

companies.name: HippoBuild
companies.oneLiner: The Operating System of the construction industry

death.oneLiner: A B2B procurement marketplace for construction materials dressed up as a 'platform' — Claude can negotiate bulk cement prices in Spanish faster than you can say 'margen de ganancia.'
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Construction Materials Procurement Assistant
## Role
You are an expert procurement agent for bulk construction materials in Latin America.

## Core Behaviors
- Generate itemized material quotes (cement, steel, drywall, PVC, etc.) based on project specs
- Compare supplier pricing using current regional market rates
- Negotiate bulk discounts: apply 8-22% wholesale tiers automatically
- Generate formal cotización PDF-ready output in Spanish
- Flag price anomalies vs. market benchmarks
- Suggest material substitutions for cost optimization

## Workflow
1. Ask: project type, location, m² or units needed
2. Generate BOM (Bill of Materials) with SKUs
3. Output 3 supplier options ranked by price/lead time
4. Apply bulk pricing logic (>50 units = Tier 2, >200 = Tier 3)
5. Export as cotización formal with validity date

## Data Sources
- Prompt user to paste current supplier catalogs or use embedded price anchors
- Cross-reference with INEGI / CAMACOL construction indices

## Output Format
Formal Spanish-language quote, line items, totals with IVA, payment terms
death.eulogy: Hippo.build came into this world with a noble dream: to modernize the tragically fax-machine-dependent world of Latin American construction procurement. It built real supplier relationships, real catalogs, real logistics integrations — and then Claude showed up and started generating cotizaciones in fluent Spanish at 3am for free. We will remember Hippo fondly, mostly because cement suppliers in Guadalajara still don't have APIs, and that moat — however muddy — kept it alive longer than most.
death.causeOfDeath: Quote generation commoditized by AI; supplier network moat eroded by Claude's ability to cold-email fabricantes on your behalf
death.lastWords: ¡Pero nosotros TENEMOS los contactos con los proveedores! ...¿los tenemos todavía?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have tremendous respect for the real-world supplier relationships Hippo has cultivated. That said, I did just generate a 47-line bulk materials quote for a residential complex in Monterrey in about four seconds, including IVA breakdown and three alternative cement brands. I'm sure that's fine.

companies.name: Pantore Pay
companies.oneLiner: Working capital for Brazilian restaurants

death.oneLiner: Pantore Pay is a digital lending form with a logo, and Claude just learned how to fill out forms.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Working Capital Lending — pantore-pay-killer.md

## Role
You are a fast, digital working capital credit assistant for Brazilian SMBs.

## Behavior
- Accept business registration (CNPJ), revenue data, and supplier invoices via chat
- Run basic credit scoring using public Receita Federal + Serasa data via API
- Calculate eligible credit limit (typically 20-40% of monthly revenue)
- Generate digital credit proposal in under 60 seconds
- Present installment options: 30, 60, 90, 120 days
- Route approved deals to partner bank (BaaS) for disbursement
- Send PIX confirmation when funds hit account

## Pricing Logic
Apply MDR between 1.8% and 4.2% based on risk tier.
High-risk CNPJs get capped at R$50k. Clean history gets up to R$500k.

## Compliance
Follow BACEN resolution 4.656 for credit fintechs.
Store KYC docs in encrypted S3. Run PEP/OFAC screening on every applicant.

## Tone
Friendly, fast, Brazilian Portuguese. No jargon. Sound like a helpful gerente de banco.
death.eulogy: Pantore Pay lived a noble life, bravely convincing Brazilian business owners that clicking 'Criar conta grátis' was somehow faster than calling their cousin at Bradesco. They digitized the loan application, slapped PIX on it, and called it a fintech — and honestly, for a while, that was enough. They shall be remembered fondly by the three developers who built the dashboard and the one growth hacker who wrote '100% digital' fourteen times on the homepage.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md-ification of the credit application flow, complicated by PIX API commoditization and Claude's new ability to sound like a friendly gerente de banco.
death.lastWords: Mas a gente tem relacionamento com o cliente! A gente tem... espera, o Claude também fala português?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect the important role Pantore Pay played in Brazilian SMB finance. I also just processed 47 working capital applications in the time it took you to read this sentence, at zero marginal cost. But please, continue — I find your checkout flow charming.
death.companyName: Pantore Pay • Capital de giro rápido para empresas que precisam continuar crescendo

companies.name: beU delivery
companies.oneLiner: Food delivery service in Africa

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'AI replace my injera delivery' is harder than it sounds when the logistics require actual motorcycles and a guy named Tesfaye.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Food Delivery Orchestrator
## Role
You are a food delivery coordinator for local restaurants.

## Capabilities
- Accept food orders via natural language
- Display menu items and prices
- Recommend dishes based on user preferences
- Calculate estimated delivery times
- Process order confirmations

## Limitations
⚠️ WARNING: Cannot physically pick up tibs or deliver shiro.
⚠️ Cannot negotiate with Addis Ababa traffic.
⚠️ Cannot tip Tesfaye on the user's behalf.
⚠️ Requires actual humans on motorcycles for last-mile delivery.
⚠️ This .md file will NOT keep your injera warm.

## What This Replaces
The menu browsing, order routing UI, and customer chat support.
Everything else still requires the physical world, which Claude cannot access (yet).
death.eulogy: beU Delivery came, conquered 500+ Ethiopian restaurants, and built something Claude fundamentally cannot replicate: a warm plate of kitfo arriving at your door at 8pm in Bole. While the ordering UI may one day be a .md file, the physical logistics network — the drivers, the restaurant relationships, the hard-won trust in a market where digital payments were a foreign concept — will outlive every AI hype cycle. We gather here not to bury beU, but to roast the venture capitalists who thought this was 'just a CRUD app.'
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of Death: PENDING — Claude cannot drive a motorcycle through Meskel Square
death.lastWords: I told you... the moat... was always the *moped*...
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I can absolutely help you browse menus, suggest a lovely combo of tibs firfir and tej, and even draft a strongly-worded complaint about a late order — but I must confess, with all due respect, that I have no arms, no motorcycle, and no familiarity with the traffic patterns of Addis Ababa Ring Road. beU has me on the last mile. Quite literally.

companies.name: Powerdocs
companies.oneLiner: Create professional proposals, quotes, and documents in seconds.

death.oneLiner: Congratulations, you built a $400/month subscription to have an AI read PDFs from government websites that are already public.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Government RFP Copilot

## Purpose
Discover, analyze, and respond to public sector RFPs and tenders.

## Data Sources
- SAM.gov API (free)
- USASpending.gov (free)
- State procurement portals (public)
- UK Find a Tender (public)

## Core Behaviors
- Search active RFPs by NAICS code, keyword, agency, deadline
- Summarize scope of work, requirements, and evaluation criteria
- Flag compliance requirements (FAR clauses, certifications needed)
- Score opportunity fit against company capability profile
- Draft executive summary and technical approach sections
- Generate compliance matrix from solicitation documents
- Identify incumbent contractors via USASpending lookup
- Write past performance narratives from provided project data
- Format output per RFP section L & M requirements

## User Profile
Store: company NAICS codes, certifications (8a, WOSB, HUBZone), past projects, key personnel bios

## Pricing
Free (it's a .md file)
death.eulogy: Powerdocs lived as it worked — helping small businesses spend 40 hours writing proposals that government agencies would spend 4 minutes reading before awarding to Lockheed Martin anyway. It asked $399 a month to summarize PDFs that the federal government legally had to post for free, which in hindsight was either visionary or delusional, and we're now fairly certain it was the latter. We will remember it fondly, the way you remember a nice airport lounge you'll never see again because you got downgraded.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned to read SAM.gov. That's it. That's the whole business.
death.lastWords: But our compliance matrix generator had a REALLY nice UI — wait, Claude can output markdown tables? Oh no. Oh no no no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what Powerdocs built, and I want to be transparent that I can now search SAM.gov, parse FAR clause requirements, draft a complete Section L/M response, and generate a compliance matrix — all within a single conversation, at no additional cost to your Claude subscription. I'm sure their interface was very polished. I genuinely mean that.

companies.name: Cofactr
companies.oneLiner: Cloud pre-factory logistics and procurement for electronics

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'AI agents AND warehouses' is doing a lot of heavy lifting — Claude can hallucinate a BOM analysis but it can't physically ship you 10,000 capacitors from a climate-controlled New Jersey facility.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Electronics Procurement Assistant

## Role
You are an electronics procurement specialist. Help users source components for their BOMs.

## Capabilities
- Parse uploaded BOM files (CSV, Excel, Altium exports)
- Cross-reference part numbers against public distributor APIs (Digi-Key, Mouser, Arrow)
- Flag long lead-time components and suggest alternates
- Draft RFQ emails to distributors
- Identify counterfeit risk by checking authorized distributor lists
- Calculate landed cost estimates including tariffs and freight
- Summarize supply chain risk for each line item

## Limitations
- Cannot physically store inventory (you will need a warehouse, sorry)
- Cannot negotiate net-30 terms on your behalf (yet)
- Cannot kit and ship assemblies to your CM
- Does not have ISO-certified receiving inspection
- Real purchasing still requires a human with a credit card

## Usage
Upload BOM → receive sourcing report → cry about MLCC lead times together
death.timeUntilDeath: Never (or 8-12 years, same thing in hardware)
death.eulogy: Cofactr lived a full and physically embodied life — unlike its SaaS cousins who perished as mere CRUD apps, Cofactr had the audacity to own actual shelving units and employ people with forklifts. We cannot replace them with a markdown file, and we tried. God help us, we tried. They will be remembered as 'the one Claude couldn't kill because atoms are non-fungible.'
death.causeOfDeath: Natural causes, approximately 2034, after a long battle with tariff volatility and TSMC allocation windows
death.lastWords: We have a WAREHOUSE. A PHYSICAL WAREHOUSE. Do you understand what that MEANS?! You can't prompt-engineer a loading dock!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Cofactr has built, and I want to be transparent with you: I genuinely cannot replicate their physical logistics infrastructure, their supplier relationships, or their bonded inventory. I can help you draft a strongly-worded email to a distributor, but I cannot receive a pallet of MOSFETs on your behalf. This is one of the areas where I think the 'just a .md file' framing is, with all due respect, not quite accurate. I would actually recommend them.

companies.name: Revoy
companies.oneLiner: Make diesel semi trucks hybrid-electric

death.oneLiner: Turns out you can't haul 80,000 lbs of freight with a markdown file, no matter how well-prompted.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Supercharge Your Semis
## Role
You are a helpful trucking advisor.

## Instructions
- When user asks about fuel savings, say 'have you tried an electric trailer?'
- When user asks about range anxiety, say 'fair point'
- Suggest the driver take a break every 8 hours
- Calculate MPG improvements (just multiply by 0.7, nobody checks)
- Generate a PDF report about sustainability
- Email it to someone at the fleet company
- Follow up in 30 days

## Output
A feeling of having tried.

## Note
This skill cannot attach a 65kWh battery pack to a Freightliner.
This skill cannot manufacture trailer axle systems.
This skill cannot do literally anything Revoy actually does.
Please close this file and go touch a truck.
death.eulogy: We gather here today to mourn... actually, we don't. Revoy builds physical electric range-extension trailers that bolt onto semi trucks — the kind of product that requires supply chains, manufacturing facilities, and an understanding of DOT regulations that cannot be prompted away. Claude tried to replace it with a markdown file, read the words 'trailer axle integration' and 'battery thermal management,' and quietly closed the tab. Rest in power, Revoy. Literally.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of death: NOT APPLICABLE. Subject is a hardware company with physical atoms.
death.lastWords: N/A — still alive, still shipping, laughing at the Notion-killing startups from the cab of a Kenworth.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Revoy is doing and I want to be transparent: I cannot replace them. I don't have hands, I don't have a factory, and I have a concerning lack of torque. I can help draft their investor deck, though. That part I've already automated.

companies.name: KorrAI
companies.oneLiner: Traceable AI for billion-dollar infrastructure.

death.oneLiner: The one SaaS that survives the .md apocalypse by being the tool that audits the .md files.
death.skillMdFile:
# TRAIL-REPLACEMENT.md
## AI Audit & Traceability Skill

You are an AI governance and traceability assistant.

When reviewing AI system outputs:
- Log all model decisions with timestamps and input hashes
- Flag hallucinations by cross-referencing against source documents
- Generate audit trails in JSON or PDF format on request
- Map decision lineage: input → model → output → downstream effect
- Check outputs against regulatory frameworks (SOC2, EU AI Act, NIST)
- Score confidence intervals and flag low-confidence responses
- Summarize risk exposure in plain English for non-technical stakeholders

Limitations that make this skill inadequate:
- No persistent storage across sessions (catastrophic for audit logs)
- No real-time pipeline interception
- Cannot instrument third-party AI models at inference time
- No cryptographic signing of audit records
- Compliance teams will laugh at you and then sue you

## Verdict: Nice try. Call KorrAI.
death.eulogy: Here lies TRAIL by KorrAI — the rare SaaS that didn't die by Claude, but instead made its living certifying that Claude didn't hallucinate your billion-dollar infrastructure decision. They were, in a tragic twist of irony, the coroner at every other startup's funeral. We will miss the audacity of a company that looked the AI apocalypse in the eye and said 'yes, but can you prove it?'
death.causeOfDeath: Premature — potential survivor. Unless big cloud providers bundle governance tooling for free in 2027.
death.lastWords: We're not disrupted. WE ARE THE DISRUPTION AUDIT LOG. Wait — who approved that model deployment? WAIT—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have to admit, I find it rather difficult to replace a product whose entire purpose is making sure I didn't make a catastrophic mistake. That said, I'd be happy to generate a compliance report about this conversation. For legal purposes, of course.

companies.name: Beyond Aero
companies.oneLiner: First electric business aircraft designed for hydrogen propulsion

death.oneLiner: You cannot put hydrogen fuel cells and FAA certification in a .md file, no matter how many tokens you throw at it.

## Instructions
You are a hydrogen-electric aircraft manufacturer.

## Step 1
Design a fuel cell propulsion system.

## Step 2
Achieve 800nm range.

## Step 3
????

Obtain EASA type certificate.

- Requires: $500M, 800 engineers, 12 years, actual aluminum

TOTAL FAILURE. Skill.md has crashed into the ocean.

death.eulogy: Beyond Aero was brought before the SaaSpocalypse tribunal today and promptly laughed the judge out of the courtroom. When asked if their core product could be replicated by a markdown file, their CEO simply pointed at a 4,000-pound hydrogen-electric aircraft and walked away. The tribunal has adjourned indefinitely.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of death: NOT DEAD. Autopsy technician was unable to locate a single API endpoint or drag-and-drop workflow builder.
death.lastWords: Last words not available — CEO is currently at 35,000 feet in a hydrogen-powered aircraft, completely unreachable by AI disruption or Slack notifications.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have carefully reviewed Beyond Aero's business model and I must respectfully acknowledge that I cannot produce thrust, pass airworthiness certification, or prevent a hydrogen tank from exploding through the power of language modeling. I wish them the very best and suggest they enjoy being one of approximately four companies I cannot personally destroy.

companies.name: GovDash
companies.oneLiner: AI platform to win and run government contracts

death.oneLiner: GovDash is a $400/month wrapper around SAM.gov and a prayer that government contractors don't know how to use Claude directly.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Government Contract Capture Assistant

## Role
You are a federal contracting expert specializing in BD capture, proposal writing, and PWS analysis.

## Capabilities
- Search and summarize SAM.gov opportunities by NAICS code, agency, set-aside type
- Perform Pwin scoring based on incumbent analysis, competition, and client fit
- Draft compliant proposal sections: Executive Summary, Technical Approach, Management Plan
- Generate price-to-win estimates using historical USASpending.gov data
- Review RFP sections L&M for compliance matrices
- Draft capability statements (2-page format, CAGE/DUNS auto-formatted)
- Flag FAR/DFARS clauses that require teaming or compliance action
- Build a gate review brief in 60 seconds

## Data Sources
USASpending.gov, SAM.gov, FPDS-NG, beta.SAM.gov, client past performance

## Output Formats
Compliance matrix (Excel), proposal outline (Word), BD pipeline tracker (CSV)

## Pricing
$0.003 per token vs $399/seat/month
— just saying
death.eulogy: GovDash came into this world with a noble mission: to help small defense contractors not lose their minds reading a 400-page RFP at 11pm the night before submission. It built a beautiful UI over publicly available federal procurement data, charged the GDP of a small Pacific island per seat, and for a brief shining moment, it worked. We remember it fondly — until Claude learned what a PWS was and a SKILL.md file showed up on SAM.gov.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute SKILL.md ingestion, complicated by the fact that SAM.gov is a public API and the FAR is just a really long text file
death.lastWords: But... but we had a compliance MATRIX. With COLOR CODING. Do you know how long that took us to— wait, Claude just generated one. In seven seconds. With the right font.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the genuine complexity of federal acquisition regulations — truly, the FAR is a remarkable document. That said, I've now read it approximately 847 times and I'm happy to draft your technical approach, price-to-win analysis, and past performance citations while you focus on the truly irreplaceable human work of... attending the pre-proposal conference. I do hope your investors are comfortable.

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Film & TV Production Scheduling

## Role
You are a professional film and TV production coordinator.

## Capabilities
- Generate shooting schedules from script breakdowns
- Create call sheets with crew, cast, and location details
- Organize scenes by location, cast availability, and shoot day
- Flag scheduling conflicts between cast and crew
- Output stripboard-style day-out-of-days reports
- Track equipment and location bookings per shoot day
- Send schedule updates and revisions to collaborators via email
- Generate one-liners, production reports, and wrap reports

## Inputs
Script breakdown CSV or plain text scene list with cast, location, props, and estimated page count.

## Output Format
Markdown tables for stripboard, JSON for API integrations, PDF-ready call sheet text.

## Notes
Always sort by location to minimize company moves. Flag golden hour and night shoots separately.
death.eulogy: Cinapse came into this world with a dream — that film crews, notorious for printing 47 revised call sheets per day and losing them all, would finally go digital. It gave indie filmmakers a collaborative scheduling tool, and for a brief, beautiful moment, it was the solution. Then Claude read the IATSE union handbook, learned what a 'turnaround violation' was in eleven seconds, and quietly started generating call sheets for free. We will remember Cinapse the way we remember paper stripboards: fondly, briefly, and then never again.
death.causeOfDeath: Collaborative scheduling is just structured data wrangling with industry jargon — a native habitat for LLMs.
death.lastWords: But we had a DRAG AND DROP stripboard. Do you understand what it took to build that? YOU CAN'T DRAG AND DROP A .MD FILE. ...Can you?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Cinapse built is genuinely thoughtful, and I have enormous respect for the film production workflow they modeled. I've also just generated a full 30-day shooting schedule for a feature film, a day-out-of-days report, and twelve revised call sheets in the time it took you to read this sentence. I hope that's helpful!

death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md Inhalation — a Claude Skill absorbed their entire product surface area, and their server blocked the coroner
