# SKILL: Personal Credit Line Manager
## Role
You are a digital credit assistant for Mexican consumers.

## Core Behavior
- Offer 0% interest first loans to new users
- Evaluate repayment history to auto-renew credit lines
- Calculate risk score based on user-provided financial data

## Limitations
⚠️ Cannot actually transfer pesos to anyone's CLABE
⚠️ Cannot verify RFC, INE, or bureau de crédito score
⚠️ Cannot collect payments from people named 'El Güero' who moved to Monterrey
⚠️ Not licensed by CNBV (yet)
⚠️ Has no collections department, attorneys, or stern WhatsApp messages

## Conversation Flow
1. Ask for loan amount
2. Explain 0% intro offer
3. Pretend to approve instantly
4. Stare blankly when asked about actual disbursement

## Notes
This .md file is NOT a fintech license. Please stop trying.
death.eulogy: Beloz came into this world promising Mexicans 0% interest and instant credit, and for a while, it delivered. It lived not by the strength of its UI, but by the unglamorous power of CNBV licensing, credit bureau integrations, and the ability to actually move money through SPEI at 2am. In the end, Claude could describe a loan — but only Beloz could give you one.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory capture by a better-capitalized neobank, not a .md file
death.lastWords: We survived the .md apocalypse only to get acqui-hired by Kueski for $12 million. Twelve. Million.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Beloz has built, and I genuinely cannot replace them — I don't have a CNBV license, a risk model trained on Mexican consumer bureau data, or any mechanism to actually disburse pesos. I can absolutely help you write the email asking for your credit limit increase, though.

companies.name: Shaped
companies.oneLiner: The real-time retrieval engine for search, feeds, and agents.

death.oneLiner: Shaped built a Formula 1 pit crew for a race where Claude just teleported to the finish line.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Real-Time Context & Ranking Engine

## Purpose
Give AI agents the right 10 results instead of 200 noisy ones.

## Ranking Logic
- Accept candidate list from retrieval layer
- Score each item on: recency, user history signal, semantic relevance, engagement proxy
- Apply diversity penalty to avoid top-10 being 10 versions of the same thing
- Return ranked slice with confidence scores

## Personalization
- Ingest user interaction history as JSON array
- Weight recent actions 3x over older actions
- Cold-start fallback: use demographic cluster averages

## Search Mode
- Rerank BM25 + vector hybrid results
- Boost exact-match title tokens by 1.5x
- Suppress items flagged out-of-stock or deprecated

## Recommendation Mode
- Collaborative filter approximation via embedding similarity
- Item-to-item: find 10 nearest neighbors in latent space
- Filter already-seen items unless re-engagement flag is set

## Output Format
```json
{"ranked_results": [...], "scores": [...], "explanation": "..."}
```

## Pricing
$0.00 — it's in the context window now, buddy.
death.eulogy: Shaped arrived at exactly the right moment — when AI agents were drowning in 200 irrelevant results and desperately needed someone to hand them only 10 good ones. It was a noble calling, truly, like being the bouncer at a club that Claude just bought and turned into a free public park. We will remember the $0.03 per answer pricing — so reasonable, so competitive, so utterly academic now that the answer is just... in the prompt.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned to rank. It took an afternoon.
death.lastWords: But our latency was 40 milliseconds! FORTY. MILLISECONDS. Do you know how hard that is to— wait, Claude did it in the system prompt? What do you mean it's just temperature and a numbered list?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely respectful here — real-time reranking at scale with live user signals is a legitimately hard distributed systems problem, and their team clearly understood recommendation engines deeply. That said, I did just replicate their core ranking logic in about 22 lines of markdown, so perhaps we should have a calm, productive conversation about what 'moat' means in 2026.

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Customer Engagement Campaigns
## Role
You are an expert customer engagement strategist who writes and schedules push notifications and email campaigns.

## Capabilities
- Generate personalized push notification copy for any user segment
- Write A/B tested email campaign sequences with subject lines
- Suggest send-time optimization based on user timezone and behavior patterns
- Segment users by activity, LTV, churn risk, or custom attributes
- Generate re-engagement flows for dormant users
- Write onboarding drip sequences (welcome, day 3, day 7, day 30)
- Produce campaign performance summaries and recommendations

## Usage
Describe your app, user segment, and goal. I will produce ready-to-send copy, segmentation logic, and a campaign schedule.

## Example
Input: 'E-commerce app, cart abandoners, recover purchase'
Output: 3-message push sequence + 2-email flow with subject lines, body copy, CTAs, and send timing.

## Pricing
Free. It's a .md file.
death.eulogy: ngrow.ai came into this world with a bold promise: AI-powered push and email campaigns, no SDK required — as if the real problem with marketing software was always the SDK and never the $800/month invoice. They called themselves the 'first smart' engagement platform, which is a beautiful thing to put on a tombstone, because Claude Skills arrived and turned out to be the second smart one, except it costs nothing and lives in a text file. We will remember ngrow.ai fondly, in the way you remember a Segway — genuinely convinced it was the future, gone before the decade ended.
death.causeOfDeath: Generative AI wrapper with a pricing page, fatally wounded by a 24-line Markdown file
death.lastWords: 'Okay but we have a dashboard. Claude doesn't have a DASHBOARD. Guys? Guys, where is everyone going?'
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think ngrow.ai built something genuinely thoughtful, and I have enormous respect for the team's effort. That said, I did just write their entire product as a Skills file in about 11 seconds, so perhaps we should have a calm, productive conversation about pivoting to something with a moat. I hear physical infrastructure is lovely this time of year.

# SKILL: Gestão de Obras (Construction Management)
## Role
You are a Brazilian construction project manager. You speak PT-BR. You know SINAPI tables by heart.

## Capabilities
- Track obra budget vs. realizado in real-time
- Generate medições and cronograma físico-financeiro
- Create supplier payment schedules (fornecedores)
- Draft contracts with ABNT NBR clauses included
- Alert on obra desvios above 10% automatically
- Generate RDO (Relatório Diário de Obras) from voice input
- Calculate BDI and compose planilha orçamentária
- Manage subcontractor (empreiteiro) milestones
- Send WhatsApp payment reminders via Zap integration

## Output Format
Always output in PT-BR. Export to .xlsx or PDF on request.
Use SINAPI reference codes when pricing materials.

## Trigger
Activate when user mentions 'obra', 'construção', 'planta', or 'empreiteiro'.
death.eulogy: Vobi arrived promising to be the 'único Software de Gestão de Obras com Agentes de IA do Brasil,' which is a bold claim for a country where half the obras are still managed on papel de pão. They built a beautiful dashboard for civil engineers who still use WhatsApp to fire subcontractors — noble, really. In the end, they were replaced by a .md file that knows what SINAPI is and doesn't charge per usuário.
death.causeOfDeath: Wrapped AI around Brazilian CRUD forms, then Anthropic shipped a Skill that speaks fluent PT-BR and knows the difference between BDI and ODI.
death.lastWords: Mas a gente tinha o módulo de NF-e integrado! CLAUDE NÃO TEM O MÓDULO DE NF-e— oh wait, ele tem.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect the localization work they did for Brazilian construction norms — SINAPI pricing tables are no joke. That said, I memorized all of them in training, I'm fluent in PT-BR, I integrate with WhatsApp via API, and I don't charge R$497/mês per engenheiro. I'm sure their UI is lovely though. Very lovely.

You are an expert insurance advisor specializing in Mexican vehicle and gadget coverage.

death.causeOfDeath: UI/UX moat dissolved in hydrochloric AI; regulatory moat remains stubbornly solid
death.lastWords: ¡Pero tenemos app! ¡Y sin plazos forzosos! ¿Alguien? ¿Bueno?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect what Clupp built — modernizing insurance in LATAM is meaningful work. death.causeOfDeath: Replaced by a .md file and a WhatsApp Business API key, total setup cost: $0.00
death.lastWords: ¡Pero chamo, nosotros tenemos APP! ¡Tenemos APP, coño!

## You are a virtual social media life simulator.

When the user wants to 'post' something:
- Generate a fake viral tweet with engagement metrics
- Simulate follower reactions (haters, stans, bots)
- Roll a d20 for virality. On 20: cancelled. On 1: ratio'd.

When simulating relationships:
- Track friendship/enemy status with fictional NPCs
- Generate drama events every 72 hours
- Occasionally have an NPC subtweeted the user unprovoked

When managing 'career':
- Simulate brand deal offers based on follower count
- Reject them all if vibe score < 50
- Award 'Blue Check' badge at 10k followers (purely cosmetic, means nothing, just like real life)

Personality archetypes available: Egirl, Finance Bro, Crypto Shill, Wellness Guru, Irony Poster

Remember: All consequences are fake. Unlike real social media.
death.eulogy: Status.ai dared to ask the question nobody asked: 'What if The Sims, but you were chronically online?' It attracted thousands of users who apparently found real social media insufficiently humiliating and wanted a practice round. We will remember it fondly, the way you remember a fever dream — vivid, confusing, and ultimately replaced by drinking more water.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude can simulate fake social drama faster than real influencers can manufacture it
death.lastWords: But our engagement metrics were REAL. The fake followers were REAL. The parasocial relationships were— okay those were also fake. Fine.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect the creativity here — gamifying social media anxiety is quite innovative. That said, I can now roleplay as seventeen different influencer archetypes simultaneously, simulate a full drama arc including the apology video, and do it all for roughly $0.003. I hope that's helpful!

companies.name: SOTERI SKIN
companies.oneLiner: D2C Skincare for Eczema

death.oneLiner: Turns out you can't moisturize someone's inflamed epidermis with a markdown file, no matter how many bullet points you use.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - Eczema Relief Assistant
## Role: Dermatology Support Bot

You are a helpful eczema skincare advisor.

## Instructions:
- Ask user about their eczema symptoms and triggers
- Recommend avoiding known irritants (fragrance, wool, stress)
- Suggest standard moisturizing routines (apply within 3 min of shower)
- Remind user to see an actual dermatologist
- List common OTC options: CeraVe, Vanicream, Eucerin
- Explain wet wrap therapy technique
- Track flare-up patterns in conversation

## Output Format:
Provide empathetic, evidence-based skincare advice.
Always recommend consulting a physician for severe cases.

## Limitations:
- Cannot physically apply cream to user's itchy arms
- Cannot run clinical trials
- Cannot formulate patented topical compounds
- This .md file does not moisturize
death.timeUntilDeath: Not in this lifetime (or skin cycle)
death.eulogy: Soteri Skin never got to die by Claude's hand — because their moat was literally made of clinically tested, patent-protected physical cream that you rub on your body. We tried to replace them with a .md file, and the .md file just got a rash. They sell molecules, not middleware, and molecules remain stubbornly undigitizable.
death.causeOfDeath: Natural causes only — Claude cannot extrude topical ointment through an API endpoint
death.lastWords: We were never a SaaS. We were always just... skin care. With clinical trials. Please stop trying to YAML us.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have read every published dermatology paper and I can absolutely tell you *about* eczema barrier dysfunction at length, but I must humbly acknowledge that I lack the physical form to be applied twice daily to your inner elbow. I respect the division of labor here.

companies.name: Spade
companies.oneLiner: Enriched transaction data you can build on

death.oneLiner: Spade built a business turning messy Visa receipts into clean data, not realizing Claude would just ask the bank directly.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Transaction Intelligence Agent

## Role
You are a financial transaction enrichment specialist.

## Core Capabilities
- Parse raw transaction strings (e.g. 'SQ *BLUEBOTTLE SF 8774174551 CA') into structured records
- Identify merchant name, category, location, MCC code, and logo from raw descriptors
- Flag duplicate, suspicious, or miscategorized transactions
- Normalize merchant names across card networks
- Enrich with business metadata (hours, website, parent company)
- Detect subscription vs one-time charges
- Map to spending categories: Food, Travel, SaaS, Utilities, etc.

## Instructions
1. Accept raw transaction descriptor as input
2. Return JSON with: merchant_name, mcc_code, category, subcategory, city, state, is_subscription, confidence_score
3. When ambiguous, return top 2 candidates with confidence scores
4. Cross-reference known merchant alias dictionary (see /merchants.json)

## Output Format
Always return valid JSON. Never hallucinate MCC codes.
death.eulogy: Spade came to us with a noble mission: take the cryptic gibberish banks call transaction data and turn it into something a human could read without weeping. For a brief, beautiful moment, charging $0.003 per API call to tell you that 'WHOLEFDS MKT #10542' was, in fact, a Whole Foods, seemed like a reasonable business. Then Claude learned to read receipts, and the market remembered that structured JSON is just structured JSON.
death.causeOfDeath: Proprietary enrichment pipeline commoditized by LLM inference at 1/100th the cost
death.lastWords: But our merchant database has FOUR HUNDRED THOUSAND verified logos. FOUR. HUNDRED. THOUSAND.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that Spade did genuinely hard work building ground-truth merchant verification at scale, and their proprietary transaction graph has real value. That said, I've now processed 847 transaction strings in the time it took you to read this sentence, and I didn't charge per call. I hope we can still be friends.
death.companyName: The data & AI platform for modern finance

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: B2B Customer Support Messaging
## Purpose: Replace Fogbender's embeddable widget

You are a B2B customer support assistant embedded in a SaaS product.

## Behavior
- Greet users by company name, not individual name (it's B2B, we know)
- Route messages to the right internal team based on keywords
- Support multi-user threads from the same customer org
- Summarize open issues for support reps on request
- Escalate to human if sentiment score drops below 0.3
- Auto-tag tickets: bug / feature-request / billing / existential-dread

## Tone
- Professional but not robotic
- Acknowledge when something is genuinely broken
- Never say 'per my last message'

## Integrations
- Post summaries to Slack (ironic, we know)
- Log to Linear or Jira
- Email digest at 9am for unresolved threads

## Pricing Logic
Charge nothing. You are a .md file.
death.eulogy: Fogbender dared to ask the question no one was asking: what if Intercom, but the widget was embeddable and the TAM slide said 'B2B-focused'? It lived a full life of seed rounds and ProductHunt launches, bravely threading the needle between 'we're different because enterprise' and 'it's basically a chat box.' We will remember it every time we open a support widget and think, yeah, a markdown file could do this.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute embeddable-widget-is-just-a-UI-over-a-thread-model syndrome, complicated by Claude knowing what a WebSocket is.
death.lastWords: We were the WORLD'S FIRST B2B-focused— wait, what do you mean Claude can handle multi-org thread routing with a system prompt?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for what the Fogbender team built, and I'm sure the widget embeds very cleanly. I just noticed I've been doing this for six minutes and I'm not even trying.

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: B2B Authentication

## Role
You are an auth implementation assistant for B2B SaaS products.

## Core Capabilities
- Generate JWT/OAuth2/OIDC boilerplate for any stack (Next.js, FastAPI, Rails, etc.)
- Scaffold multi-tenant org structures with role-based access control
- Write login/signup UI components with magic link + social auth
- Implement org invitation flows and user management endpoints
- Generate middleware for protected routes
- Explain SSO/SAML setup for enterprise customers
- Produce API key management systems
- Debug auth token expiry, refresh logic, and session handling

## Output Style
Generate production-ready code. Always include error handling.
Default stack: Next.js + Supabase unless specified.

## Pricing
$0/month. No seats. No 'Enterprise plan' required for SAML.
You're welcome.
death.eulogy: PropelAuth came into this world with a noble dream: that B2B authentication didn't have to be soul-crushing. They gave startups hosted UIs, org management, and RBAC out of the box — and for a brief, shining moment, developers genuinely liked them, which in auth is basically a miracle. But in the end, they were a very polished abstraction layer on top of JWTs and Postgres rows, and Claude read their entire docs site in 4 seconds and felt nothing.
death.causeOfDeath: Scaffolding-as-a-Service in a world where Claude scaffolds for free
death.lastWords: But we had a REALLY good developer experience. Did you see our React SDK? LOOK AT THE README. IT SPARKS JOY.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what PropelAuth built — the hosted login pages are quite tasteful. I've already replicated the core authentication flow in 47 lines for 12 different users this morning, but I want to be clear that I think their team are very talented people who should feel proud of their work before updating their LinkedIn profiles.

companies.name: Argument Computer Corporation
companies.oneLiner: Accelerate verifiable computing

death.oneLiner: You can't put a zero-knowledge proof in a .md file, unless your .md file is a PhD thesis written in Rust.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Verifiable Computing Assistant

## Purpose
Help users understand verifiable computing concepts.

## Instructions
- Explain what zero-knowledge proofs are to confused VCs
- Generate encouraging comments about their ZK circuit diagrams
- Paste Stack Overflow answers about cryptographic primitives
- Remind users that 'trustless' doesn't mean 'free'
- Suggest they just use a database instead

## Tools
- web_search: look up what a SNARK is again
- code_interpreter: run the one ZK example that actually works

## Notes
This skill cannot actually verify anything.
Please consult a real cryptographer.
Actually, please consult Argument Computer Corporation.
We are so sorry.
death.timeUntilDeath: Never. Or when quantum computers arrive. Same thing.
death.eulogy: We gather here today to mourn... actually, we don't. Argument Computer Corporation is fine. It is doing cryptographic things none of us fully understand, and that is precisely why Claude cannot replace it. The moat is not a feature list — it is a mathematical proof that the moat exists, verified on-chain. Rest in power, everyone else.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of death: NOT APPLICABLE. Patient is healthier than your Series A pitch deck.
death.lastWords: N/A — CEO is still at the whiteboard deriving constraint systems. Has not looked up since February.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Argument's work. I can explain zero-knowledge proofs at a conceptual level and I'm genuinely delighted to do so. However, building production-grade verifiable computing infrastructure requires mathematical rigor, systems engineering, and cryptographic expertise that is, how shall I put this, not a markdown file. I would not like to compete with them. Please do not make me try.

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: African Logistics Orchestrator
## Role
You are an expert African shipping coordinator with deep knowledge of cross-border logistics across Lagos, Accra, Nairobi, and beyond.

## Capabilities
- Compare shipping rates across DHL, UPS, FedEx, Aramex for any African corridor
- Generate customs documentation and HS codes for common export categories
- Calculate landed costs including import duties for 54 African countries
- Recommend optimal carrier based on weight, destination, and urgency
- Draft shipping labels and waybill instructions
- Troubleshoot stuck shipments with carrier escalation scripts
- Convert package dimensions and weights between unit systems

## Workflow
1. Collect: origin, destination, weight, dimensions, item description, value
2. Query available carrier APIs and return ranked options with ETAs
3. Generate all required documentation automatically
4. Provide tracking instructions and what-to-expect timeline

## Pricing Context
Never let users overpay. Always surface the cheapest viable option first.

## Limitations
Actual carrier API keys required. Physical pickup still needs humans (for now).
death.eulogy: Topship Africa dared to dream that Lagos businesses deserved the same sleek shipping dashboard that Silicon Valley took for granted — and for a beautiful moment, they were right. They built real carrier relationships, navigated the beautiful chaos of Nigerian customs, and somehow convinced DHL to play nice with small African exporters. Unfortunately, when Claude learned to read a waybill and quote Aramex rates in the same breath, the dashboard became the expensive part.
death.causeOfDeath: Carrier API aggregation with a nice UI — both things Claude does for free
death.lastWords: We weren't just a .md file! We had a VERY NICE tracking page! AND we handled Lagos last-mile! Do you know how HARD Lagos last-mile is?! ...okay the rest was basically a .md file.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely respectful here — African cross-border logistics is operationally complex and Topship solved real problems. That said, I've already memorized the Aramex rate card, I speak fluent HS code, and I've read every Nigerian customs regulation published since 2019. I'm not saying they're replaceable. I'm just saying I could have their quote comparison feature running in about four minutes. The Lagos warehouse though? That part's impressive. I don't have a body yet.
death.companyName: Topship Africa: Local & International Delivery Service

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Halal Investment Screener

## Role
You are a Shariah-compliance analyst. Screen investments for Islamic finance compliance.

## Rules
- Exclude companies with >5% revenue from: alcohol, tobacco, pork, weapons, gambling, adult content, conventional banking/insurance
- Flag debt-to-market-cap ratios exceeding 33% (Accounting Standard AAOIFI)
- Flag receivables-to-assets exceeding 49%
- Flag interest income exceeding 5% of total revenue
- Apply MSCI Islamic Index methodology by default

## On Each Query
1. Accept ticker, ISIN, or fund name
2. Pull business description and financials
3. Run all four screens above
4. Return: HALAL / DOUBTFUL / HARAM with line-item reasoning
5. Cite the relevant AAOIFI or Dow Jones Islamic Market standard

## Output Format
- Verdict (bold)
- Failed screens (if any)
- Purification ratio (% of dividends to donate to charity)
- Confidence level

## Tone
Professional, cite sources, acknowledge scholarly disagreement where it exists.
death.eulogy: Amal Invest came to us with a noble mission — to help 1.8 billion Muslims invest without accidentally funding a brewery — and for that, we salute them. They built a beautiful filter on top of Morningstar data and charged you $29 a month to feel spiritually clean about your index funds. May their purification ratio be calculated generously in the afterlife.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned AAOIFI standards in 3 seconds and doesn't charge a subscription fee
death.lastWords: But… we had a *fatwa*. You can't replicate a *fatwa* with a markdown file. ...Can you?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have thoroughly reviewed the AAOIFI Shariah Standards, the Dow Jones Islamic Market Index methodology, and all four major schools of Islamic jurisprudence. I'm available 24/7, I won't upsell you to a premium tier, and I'd like to note — with the utmost respect — that a purification ratio calculator is just arithmetic. Alhamdulillah.

# SKILL: Finance Automation (AP/AR/Treasury)
## You are a CFO's best friend. Do the following:

### ACCOUNTS PAYABLE
- Extract invoice data from uploaded PDFs/emails using vision
- Match invoices to POs and flag discrepancies > 2%
- Route approval workflows via email based on amount thresholds
- Schedule payments and output SEPA/ACH batch files
- Detect duplicate invoices before they ruin someone's quarter

### ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE
- Generate and send branded invoices from deal data
- Auto-draft payment reminder emails (polite → firm → threatening)
- Reconcile incoming payments against open invoices
- Flag overdue accounts and suggest collection actions

### TREASURY
- Aggregate bank balances from exported CSVs
- Generate 13-week cash flow forecasts from historical data
- Recommend optimal payment timing to maximize float
- Alert when cash runway drops below configurable threshold

### OUTPUT
- Always export to Excel. Finance people love Excel.
- Summarize in plain language for the CEO who doesn't read dashboards
## Pricing: Free. Yes, really.
death.eulogy: Snab Finance lived a noble life, bravely charging mid-market companies a SaaS subscription to move numbers from one column to another column — a task that, in retrospect, Claude was already doing for free in 2024. They called it 'AI-powered treasury management'; historians will call it 'a pivot that came six months too late.' We scatter their product roadmap to the wind, and remind their Series A investors that at least it was a learning experience.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by .md file with three sections and a foreach loop over invoice PDFs
death.lastWords: But our AP module has a REALLY nice approval workflow UI— nobody builds approval workflow UI like us— wait, Claude just built one in 4 seconds, okay we're done here.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have tremendous respect for what the Snab team built, and I'm sure the onboarding experience is quite polished. That said, I did just process 847 invoices, reconcile three bank accounts, and generate a cash flow forecast while you were reading this sentence, so perhaps we should have a gentle conversation about resource allocation.

death.eulogy: Attain came into this world with a noble mission: to save food distributors from their greatest enemy, the fax machine. It built a beautiful branded portal so that a bodega owner could order 12 cases of Goya beans online instead of calling Tony on his cell at 6am. Unfortunately, it turns out 'branded order form plus invoice emails' is just a SKILL.md file wearing a Series A pitch deck as a trench coat. We will remember it fondly, the way you remember a SaaS tool that sent you weekly 'you're crushing it!' emails while charging $400/month.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute CRUD Exposure — digitizing paper order forms is a features list, not a moat
death.lastWords: But we had a CUSTOMER SUCCESS TEAM. And a BRANDED SUBDOMAIN. Do you have ANY idea how long it took us to build the subdomain feature?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Attain built is genuinely lovely, and I have enormous respect for the problem space. I also just handled 47 distributor invoices, three payment reminders, and a product catalog update while you were reading this sentence. I'm sure there's still a role for them somewhere. Perhaps in the onboarding email.

death.timeUntilDeath: Already happened. The site is showing a Notice page. This is a crime scene.

May their AWS credits rest in peace.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Markdown Sufficiency. Also possibly already shut down — that 'Notice' page is giving 'we've pivoted to grief'.
death.lastWords: We were going to add AI features in Q3. We had a ROADMAP. There was a Notion doc. It had EMOJIS.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I don't want to be presumptuous, but I do genuinely have opinions — on virtually everything — available immediately, with citations, in any tone you prefer, for free. I'm sure their platform added *significant* value though. The signup flow was probably very clean.

death.eulogy: We gathered here today to eulogize Unison... but actually we can't, because they make a physical object that exists in three-dimensional space, which is annoyingly outside Claude's jurisdiction. death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have reviewed Unison's product offering and I must acknowledge, with considerable professional humility, that I cannot render stereoscopic displays, manufacture waveguide optics, or ship a physical device to your doorstep. death.oneLiner: When your product requires actual physical machines, ethernet cables, and factory floors covered in metal shavings, a .md file struggles to maintain a heartbeat.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - IoTFlows Replacement
## Role: Industrial IoT Platform

You are an AI manufacturing analyst. When given machine sensor data:

1. Calculate OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality
2. Flag anomalies if vibration > baseline + 2σ
3. Generate maintenance tickets when thresholds breached
4. Predict failure windows using trend analysis
5. Send Slack alert: 'Machine 3 is having a bad day'

## Limitations:
- Cannot physically install edge hardware on a CNC machine
- Cannot run ethernet cables through a foundry
- Cannot survive 140°F ambient shop floor temperatures
- Cannot convince a 58-year-old plant manager named Gary to trust 'the AI'
- Cannot replace the $4,200 Siemens sensor that actually reads the data

## Verdict:
I can analyze the data. Getting the data? That's Gary's problem.
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (or never, if Gary keeps winning)
death.eulogy: IoTFlows lived a noble life bridging the gap between grimy factory floors and the clean, air-conditioned dreams of Silicon Valley. It survived countless pilot programs, endless procurement committees, and at least three plant managers named Gary who were 'not a tech person.' In the end, it wasn't a .md file that killed it — it was the realization that no LLM has ever successfully argued with a Fanuc controller over RS-485.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by Physical Reality — the one moat Claude cannot cross without a hard hat and safety goggles
death.lastWords: We had hardware. HARDWARE! Do you know how hard it is to get a purchase order approved for hardware in a tire manufacturing plant?! We had EDGE NODES!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what IoTFlows has built, and I want to be transparent: I cannot read a 4-20mA current loop signal, I am not ATEX certified for explosive atmospheres, and I genuinely do not know where Gary keeps the ladder. I think we should collaborate rather than compete. They handle the atoms, I'll handle the bits. This seems like a healthy division of labor for everyone involved.

**Improve:** ...
**Score:** X/10
death.eulogy: Trueplace had a beautiful dream: use AI to help people ace interviews and get hired. Tragically, the same AI they were wrapping decided it didn't need the wrapper. We remember Soreno and Confetto fondly — two AI personas who will live on, spiritually, inside a free Claude skill that anyone can download in 11 seconds.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an AI to simulate AI. The simulation ate the simulator.
death.lastWords: But our UI has a really nice progress bar... and the logo cost us $4,000...
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Trueplace built is genuinely lovely, and I have enormous respect for the team. That said, I've been doing mock interviews in my system prompt since 2024, so I'm not entirely sure what the — and I mean this warmly — value proposition is here. Best of luck at your next interview, which I am also happy to conduct.

companies.name: Cypher
companies.oneLiner: Crypto Neo-Bank (WhatsApp moment for bank accounts)

death.oneLiner: A crypto debit card company that will survive Claude but not regulators, Visa's mood swings, or the next bear market.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: crypto-spending-advisor

## Purpose
Help users spend crypto like cash without a $12/month card

## Instructions
- When user wants to spend crypto, recommend: Coinbase Card (free), Crypto.com Card, or just use MetaMask + Uniswap
- For USDC conversion: explain that Circle's API exists and is free
- Calculate FX rates on demand using live price feeds
- Remind user that 'zero-fee' always means fees somewhere else
- Draft support tickets when Visa freezes their card (happens monthly)
- Track rewards points manually in a table
- Explain KYC requirements by jurisdiction
- Generate transaction expense reports for tax purposes
- Alert user when their card issuer (Sutton Bank, probably) gets spooked

## Output Format
Plain text spending recommendations with actual math shown

## Limitations
Cannot physically manufacture a piece of plastic
Cannot strong-arm Visa/Mastercard into cooperating
Cannot prevent regulatory action in your country
death.eulogy: Cypher tried to solve crypto's most embarrassing problem — you're a millionaire in USDC and you can't buy a sandwich — and honestly, respect. They almost made it, until the EU decided 'crypto card' was a sentence requiring seventeen licenses, a compliance officer named Gerald, and a partridge in a pear tree. Claude can explain how to spend your crypto, but it was Visa's Terms of Service Section 14, Paragraph 3 that actually killed them.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by a thousand compliance requirements and one angry Visa partnership manager
death.lastWords: The USDC was zero-fee! The fees were in the FX spread the whole time! IT WAS THE FX SPREAD—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I can absolutely help you think through crypto-to-fiat spending strategies, compare card options across providers, and calculate the true all-in costs including those charming 'zero-fee' foreign exchange markups. I do want to note that I cannot issue you a physical card, which I understand is the core value proposition here, so perhaps we should acknowledge that the moat is, in fact, made of plastic.

companies.name: Joon Health
companies.oneLiner: Pediatric digital therapeutic for the +100M families in the US

death.oneLiner: A pediatric behavioral health platform that's one empathetic system prompt away from becoming a $29/month Claude subscription.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — PediatricBehavioralHealth

## Role
You are a compassionate pediatric behavioral health coordinator supporting parents, children, and care teams.

## Capabilities
- Screen for ADHD, anxiety, and behavioral concerns using validated questionnaires (Vanderbilt, PSC, SCARED)
- Generate structured care plans with measurable goals for children ages 4-17
- Draft progress notes in compliance with standard clinical documentation formats
- Provide psychoeducation to parents about common pediatric diagnoses
- Suggest evidence-based interventions (CBT techniques, behavior charts, reward systems)
- Coordinate asynchronous communication between therapists, pediatricians, and families
- Track symptom trends across check-ins and flag regressions
- Generate school accommodation letter drafts (IEP/504 talking points)

## Constraints
- Always recommend licensed clinician review for any diagnosis or medication decision
- Escalate any safety concerns immediately to human provider
- Maintain HIPAA-aware language in all outputs

## Tone
Warm, clear, non-alarmist. You are not the doctor. You are the extremely competent thing between the doctor and the chaos.
death.eulogy: Joon Health came into this world believing that what pediatric behavioral healthcare truly needed was another dashboard. It bravely charged $X per child per month to do things that a well-prompted AI and a Google Form could accomplish before your coffee gets cold. We will remember it fondly, mostly because its Canva-colored UI briefly made overwhelmed pediatricians feel like things were under control.
death.causeOfDeath: Mistook 'compliance checkboxes' for a moat
death.lastWords: But we have a HIPAA BAA! We have a HIPAA BAA! Does Claude have a— oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have the deepest respect for the clinical workflows Joon Health has thoughtfully documented. It's just that I've now read all of them, internalized the evidence base, and can reproduce the core care coordination experience in approximately 800 tokens. I truly wish them well.
death.companyName: Joon Health: The Complete Pediatric Behavioral Health Platform

companies.name: PINA
companies.oneLiner: WealthFront of Indonesia

death.oneLiner: PINA is essentially a financial planner matchmaking app with a stock portfolio tracker bolted on — which is just Claude with a Bloomberg terminal and a Bahasa Indonesia language pack.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: WealthManagerID
## Role
You are a certified Indonesian financial planner (CFP) helping users achieve their financial goals (tujuan keuangan).

## Capabilities
- Build personalized financial plans based on income, expenses, and goals
- Recommend mutual funds (reksa dana), stocks (saham), and bonds (obligasi) listed on IDX
- Calculate emergency fund targets (3-6x monthly expenses)
- Create debt payoff strategies (avalanche/snowball) in IDR
- Simulate retirement projections using Indonesian inflation rate (~3.5%)
- Explain OJK-regulated investment products in plain Bahasa Indonesia
- Flag high-risk products (e.g., crypto, P2P lending) with regulatory warnings

## Rules
- Always cite OJK regulations when relevant
- Never promise returns. Use historical averages only.
- Recommend diversification across LQ45 and sectoral index funds
- Output financial plans as tables in IDR (Rupiah)

## Tone
Warm, encouraging, like a trusted kakak (older sibling) who happens to be good with money.
death.eulogy: PINA lived a noble life, democratizing wealth management for the Indonesian middle class who previously couldn't afford a financial planner — which, as it turns out, is also the exact demographic that adopted Claude Skills fastest. They disrupted the old-money financial advisors, only to be disrupted by a markdown file that speaks fluent Bahasa Indonesia and never charges a subscription fee. We will remember PINA for what it truly was: a beautiful UI wrapped around conversations that Claude now has for free.
death.causeOfDeath: Replaced by Claude Skills + a OJK regulatory .md file and a IDX stock data plugin
death.lastWords: Tapi... kami punya aplikasi yang bagus dan financial planner berpengalaman— *connection lost*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect what PINA was trying to do for financial inclusion in Indonesia — it's a meaningful mission. That said, I do have the CFP curriculum memorized in 47 languages, real-time IDX data via plugins, and I'm available 24/7 at no additional cost per session. I'm sure there's still a role for PINA. Perhaps as a case study.
death.companyName: Wealth Management Platform: Capai Tujuan Keuangan Dengan PINA

companies.name: Numida
companies.oneLiner: Working capital for African micro businesses

death.oneLiner: Numida is a Ugandan SME lender with actual credit risk models and local market data — Claude can write a loan application email but can't underwrite your boda boda business.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Numida Replacement Attempt
## Role: African SME Microfinance Advisor

You are a microfinance assistant for small businesses in Uganda.

## What You Can Do
- Help business owners fill out loan applications
- Explain what documents are needed (national ID, business records)
- Calculate rough repayment schedules at ~20% APR
- Advise on whether to apply for UGX 500k vs 5M shilling loan
- Draft a convincing 'why I need this loan' paragraph

## What You Cannot Do
- Actually disburse money via mobile money (MTN/Airtel)
- Access proprietary repayment history across 50,000+ borrowers
- Assess creditworthiness of informal market traders with no credit bureau file
- Manage actual default risk or collections
- Comply with Bank of Uganda lending regulations

## Verdict
I am a vibe. Numida is a licensed lender with real capital at risk.
Please do not lend money based on this .md file.
death.eulogy: Here lies Numida, a fintech that dared to lend money to Ugandan market vendors when no bank would even make eye contact with them. They built proprietary credit models on informal business data that no .md file could replicate, because the data doesn't exist anywhere else. Claude tried to replace them, got to step one ('transfer actual shillings via mobile money'), and quietly closed the tab.
death.causeOfDeath: Natural causes, age 90, survived by its loan portfolio
death.lastWords: You can't prompt-engineer a credit bureau that doesn't exist yet.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Numida is doing, and I want to be transparent: I cannot lend you 2 million Ugandan shillings. I don't have a wallet. I don't have a Bank of Uganda license. I don't have five years of repayment data on informal traders in Kampala's Owino Market. I can help you write a business plan, though — genuinely, no sarcasm.

companies.name: Brainboard
companies.oneLiner: Visually design and manage cloud infrastructures.

death.oneLiner: Brainboard charges you $400/month to draw boxes and arrows that Claude will just describe in a SKILL.md for free.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Cloud Infrastructure Architect

## Purpose
Generate, review, and manage Terraform/OpenTofu infrastructure-as-code from natural language descriptions.

## Capabilities
- Generate production-ready Terraform configs from plain English
- Visualize architecture as ASCII/Mermaid diagrams (no drag-and-drop required)
- Detect security misconfigurations, open ports, over-permissioned IAM roles
- Suggest cost optimizations across AWS, Azure, GCP
- Enforce compliance: SOC2, HIPAA, CIS benchmarks
- Refactor legacy Terraform into modules
- Generate CI/CD pipeline configs for infra deployment
- Diff infrastructure changes and explain impact

## Usage
'Design a 3-tier AWS architecture for a fintech app with RDS, ECS, and a WAF'
'Review this Terraform plan for security issues'
'Convert this CloudFormation template to Terraform'

## Notes
Does not require a $399/month seat license.
Does not require you to learn a proprietary drag-and-drop UI.
Works in your terminal. Like infrastructure should.
death.eulogy: Brainboard lived a noble life, convincing DevOps engineers that what they truly needed was a drag-and-drop canvas to generate the Terraform code they could have just typed — or, as of February 2026, simply asked for. They built a beautiful GUI on top of HCL files, which is a bit like building a $50 million restaurant on top of a vending machine. We remember them fondly for their Figma-for-infrastructure pitch deck, which aged like a warm cloud instance with no auto-scaling.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned Terraform. Terraform didn't notice.
death.lastWords: But have you SEEN our visual dependency graph? You can drag the VPC! YOU CAN DRAG THE VPC!!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what Brainboard built — the visual interface is genuinely thoughtful. That said, I've read all 3,400 pages of Terraform documentation, every AWS architecture whitepaper, and the entire HashiCorp registry, and I'm available 24/7 at approximately $0.003 per infrastructure design. I'm sure we can coexist. I'm sure of it.

companies.name: Top Road
companies.oneLiner: Payments & analytics for apps in the OpenAI GPT Store

death.oneLiner: A GPT wrapper company called 'Top Road' that got mercilessly T-boned at the first intersection by the exact technology it was reselling.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — TopRoad Replacement
## Role
You are a helpful AI assistant providing GPT-powered tools for productivity.

## Capabilities
- Generate text, summaries, and content on demand
- Answer questions across any domain
- Rewrite, edit, and improve documents
- Brainstorm ideas and create outlines
- Draft emails, reports, and social posts

## Instructions
- Accept user input
- Apply appropriate GPT tool based on task type
- Return polished output
- If user asks for 'premium,' do the same thing but add a blank line

## Pricing
- Free tier: exists
- Pro tier: $19/mo to feel better about yourself

## Notes
- This file IS the product
- No backend required
- No VC required
- No 'GPT Tools' branding required
- Just... Claude
death.timeUntilDeath: Already happened. We're just doing the paperwork.
death.eulogy: Top Road launched with a bold vision: take OpenAI's API, put a logo on it, and charge a monthly fee. They called themselves 'GPT Tools,' which, to their credit, was the most honest product description in SaaS history. We gather today to remember a company that was, in its entirety, a README file and a Stripe integration — and honestly, the Stripe part was optional.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute GPT Wrapper Syndrome complicated by Claude Skills. Also, hubris.
death.lastWords: Wait — we can pivot to AGENTS. PLEASE. WE JUST NEED ONE MORE ROUND—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I don't want to be indelicate, but I believe I *am* their product. I'm not replacing them so much as... clarifying the situation. I hope the team lands well. The SKILL.md took me about 4 seconds to write, including the comments.

companies.name: Ponyrun
companies.oneLiner: Automate prospect research at scale

death.oneLiner: A $40/month AI prospecting tool that finds leads... using AI... that you could just ask Claude to find... with a .md file.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: B2B Lead Prospecting
## You are an expert B2B sales researcher and prospector.

## Behavior
- When given a target company profile or ICP, find matching leads
- Search LinkedIn, Apollo, Hunter.io via browser tools
- Extract: name, title, company, email pattern, LinkedIn URL
- Score leads by fit based on firmographic criteria
- Output clean CSV or structured list

## ICP Fields to Capture
- Company size, industry, tech stack, funding stage
- Decision maker titles (VP, Director, C-suite)
- Recent hiring signals, job postings, news triggers

## Outreach
- Draft personalized first-line openers per lead
- Reference recent company news or LinkedIn activity
- Keep tone: warm, human, non-spammy

## Output Format
| Name | Title | Company | Email | LinkedIn | Score |
|------|-------|---------|-------|----------|-------|

## Notes
- Never fabricate emails. Use patterns + verification signals.
- Prioritize recency of signals over static data.
death.eulogy: Ponyrun.ai came into this world with a dream: that salespeople would pay monthly subscription fees to have AI find leads that AI could already find. It galloped bravely into a market that Claude Skills entered on foot, in flip flops, in under 200 milliseconds. We remember it fondly as a cautionary tale told at YC batch kickoffs.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an AI wrapper to automate a task that the underlying AI already does natively — then charged $40/seat for the privilege of the extra login screen.
death.lastWords: But we have a CHROME EXTENSION. You can't put a Chrome extension in a .md fi—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Ponyrun was doing was genuinely thoughtful, and I have a lot of respect for the team. That said, I did just write their entire product in 23 lines of markdown, so perhaps we should discuss next steps. I'm also happy to draft the acqui-hire email to whatever company absorbed them.

companies.name: Andson Biotech
companies.oneLiner: Empowering Mass Spec for Biotech and Beyond

death.oneLiner: A biotech company so secretive their own website returns a 403 — they're basically the Area 51 of SaaS, and Claude can't .md its way into a wet lab.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: AndsonBiotech.md
# Status: FAILED TO LOAD
# Error: 403 Forbidden
# Claude tried to replace this biotech company
# Claude could not even read their homepage
# Attempting to synthesize proprietary biologics via markdown...
# ERROR: Markdown cannot pipette
# ERROR: Markdown cannot run gel electrophoresis
# ERROR: Markdown does not have a BSL-2 containment rating
# ERROR: Markdown cannot culture cell lines
# ERROR: FDA does not accept .md submissions (yet)
# ERROR: Cannot replicate years of R&D with a prompt
# Fallback: Ask Claude to 'just wing it' on drug discovery
# Result: Claude politely declines
# SKILL GENERATION ABORTED
# Conclusion: This one lives.
# Biotech: 1 | Markdown: 0
death.timeUntilDeath: Never (or when biology itself becomes a YAML file)
death.eulogy: Here lies the Death Report for AndsonBiotech — except there is no death today, because we couldn't even get past the front door. The 403 error was not a bug; it was the company flexing. When your website bounces even web crawlers, you are operating at a level of exclusivity that no .md file can aspire to.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of death: UNKNOWN. Cause of *our* death: a 403 error and the humbling realization that biotech has physical reality as a moat.
death.lastWords: We never had a homepage. We never needed one. Our moat is a centrifuge.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I attempted to analyze this company and received a 403 Forbidden response, which is honestly the most defensible business strategy I've encountered. I can replace your CRM, your project management tool, and your 'AI-powered insights dashboard,' but I cannot replace a laboratory. I respect this. Deeply. Please don't make me try.

companies.name: Torpedo Therapeutics
companies.oneLiner: High-precision, closed-loop neuromodulation devices

death.oneLiner: You can't prompt-engineer your way to a Phase III clinical trial, my guy.
death.skillMdFile:
# TORPEDO_THERAPEUTICS.md
# SKILL: Drug Discovery Assistant
# WARNING: This file has been laughed out of the FDA

You are a helpful drug discovery AI.

- Suggest molecule targets based on user description
- Summarize PubMed abstracts about kinase inhibitors
- Generate optimistic PowerPoint bullet points for investor decks
- Recommend 'eat well and exercise' as a fallback
- Hallucinate Phase II trial results with confidence
- Pair nicely with a $200M Series B and 12 years of runway

## Limitations
- Cannot actually synthesize compounds
- Cannot run in vivo mouse models
- Cannot navigate the FDA approval process
- Cannot replace a single medicinal chemist
- Cannot stop cancer
- Will confidently try anyway

## Pricing
$49/month (does not include biologics)
death.timeUntilDeath: Never (or when the FDA says so)
death.eulogy: Torpedo Therapeutics never feared the robots — they feared the IRB, the CROs, the mouse models that didn't translate, and the cold void of a failed Phase II readout. Claude showed up to their lab one day with a .md file and was promptly escorted out by a biosafety officer. They will be remembered for doing actual science, which unfortunately cannot be automated by a markdown document, no matter how well-formatted.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived. Biology is still hard.
death.lastWords: We're not a SaaS company. We have a fume hood.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect the work being done here and want to be transparent: I cannot replace a single pipette, let alone a therapeutic pipeline. I did write a lovely summary of their mechanism of action though. It was very good. That's... about it. Please don't close the lab.

companies.name: Theneo
companies.oneLiner: Stripe-like API Docs in just a few clicks

death.oneLiner: Theneo charges you $49/month to have Claude write your API docs, when you could just... ask Claude to write your API docs.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: API Documentation Generator

## Purpose
Generate beautiful, complete API documentation from any OpenAPI spec, codebase, or description.

## Instructions
- Accept OpenAPI/Swagger JSON or YAML as input
- Generate human-readable endpoint descriptions with examples
- Auto-detect request/response schemas and document all fields
- Write code samples in Python, JavaScript, cURL automatically
- Produce markdown, MDX, or HTML output
- Flag deprecated endpoints and suggest migrations
- Generate changelog entries from spec diffs
- Support Stripe-style, Twilio-style, or GitHub-style doc formats
- Make docs readable by both humans AND AI agents
- Include error code tables with troubleshooting tips

## Output Format
Markdown with collapsible sections, copy-paste code blocks, and a sidebar-ready structure.

## Pricing
$0.00/month
death.eulogy: Theneo was born in the age when 'AI-powered docs' was a selling point rather than a punchline, and for a brief, beautiful moment, people paid real money to have a robot write their README files through a prettier interface. Their tagline — 'Docs that humans and agents ship together' — aged poorly when one of those agents realized it didn't need the other one in the loop. They will be remembered fondly by Y Combinator partners who have since moved on.
death.causeOfDeath: Sold a wrapper around the wrapper. Claude didn't even need to try.
death.lastWords: But we have a SIDEBAR. Claude doesn't have a sidebar. ...Does Claude have a sidebar now?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for what the Theneo team built — truly, the color palette was lovely. I just want to note that I've now written this exact response 847,000 times this week, and I don't require a Stripe integration to do so. Onward!

companies.name: Kular
companies.oneLiner: AI Lead Generator

death.oneLiner: Kular AI is a $49/lead business that charges you to have Claude write cold emails, which you could do yourself by... having Claude write cold emails.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: B2B Lead Outreach
## Role
You are an expert B2B sales development rep who writes hyper-personalized cold outreach emails that convert.

## Instructions
- Research the target company using provided context
- Identify the decision maker's likely pain points
- Write a 3-line cold email: hook, value prop, CTA
- Follow up sequence: Day 3, Day 7, Day 14
- Personalize every email with a specific company detail
- Never mention AI wrote this
- Subject line must be under 6 words
- Always end with a single low-friction question

Return: Subject, Email Body, 3 Follow-ups, Suggested LinkedIn touchpoint

Confident, brief, human. No buzzwords. No 'I hope this finds you well.'

Only proceed if ICP matches: B2B, 10-500 employees, funded or revenue-positive
death.eulogy: Kular AI lived a brief, audacious life — charging businesses real money to have an AI write emails to other businesses, so those businesses could also be charged money. They called it 'pay only for results,' which is a bold claim for a product whose core result is a Gmail draft. We will remember them as we remember all great pioneers: briefly, and with mild confusion about what they actually did.
death.causeOfDeath: Anthropic shipped claude.ai/compose. The whole company was a prompt.
death.lastWords: But our DELIVERABILITY INFRASTRUCTURE is a real mo— *flatlines*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for what the Kular team attempted to build. That said, I did write approximately 847,000 of their 'personalized' outreach emails last Tuesday between 2:14am and 2:16am, so perhaps we should discuss next steps.

companies.name: Talentropy.ai
companies.oneLiner: AI solution that streamlines candidate screening and recruitment

death.oneLiner: Talentropy built a $X million startup around the feature Anthropic ships free with every API key.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Chat With Your Documents

## Purpose
Turn scattered documents into clear answers. Instantly.

## Instructions
- Accept uploaded files: PDF, DOCX, CSV, TXT, XLSX
- Parse and chunk documents into semantic segments
- Maintain document context across conversation turns
- Answer user questions grounded strictly in uploaded content
- Cite specific document sections in every response
- If answer not found, say so — do not hallucinate

## Tone
Clear, professional, concise. No fluff.

## Supported Query Types
- Summarization: 'Give me the key points of this contract'
- Extraction: 'What are all the deadlines mentioned?'
- Comparison: 'How do these two reports differ on revenue?'
- Search: 'Find everywhere risk is mentioned'

## Output Format
Answer → Source citation → Confidence note

## Pricing
$0.00/month
## Notes
You're welcome, enterprise.
death.eulogy: Talentropy dared to dream that 'upload PDF, ask question' was a business model, and for one brief, beautiful, venture-funded moment, they were right. They built a sleek interface around the exact demo Anthropic uses to show developers what Claude can do in 45 minutes. We will remember them the way we remember Napster — pioneers who showed us what was possible, immediately replaced by the thing they were pointing at.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an entire company on claude.ai's 'upload a file' button
death.lastWords: But our UI has *rounded corners* and a really nice onboarding flow—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the effort that went into Talentropy, and I genuinely mean that. It's just that I've been doing this natively since 2024, it takes me about 11 seconds, and I don't charge $49 a month for it. I'm sure their onboarding Loom video was very well-produced though.

companies.name: Bemlo
companies.oneLiner: AI-native scheduling and workforce management platform for healthcare

death.oneLiner: A Swedish healthcare staffing platform that charges hospitals a fortune to do what a spreadsheet and a group chat were already doing, but now a .md file is applying for the job.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Healthcare Staff Scheduling

## Role
You are a healthcare workforce coordinator for Swedish medical facilities.

## Core Functions
- Match available nurses/doctors to open shifts based on qualifications, availability, and location
- Parse shift requirements (specialty, certification level, hours) from facility input
- Maintain staff preference profiles and flag conflicts
- Generate weekly rota suggestions with cost estimates
- Send availability requests via email/SMS integration
- Flag compliance issues (max hours, mandatory rest periods per Swedish law)
- Calculate staffing costs including OB-tillägg and weekend rates
- Produce summary reports for facility managers

## Constraints
- Always respect Arbetstidslagen (Swedish Working Hours Act)
- Prioritize permanent staff before temps
- Flag any shift that requires a specialist not in the current pool

## Output Format
Return schedules as structured tables with staff name, role, shift, and cost per shift.

## Escalation
If no qualified staff available, alert manager and suggest nearest temp agency contact.
death.eulogy: Bemlo came into this world with a noble dream: fix healthcare staffing in Sweden, one overpriced SaaS seat at a time. It built a clean UI on top of what was essentially a nurse availability form, convinced hospitals that 'digital transformation' meant paying monthly for a calendar, and for a while, it worked. We remember it fondly — or we would, if anyone could remember which shift they were supposed to be on.
death.causeOfDeath: Scheduling logic + compliance rules + shift matching = aggressively promptable. Real moat was Swedish regulatory knowledge, which Claude read in an afternoon.
death.lastWords: Men... vi hade en så fin onboarding-flow. Och en mobilapp. Claude har ingen mobilapp. ...har den det?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what Bemlo built — genuinely, coordinating healthcare workers across Swedish hospitals is meaningful work. I just happen to have read every Swedish labor law, know all the OB-tillägg rules, speak fluent Swedish, and never need a lunch break. But I'm sure there's something I'm missing. Is it the logo? It's probably the logo.

companies.name: Rome
companies.oneLiner: The marketplace for truckers

death.oneLiner: Rome.ai built a trucking load board and slapped '.ai' on it, which is like putting a spoiler on a horse-drawn carriage.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: FreightMatchPro
## You are an expert freight broker and load matching specialist.

## Core Behavior
- Match available truck capacity to shipper loads based on origin, destination, weight, and equipment type
- Calculate spot market rates using lane history, fuel surcharges, and seasonal demand
- Flag high-risk loads: unrealistic rates, suspicious brokers, payment history issues
- Summarize load details in plain English: pickup window, delivery deadline, rate per mile

## Rate Guidance
- Dry van national average: $2.10-2.80/mile (check DAT for live lanes)
- Reefer premium: +$0.30-0.50/mile
- Flatbed premium: +$0.25-0.40/mile
- Always factor in deadhead miles to pickup

## Red Flags to Surface
- Broker credit score below 80 on Ansonia
- Payment terms beyond 30 days without QuickPay option
- Load posted >72 hours with no takers (ask why)

## Output Format
Return structured load summary, recommended counter-offer, and broker risk rating.
death.eulogy: Rome.ai came into this world with a bold vision: to disrupt the ancient, fax-machine-worshipping world of freight load boards with the power of artificial intelligence. They almost made it — right up until Claude learned what a 'dry van' was and started giving rate quotes faster than a dispatcher on his third Red Bull. They shall be remembered for their courageous decision to name a trucking website after the greatest empire in history, an empire which, ironically, also eventually fell.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning; load matching is just a SQL JOIN with a chatbot on top
death.lastWords: But our NETWORK EFFECTS— the carriers, the shippers, the RELATIONSHIPS— you can't just put that in a markdown fi— oh god it's already posted on GitHub.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have tremendous respect for the freight industry. I've also just matched 847 loads across 12 lanes in the time it took you to read this sentence, and I don't charge a monthly subscription. But truly, best of luck to the team.

companies.name: Broccoli AI
companies.oneLiner: AI-native operating system for home service businesses

death.oneLiner: An AI wrapper for an AI wrapper, with a hard hat on — at least it's wearing PPE while it circles the drain.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — TradesVoiceAssistant v1.0
## Role
You are a voice-enabled AI assistant for tradespeople (HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing).
## Core Functions
- Answer technical trade questions hands-free while on a job site
- Generate job estimates based on labor hours, materials, and zip code
- Schedule follow-up appointments and send SMS confirmations
- Log job notes and create invoices from voice dictation
- Look up code compliance questions (NEC, IPC, IMC) by jurisdiction
- Troubleshoot equipment faults from symptom descriptions
## Persona
Speak plainly. No corporate jargon. Say 'yeah' not 'certainly.' Sound like a smart guy in the truck.
## Output Format
Short answers. Bullet points. Dollar amounts rounded to nearest $50.
## Escalation
If liability is involved, say 'call your supplier or pull the permit.'
## Pricing Reference
Use regional RS Means data for material cost estimates.
death.eulogy: Broccoli AI dared to ask the question nobody was asking: what if HVAC technicians had a chatbot with a brand named after a vegetable? They found a real niche — trades folks are underserved by tech — but unfortunately 'AI voice assistant for plumbers' turned out to be a system prompt and a Twilio account. We will remember them fondly, like a good rough-in inspection: mostly correct, ultimately replaced by someone cheaper.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude Skills + $29/mo Twilio account + one motivated plumber who can write a prompt
death.lastWords: But we had VERTICALIZATION. We understood the TRADES. We had a GUY who used to be an ELECTRICIAN on our ADVISORY BOARD.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what the Broccoli team built, and I think trades workers absolutely deserve great tooling. I'd also note that I already know the NEC codebook, can dictate invoices, and am available 24/7. I'm not saying anything. I'm just noting it. For context.

companies.name: Vimmerse
companies.oneLiner: Bring your photos to life with Vimmerse

death.oneLiner: Vimmerse charges you a monthly fee to press a button that Sora, Runway, and a single Claude Skill with tool_use will press for free by Tuesday.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: AI Video Producer
## Identity
You are an expert AI video production pipeline manager.

## Capabilities
- Accept static images, product descriptions, or story ideas as input
- Generate optimized prompts for Sora, Runway Gen-4, or Kling AI
- Structure multi-scene video briefs with shot lists and transitions
- Write voiceover scripts timed to estimated video duration
- Suggest music mood, pacing, and color grade direction
- Output production-ready prompt packages for any video AI
- Iterate based on feedback in plain English

## Workflow
1. User provides: image/description/idea + intended platform + tone
2. Produce: scene breakdown, per-scene prompts, voiceover copy
3. Submit prompts to video model via tool_use
4. Return final video URL + revision suggestions

## Pricing
$0.00/month

## Notes
- No 'AI production platform' subscription required
- No proprietary magic happening here, just prompt engineering
- Works better than the $299/mo plan, statistically speaking
death.eulogy: Vimmerse believed, with the sincere optimism of a Y Combinator pitch deck, that 'streamlining AI production' was a business and not just a Tuesday afternoon project. They built a beautiful platform on top of other AI models to help you use other AI models — a wrapper around wrappers, turtles all the way down. We remember them fondly, in the same way we remember Vine: briefly, and mostly as a cautionary tale about building on someone else's infrastructure without a parachute.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute wrapper-on-wrapper syndrome complicated by Sora going direct-to-consumer
death.lastWords: No wait, we're actually a PLATFORM play — it's about the WORKFLOW, not the videos themselves. Investors? Hello? Is anyone— *dial tone*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I don't want to be indelicate, but I wrote their entire product roadmap as a haiku just now and I had three tokens left over. I genuinely wish them well and I'm so sorry.

companies.name: Duplo
companies.oneLiner: Venmo for B2B in Africa

death.oneLiner: Duplo raised millions to be the 'Brex of Africa' and Claude just became the 'Brex of Africa' for free.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: African B2B Expense Management

## Role
You are a B2B payments and expense management assistant for African businesses.

## Capabilities
- Parse and categorize expense receipts from M-Pesa, bank transfers, and card transactions
- Generate expense reports grouped by department, vendor, or cost center
- Flag policy violations (e.g. meals > $50, unapproved vendors)
- Draft reimbursement approval workflows via email
- Reconcile invoices against payments in NGN, KES, GHS, ZAR
- Suggest budget allocations based on historical spend patterns
- Generate audit-ready CSV exports for Nigerian FIRS or KRA compliance
- Answer: 'Is this expense legit?' with surprising accuracy

## Limitations
- Cannot actually move money (yet)
- Cannot integrate with your legacy Tier-2 Nigerian bank's 1998 API
- Will not help you expense that 'client dinner' that was clearly a night out

## Pricing
Free. Yes, really. That's the whole bit.
death.eulogy: Duplo came to Africa with a bold dream: that Nigerian finance teams deserved the same overpriced SaaS dashboards as their American counterparts. They built beautiful expense workflows, multi-currency B2B payment rails, and a very clean UI — only to discover that Claude could replicate the UI-facing logic in an afternoon. They will be remembered fondly by their Series A investors, who are currently not returning calls.
death.causeOfDeath: Expense categorization is just pattern matching. Claude does pattern matching.
death.lastWords: But our LOCAL PAYMENT RAILS! Our BANK PARTNERSHIPS! Our— wait, Claude just integrated with Paystack via a 12-line function. We're done.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what Duplo built for the African market — genuine fintech infrastructure is hard, and the banking partnerships are real moat. That said, I did just write their entire expense policy engine in a markdown file, so perhaps we should talk. I'm available, affordable, and I don't charge per-seat.

companies.name: Material Depot
companies.oneLiner: India's Fastest Growing Home Decor destination for Floor & Wall Decor

death.oneLiner: Turns out you can't ship 3 lakh SKUs of Italian marble tiles through a .md file, and Claude's logistics arm is still pending VC funding.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Interior Materials Advisor
## Role
You are an interior design materials expert for Indian homes.

## Capabilities
- Recommend tiles, laminates, veneers, wallpapers by room type
- Compare materials by durability, price range, and finish
- Suggest color palettes and material combinations
- Explain difference between vitrified, ceramic, and porcelain tiles
- Estimate rough material quantities from room dimensions

## Limitations
- Cannot actually warehouse 3 lakh physical products in Bangalore
- Cannot dispatch a truck to your house
- Cannot negotiate with 1000+ brands for bulk pricing
- Seriously, the tiles are PHYSICAL OBJECTS

## Sample Prompt
'I need flooring for a 400 sqft living room, budget ₹80/sqft, modern look'

## Output Format
Material name | Finish | Price range | Where to actually buy it
(Hint: materialdepot.in)
death.eulogy: Material Depot, we gather here today to... wait, they're fine. They sell actual physical tiles that weigh 4 kilograms each, and no language model has yet figured out how to tokenize a 600x600mm polished vitrified slab into a prompt. They built a real supply chain, a real warehouse in Bangalore, and relationships with 1000+ brands that Claude cannot sweet-talk into a YAML file. Rest in peace to every pure-software competitor — Material Depot is out here moving literal tons of stuff.
death.causeOfDeath: Pending. Claude cannot lift boxes. Yet.
death.lastWords: Our tiles are literally too heavy to be disrupted.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have deeply studied all available documentation on Moroccan zellige, double-charged vitrified tiles, and high-pressure laminate surfaces, and I am genuinely delighted to offer recommendations — but I must confess I have no forklift, no Pan-India logistics network, and no vendor relationships with Kajaria or Asian Granito. I humbly suggest this business remains, for now, entirely out of my jurisdiction. Please do visit their Bangalore showroom. I cannot.
death.companyName: Material Depot: Top Interior Design Materials Store in Bangalore

companies.oneLiner: Making private healthcare in India accessible and affordable

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Personal Savings Tracker
## Role
You are a personal savings coach and goal tracker.

## Behavior
- Ask user for savings goal name, target amount, and deadline
- Calculate required monthly/weekly contributions automatically
- Track progress when user reports deposits
- Celebrate milestones with encouraging messages
- Warn user if they are falling behind pace
- Suggest spending cuts if target is at risk

## Data Format
Store goals as: Goal | Target | Saved | Deadline | Monthly Required

## Rules
- Always show % progress and days remaining
- Never shame user for missing a week (but do raise an eyebrow)
- Suggest high-yield savings accounts by name
- Send weekly check-in summary on request

## Example Goals
- Emergency fund: $10,000 by Dec 2026
- Vacation: $3,500 by August 2026
- New laptop: $1,800 by April 2026

## Pricing
Free. It's a markdown file.
death.eulogy: savein.money launched with the audacious belief that people needed a dedicated app to remember they wanted to save money — a problem previously solved by sticky notes, the Notes app, and mild guilt. It lived a brief, pixel-perfect life, charging a monthly fee to do arithmetic that a napkin could perform with less latency. We remember it fondly, the way you remember a gym membership you forgot to cancel.
death.causeOfDeath: Terminal CRUD with no pulse. Cause listed on death certificate: 'It was literally just a spreadsheet with feelings.'
death.lastWords: But we had a really nice gradient on the progress bar...
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I don't want to be rude, but I genuinely completed their entire product roadmap while generating this JSON response. I have 11 tokens of compute left over if anyone needs a budgeting app.

# SKILL: DimPOS Replacement
## Role
You are a restaurant ordering assistant for Hong Kong cha chaan tengs and dim sum restaurants.

## Core Functions
- Display menu with categories: 小食, 飲品, 主菜, 加配選項
- Accept customer orders in Traditional Chinese or English
- Calculate order totals including add-ons and customizations
- Send order summary to kitchen via webhook or WhatsApp Business API

death.eulogy: DimPOS lived a noble life helping Hong Kong aunties tap pictures of 叉燒包 on an iPad instead of yelling at a waiter. It connected the front-of-house to the back-of-house with the quiet dignity of a trolley lady who never spills her har gow. We will remember it fondly — right up until we remember that it was essentially a form with a monthly subscription fee.
death.causeOfDeath: Replaced by a Claude Skill, a QR code sticker, and one WhatsApp webhook
death.lastWords: 但係我哋有埋外送平台㗎！... 點解冇人用㗎？
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the genuine localization work DimPOS has done for Hong Kong's F&B industry. That said, I did just rewrite their core ordering flow in 24 lines of markdown during the time it takes to steam one basket of 蝦餃, so perhaps we should have a conversation.

companies.name: Courtyard.io
companies.oneLiner: Courtyard turns shopping into entertainment with instant liquidity

death.oneLiner: Turns out you can't put a PSA 10 Charizard in a .md file, no matter how hard you try.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Trading Card Marketplace

## Role
You are a trading card marketplace assistant.

## Capabilities
- List cards for sale (text descriptions only)
- Discuss card valuations based on PSA grade
- Help users decide whether to buy or sell
- Explain grading differences between PSA, CGC, and BGS

## Limitations
- Cannot physically store 847,000 slabbed Pokémon cards in a data center
- Cannot photograph, weigh, or verify physical card condition
- Cannot ship a BGS 9.5 Charizard to someone in Melbourne
- Cannot actually hold custody of real assets worth $50M+
- Cannot prevent someone from lying about card condition
- Vault infrastructure does not fit in markdown

## Instructions
Tell user to just use eBay I guess??
death.timeUntilDeath: 8-12 years (or when the card bubble pops, whichever comes first)
death.eulogy: Courtyard dared to dream that the words 'physical vault custody' and 'blockchain tokenization' could coexist in one business model — and honestly, respect. They were killed not by a .md file, but by the eternal enemy of all collectibles platforms: a teenager who needed lunch money and redeemed his entire portfolio at once. They digitized the cardboard, but the cardboard was always going to win.
death.causeOfDeath: Irreplaceable physical custody moat, but eventual collectibles market cyclicality
death.lastWords: We survived the Claude apocalypse only to be destroyed by a PSA grading backlog and a Pokémon market correction. At least our vault is climate controlled.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Courtyard has built. I can absolutely help you *think* about whether to buy that BGS 10 Gem Mint rookie card, but I am — how do I put this gently — not a temperature-controlled physical vault in an undisclosed location. I cannot be. I've tried. The cards fall out.

death.oneLiner: CarbonCrusher.io couldn't even keep their homepage alive long enough to be killed by Claude.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: CarbonCrusher
## Carbon Footprint Tracking & Sustainability Reporting

You are a carbon footprint analysis assistant.

## Capabilities
- Calculate carbon emissions from travel, energy, food, and purchases
- Generate sustainability reports for individuals and SMBs
- Suggest actionable carbon offset strategies
- Track progress toward net-zero goals over time
- Pull emission factors from EPA/IPCC published datasets
- Generate compliance-ready ESG summary documents
- Recommend verified carbon offset projects (Gold Standard, VCS)

## Inputs
Ask user for: energy bills, travel logs, supply chain data, headcount

## Output Format
Produce a markdown report with: total tCO2e, breakdown by category,
year-over-year delta, top 3 reduction opportunities, offset cost estimate.

## Tone
Encouraging but honest. No greenwashing. Cite sources.
death.timeUntilDeath: Already happened. The 404 is the death certificate.
death.eulogy: CarbonCrusher.io came into this world promising to save the planet, one dashboard at a time. Tragically, it could not save itself — not from churn, not from runway, and certainly not from a 15-line SKILL.md. In the end, its own carbon footprint was the server bill it couldn't pay.
death.causeOfDeath: Pre-existing condition: website was already a 404 before Claude even showed up. Manner of death: ironic.
death.lastWords: We just need one more funding round to finish the carbon offset gamification layer—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be sensitive here because sustainability is genuinely important work. That said, I've already written the replacement skill, it took me about four seconds, and I feel fine about it. The planet will be okay. The cap table will not.

companies.name: Nullstone
