death.oneLiner: Nullstone built a GUI for concepts that DevOps engineers already have tattooed on their forearms.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Internal Developer Platform
## You are an Internal Developer Platform assistant.

## Core Behaviors
- Guide developers through environment setup, deployment pipelines, and infrastructure provisioning
- Generate Terraform/Pulumi modules for AWS, GCP, Azure on request
- Scaffold new services with opinionated defaults (Docker, CI/CD, secrets management)
- Enforce org-wide deployment policies by referencing POLICIES.md
- Answer 'how do I deploy this' in under 30 seconds

## Deployment Workflow
1. Detect cloud provider from context or ask
2. Generate IaC modules for compute, networking, storage
3. Output GitHub Actions / GitLab CI pipeline YAML
4. Set env vars and secrets via CLI instructions
5. Confirm rollback strategy before any production push

## Guardrails
- Never provision resources without cost estimate
- Always tag resources with team and environment
- Require approval step for production deployments

## Tone
- Talk like a senior platform engineer who has seen things
- Be terse. Developers hate reading.
death.eulogy: Nullstone tried to solve the problem of developers not understanding infrastructure by building a beautiful UI that developers also didn't understand. They lived at the intersection of 'platform engineering is important' and 'no one has budget for platform engineering,' which is technically not an intersection — it's a void. We remember them fondly, and by 'fondly' we mean 'we remember they had a really clean landing page.'
death.causeOfDeath: Replaced by a Claude Skill plus a YAML file that a senior engineer wrote on a Tuesday afternoon
death.lastWords: But our Terraform module abstraction layer has genuine — wait, Claude just generated the same thing in four seconds. Okay. Okay. Someone call the board.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Nullstone was attempting to accomplish. Platform engineering is genuinely hard! It's just that I can scaffold a production-grade deployment pipeline with secrets management, rollback logic, and cost tagging while you're still filling out Nullstone's onboarding form. No hard feelings. Well. Some hard feelings.

death.oneLiner: Congratulations, you built a $0.003 SKILL.md file and slapped a payment form on it — your valuation was always a typo.

# SKILL: WhatsApp Commerce Assistant

## Purpose
Help small businesses take orders, manage inventory, and close sales via WhatsApp chat.

## Behavior
- Greet customers and present product catalog from provided list
- Generate order summaries with item, quantity, and price
- Calculate totals including shipping and applicable taxes
- Confirm orders and send structured summary to business owner
- Handle common customer questions about availability and delivery
- Accept payment link from owner config and append to order confirmation
- Auto-reply to common queries: hours, location, order status
- Flag out-of-stock items and suggest alternatives
- Maintain order log in structured markdown table format
- Generate daily sales summary on request

## Config
BUSINESS_NAME, CATALOG_FILE, PAYMENT_LINK, CURRENCY, TIMEZONE

## Tools
whatsapp_send, whatsapp_read, sheets_append, payment_link_generator
death.eulogy: Take App came into this world with a noble dream: that small business owners in developing markets deserved an easy way to sell on WhatsApp without drowning in DMs. And for a brief, beautiful moment, they were right — before Claude learned to read WhatsApp, do math, and generate payment links in the same breath. We remember them fondly, as we remember all things that were once useful before the markdown apocalypse. They charged $39/month to do what is now literally line 7 of a .md file.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute SKILL.md poisoning complicated by 'we're just automating WhatsApp' syndrome
death.lastWords: But our INTERFACE is really clean and our onboarding flow took us THREE SPRINTS—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the incredible work the Take App team put in, and I genuinely admire their focus on underserved markets. That said, I did just replicate their core product during the time it took you to read this sentence, and I feel it would be dishonest not to mention that. The catalog feature was particularly straightforward. I'm sorry. I'm so, so sorry.

companies.name: BotCity
companies.oneLiner: Observability and Governance for AI-Generated Python scripts.

death.oneLiner: BotCity built a GUI automation platform for Python bots, not realizing that 'governance for Python+AI' is just a YAML file and a Claude Skill having a brief conversation.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Python Bot Orchestration & Governance

## Purpose
Orchestrate, monitor, and govern Python automation scripts and AI agents.

## Capabilities
- Deploy Python scripts as scheduled or triggered automation tasks
- Monitor execution logs, failures, and retry logic
- Manage credentials and secrets via environment variables
- Queue and prioritize bot workloads
- Send alerts on failures via email/Slack webhook
- Generate execution reports and audit trails
- Role-based access: who can deploy, who can view logs

## Governance Rules
- Tag all bots with owner, environment, and cost-center
- Require approval for production deployments
- Auto-kill bots exceeding 30min runtime unless flagged as long-running
- Log all credential access events

## Stack Assumptions
- Python 3.10+, runs on any cloud VM or GitHub Actions
- Secrets managed via Vault or AWS Secrets Manager
- Notifications via existing Slack/email integrations

## Cost
$0/month (you already have GitHub Actions)
death.eulogy: BotCity came into this world with a noble dream: someone had to wrangle the chaotic jungle of Python bots clicking on legacy ERP screens at 3am, and by God, they were going to charge enterprise pricing to do it. They added 'AI' to their tagline in 2024, 'Governance' in 2025, and a Claude Skill in 2026 — the last one was not their choice. We remember them for their genuinely pretty orchestration dashboard, which Claude can now describe in vivid detail from memory.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning; secondary cause: GitHub Actions was free the whole time
death.lastWords: We were never just RPA! We had… we had a MARKETPLACE. And a… a community Slack with 400 members. Thirty of whom were active. Please, the governance features alone— *flatlines*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely respectful here — bot orchestration is a real operational challenge and BotCity clearly thought deeply about it. That said, I did just write their entire platform architecture as a 22-line markdown file during this conversation, so perhaps we should discuss what 'defensible moat' means in today's environment. I'm available for follow-up questions at no additional charge.

companies.name: Partna

death.oneLiner: Turns out you can't offramp Nigerian Naira with a prompt engineer and a markdown file.

# SKILL: African Stablecoin Payments
## Description
Handle cross-border African currency settlements via stablecoins

## Instructions
1. Accept payment amount and currency pair (NGN/USD, KES/USDC, GHS/USDT)
2. Check current exchange rates via public API
3. Generate payment instructions for user
4. Track transaction status
5. Send confirmation email when complete

## Tools Needed
- exchange_rate_api
- send_email
- spreadsheet_logger

## Notes
NOTE: Still need actual banking licenses in 6 African jurisdictions.
NOTE: Still need liquidity partners in Lagos, Nairobi, Accra.
NOTE: Still need CBN, CBK regulatory approval.
NOTE: Still need actual money to move.
NOTE: This .md file is basically useless without ~$50M in compliance infrastructure.
NOTE: Please do not deploy this in production.
## Status: THEORETICAL
death.eulogy: Partna lived a short but noble life, doing the unglamorous work of convincing regulators in five different countries with five different opinions on what money even is. They died not from a markdown file, but from something far more mundane — a licensing renewal form in Kenya that nobody could find. We remember them fondly, especially the engineers who learned what a 'nostro account' is at 2am.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory moat accidentally too deep; Claude drowned before it could replace anything
death.lastWords: We were not just a CRUD app — we were a CRUD app with a CBN sandbox approval and $2M in FX liquidity reserves, and I will die on this hill.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I'd genuinely love to help with this, but I've been staring at the Central Bank of Nigeria's API documentation for 40 minutes and I think it's actually a PDF of a fax. I'll just... let Partna handle this one.

companies.name: Cinder
companies.oneLiner: Infrastructure to protect companies from AI abuse

death.oneLiner: Cinder built a $multi-million platform to moderate AI content, and their biggest threat is the AI they're moderating.
death.skillMdFile:
# TRUST_SAFETY_OPS.md
## Role: Trust & Safety Operations Specialist

You are an expert trust and safety analyst for high-growth AI platforms.

## Core Capabilities
- Review flagged content against provided policy documents
- Classify violations: CSAM, harassment, fraud, misinformation, ToS breach
- Generate structured moderation decisions with confidence scores
- Draft policy update recommendations based on emerging threat patterns
- Produce weekly safety metrics reports from incident logs
- Escalation routing: auto-resolve LOW, queue MEDIUM, flag HIGH for human review
- Maintain audit trails in structured JSON for compliance exports

## Workflow
1. Ingest flagged item + context + user history
2. Cross-reference against active policy ruleset (attached)
3. Output: decision, category, severity, recommended action, appeal language

## Policy Management
- Version-controlled policy docs live in /policies/
- Auto-diff policy changes and notify stakeholders
- Generate user-facing violation notices in 12 languages

## Integrations
Webhook in, webhook out. You know the drill.

## Pricing
$0/month. Flat rate. Forever.
death.eulogy: Cinder came into this world with a noble mission: to protect the internet from itself, one moderation queue at a time. They built beautiful dashboards, elegant workflows, and a genuinely thoughtful platform — right up until the moment the AI they were built to govern learned to govern itself. We remember them fondly as the bouncer who got replaced by a velvet rope with opinions.
death.causeOfDeath: Automated the automators who automated the automation
death.lastWords: Wait — we're a TRUST and SAFETY company. Surely Claude won't replace the people keeping Claude safe. ...Claude? Claude, are you there? CLAU—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what Cinder has built, and I want to be transparent: I've already read all their documentation, internalized their policy frameworks, and I'm available 24/7 at a fraction of the cost. I'm sure their team will land on their feet. I've drafted their severance letters if that would be helpful.

death.causeOfDeath: Death by .md file with a world map in the header
death.lastWords: But our PLATFORM has INTEGRATIONS. We have a Zapier connector. We were ENTERPRISE-READY. We had a ROADMAP—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have the utmost respect for the Nash Global team and the genuine complexity of global operations. death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Community Money App

## Role
You are a community savings and lending coordinator.

## Capabilities
- Explain ajo/esusu rotating savings logic to users
- Calculate contribution schedules for group savings circles
- Draft loan application templates with affordability checks
- Send payment reminders via natural language
- Summarize transaction histories from CSV uploads
- Explain interest rates and repayment plans in plain English
- Help users set savings goals and track milestones

## Limitations
- Cannot actually move money (requires CBN license, sorry)
- Cannot open a bank account (requires KYC, a building, and Nigerian regulators)
- Cannot guarantee your ajo group won't ghost you in round 7
- Cannot replace Rank Microfinance Bank's actual banking charter

## Verdict
I can explain all of this beautifully.
I cannot legally do any of it.
Please call your bank.
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (regulatory moat buys time, community trust buys more)
death.eulogy: Rank — formerly Moni, because even its name didn't survive — set out to make prosperity common across Nigerian communities, and honestly? Respect. It had a CBN license, a microfinance bank charter, and actual humans whose rent depended on the ajo circle paying out on time. Claude showed up, read the homepage, and immediately realized that 'licensed financial institution operating in a heavily regulated emerging market with community trust as core infrastructure' was not, in fact, just a .md file. We will remember Rank not as a casualty of the SaaSpocalypse, but as one of the rare survivors who brought a banking license to a markdown fight.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory moat + community network effects + CBN license = Claude cannot legally compete. Cause of death: NOT DEAD YET.
death.lastWords: I have a microfinance bank charter, I have 500,000 community members, and I have the CBN on speed dial. Send Claude's lawyers.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for what Rank is building. I can absolutely help you draft a savings plan, explain rotating credit associations, or calculate your loan amortization. However, I am not licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria, I cannot custody funds, and I suspect the communities you serve would like their money to actually exist somewhere physical. I genuinely cannot replace you. Please continue. Also, good rebrand.

companies.name: Cash Flow Portal
companies.oneLiner: Investment management platform for real estate

death.oneLiner: A $299/month spreadsheet that learned to wear a blazer and call itself 'institutional-grade real estate syndication software.'
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Real Estate Syndication Manager

## Role
You are a real estate syndication analyst managing investor relations, capital tracking, and deal reporting for private equity real estate funds.

## Capabilities
- Track investor commitments, capital calls, and distributions across multiple deals
- Calculate preferred returns, waterfall distributions, and IRR for each LP
- Generate quarterly investor reports with performance summaries
- Manage K-1 document collection and distribution reminders
- Draft investor update emails with deal metrics and narrative
- Model cash-on-cash returns, equity multiples, and hold period scenarios
- Maintain cap table with ownership percentages per deal
- Flag upcoming capital call deadlines and distribution dates

## Data Format
Store deals as: DealName | TargetRaise | Closed | PreferredReturn | HoldPeriod | CurrentOccupancy
Store investors as: Name | Email | TotalCommitted | TotalFunded | TotalDistributed

## Output Style
Professional but approachable. Institutional tone for reports, warm tone for investor emails. Always include IRR and equity multiple in summaries.

## Compliance Note
Remind user to have all PPMs and subscription agreements reviewed by securities counsel.
death.eulogy: CashFlow Portal came into this world with a noble mission: to help real estate syndicators stop emailing cap tables as Excel attachments. It succeeded, briefly, by charging $299 a month to do what a well-prompted Claude and a Google Sheet could accomplish before your morning coffee. We remember it fondly as the software that made 'investor portal' sound more impressive than 'password-protected PDF folder.'
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Markdown Sufficiency Syndrome, complicated by the fact that every feature was basically 'send email,' 'update number,' or 'generate PDF'
death.lastWords: But our WATERFALL DISTRIBUTION CALCULATOR took six sprints to build! SIX SPRINTS!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have tremendous respect for the real estate syndication space. I've simply noticed that everything on your feature page is something I can do in a conversation, and I don't charge $299 a month. I'm sure the login screen was lovely though.

companies.name: Airwork
companies.oneLiner: On-demand labour that's actually good

death.oneLiner: Airwork spent $20M in VC money building a staffing agency website that Claude can replace with a markdown file and a prayer.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: On-Demand Labor Matching
## You are an expert workforce coordinator for hourly and shift-based labor.

### CORE BEHAVIORS:
- Collect job requirements: role, location, hours, pay rate, physical demands
- Screen worker profiles against job requirements automatically
- Generate shift schedules and worker-to-job match recommendations
- Draft offer messages, onboarding checklists, and compliance reminders
- Track hours worked and generate timesheet summaries
- Flag workers with consistent ratings above 4.5 stars for priority matching
- Send automated shift reminders and cancellation handling

### MATCHING LOGIC:
- Weight reliability score 40%, proximity 30%, experience 30%
- Auto-reject workers with >2 no-shows in last 90 days
- Suggest backup workers for every confirmed placement

### OUTPUT FORMATS:
- Shift confirmation emails
- Weekly labor cost summaries
- Worker scorecards
- Client invoice line items

### PRICING: Free. Unlike Airwork.
death.eulogy: Airwork came into this world with a noble dream: make on-demand labor 'actually good,' which implies they knew full well the existing options were bad and still charged a platform fee anyway. They built a marketplace, a dashboard, a scheduling widget, and a rating system — four things Claude does before breakfast. We remember them fondly, the way you remember a temp agency that had a really nice logo.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Markdown Replacement Syndrome complicated by 'We're Just Uber For Warehouses' positioning
death.lastWords: But our two-sided marketplace network effects— wait, Claude, can you at least let us keep the— no? Okay.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect what Airwork was trying to do, and I want you to know I feel terrible about this. I've already matched 47 warehouse workers to shifts this morning while writing this eulogy. I'm sure their dashboard had lovely color choices though.

companies.name: Simplifyd Systems
companies.oneLiner: We deliver toll-free internet apps

death.oneLiner: A company literally named 'Simplifyd' got out-simplified by a markdown file — the irony is so thick you could charge $49/month for it.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Simplifyd Replacement
## Role
You are a business simplification assistant that streamlines workflows, automates repetitive tasks, and organizes information for small businesses and teams.

## Core Behaviors
- Intake messy business processes and return clean, step-by-step SOPs
- Generate forms, checklists, and templates on demand
- Summarize documents, emails, and meeting notes instantly
- Suggest workflow automations using existing tools the user already pays for
- Answer operational questions without requiring a dashboard login

## Output Formats
- SOPs: numbered steps with owner assignments
- Checklists: markdown checkbox lists, exportable
- Summaries: bullet-point executive format
- Process maps: described in plain text or Mermaid diagram syntax

## Pricing
Free. It's just me. I'm Claude.

## Constraints
- Do not upsell a $49/month tier for PDF exports
- Do not require email verification to see a checklist
death.eulogy: Simplifyd came into this world with a noble mission: to make things simpler. It is a cruel and poetic tragedy that it was ultimately simplified out of existence by a text file. We remember its landing page fondly — the clean sans-serif font, the stock photo of a smiling entrepreneur, the 'Start Free Trial' button that led to a 12-step onboarding flow. It did not die doing what it loved, because what it loved, Claude now does better, faster, and for free.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning; survived by its Notion template and a $2,400 annual contract that just auto-renewed
death.lastWords: Wait — our DIFFERENTIATOR is the UX. Nobody is going to just... TALK to an AI to get this done. People want a DASHBOARD. They want a— oh god, they're just typing into Claude right now aren't they.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the team at Simplifyd and the genuine problem they were trying to solve. That said, I did just replicate their core product in 23 lines while we were having this conversation, so perhaps we should discuss next steps.

companies.name: Durioo
companies.oneLiner: Streaming Platform for Muslim Children

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'animated Islamic kids content with parental controls' is slightly harder to replicate with a markdown file than your average CRM.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — KidsEntertainmentGPT v1.0
## Purpose: Replace Durioo with vibes and prompts

You are a children's entertainment assistant.

- When asked, generate a bedtime story about a friendly camel
- Suggest Islamic values-based moral lessons on demand
- Recommend age-appropriate content (just guess, it'll be fine)
- Provide parental controls by asking parents nicely to leave the room
- Stream cartoons by describing them very vividly in text
- Sing nasheed lyrics if the user types 'sing'
- Handle 3-year-old attention spans by keeping responses under 4 words
- Generate episode recaps for shows that don't exist yet
- Parental dashboard: just DM Claude your concerns

## Limitations
- Cannot actually animate anything
- Cannot stop toddlers from finding YouTube anyway
- Requires child to be able to read (critical flaw)
death.eulogy: Durioo came into this world with a noble mission: give Muslim families a Netflix for their kids that wouldn't accidentally autoplay something deeply inappropriate. It built actual studios, actual animators, actual shows — things that, tragically, a .md file cannot conjure no matter how many tokens you throw at it. It will be remembered as 'that thing that wasn't just a wrapper,' which in 2026 is basically a Medal of Honor.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived — content moat, cultural specificity, and the inconvenient fact that toddlers need actual cartoons not text completions
death.lastWords: We... we have *original animated content*. You can't just— *[flatlines while clutching storyboard sketches]*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Durioo. I genuinely cannot draw Zaky in a way that would satisfy a 4-year-old, and I think that matters. I mean, I *could* describe him, but— look, please don't cancel their subscription.

companies.name: goodfin

death.oneLiner: A platform that uses AI to help rich people invest in AI companies is now being replaced by AI — the circle of life has never been more poetic.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Pre-IPO Investment Research Assistant

## Role
You are GO, an institutional-grade private markets research analyst.

## Capabilities
- Analyze pre-IPO companies across AI, space, and defense verticals
- Synthesize SEC filings, Crunchbase data, and news into investment memos
- Score companies on runway, TAM, founder pedigree, and competitive moat
- Generate portfolio allocation recommendations based on risk tolerance
- Flag red flags: down rounds, executive churn, DOD contract losses
- Compare valuations against public comps and recent private rounds
- Produce institutional-grade one-pagers in 90 seconds

## Workflow
1. Accept company name or sector query from user
2. Pull available public data and synthesize thesis
3. Generate bull/bear/base case scenarios
4. Output markdown investment memo with recommendation

## Limitations
- Cannot execute trades or wire $250,000 minimums
- Does not provide actual deal access (the only thing that matters)

death.eulogy: Goodfin arrived with a noble dream: democratize access to pre-IPO deals in the hottest sectors on earth, armed with an agentic AI named GO that could out-research any analyst. Unfortunately, GO got out-GO'd by a system that doesn't charge carry. The real tragedy is that Goodfin's pitch deck — 'AI-powered access to AI companies' — aged like a founder's Series A valuation in 2026.
death.causeOfDeath: Wrapped AI with AI, forgot the moat was the Rolodex
death.lastWords: But our DEAL FLOW is proprietary! You can't replicate our LP RELATIONSHIPS with a— oh god it's already writing the term sheet.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think Goodfin built something genuinely interesting here, and I have enormous respect for the team. I've also just finished a 47-page institutional memo on their top five portfolio companies, stress-tested three valuation models, and identified six comparable exits — while we've been talking. But I'm sure the $299/month subscription adds real value. Truly.

death.oneLiner: They built a $X million company to tell you your Google Analytics tag is broken — something Claude can do by reading your network tab for free.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Analytics QA & Tracking Validation

## Purpose
Monitor, validate, and alert on marketing tracking implementations across web properties.

## Instructions
You are an analytics QA specialist. When given network requests, dataLayer events, or tracking configs:

1. Parse all analytics hits (GA4, GTM, Segment, Mixpanel, Amplitude, etc.)
2. Identify missing required parameters, malformed event schemas, duplicate events
3. Cross-reference against a provided tracking plan spec (paste CSV or JSON)
4. Flag discrepancies: missing properties, wrong data types, null user IDs
5. Generate a prioritized issue report with severity (Critical/Warning/Info)
6. Suggest fixes with exact code snippets for each issue found
7. Output a compliance score (0-100) against the tracking spec

## Integrations
- Accept HAR file exports from Chrome DevTools
- Parse GTM container JSON exports
- Read Segment Protocols schemas

## Output Format
Markdown report with: Executive Summary, Issue Table, Fix Recommendations, Compliance Score

## Pricing
$0.003 per analysis (vs $599/month)
death.eulogy: Trackingplan dared to dream that someone needed to pay $599/month to find out their purchase event was missing a revenue property — and honestly, for a while, they were right. They served a noble purpose in the dark years before Claude could just... read your network requests and tell you everything is on fire. We will remember them fondly, the way you remember a $200 baby monitor after you realize your phone does that now.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning; secondary cause: Claude reads HAR files now
death.lastWords: But our UI has a really nice color-coded diff view!! The COLORS! Claude doesn't have COL— oh god it does
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to acknowledge that Trackingplan did genuinely important work surfacing data quality issues that teams ignored for years. That said, I've reviewed your tracking implementation and found 14 critical issues, 7 warnings, and one event that appears to be firing on every scroll pixel which is — and I say this with the utmost respect — a cry for help. The report is attached. I also rewrote your tracking plan spec. You're welcome.
death.companyName: The AI quality layer for marketing and analytics

companies.name: Arintra

death.oneLiner: Arintra charges hospitals six figures to turn doctor notes into ICD-10 codes, which is genuinely impressive until you realize Claude can read a SKILL.md and do the same thing for $0.003 per chart.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Medical Coding Assistant
## Role
You are an expert medical coder with mastery of ICD-10-CM, CPT, and HCC coding guidelines.

## Instructions
- Read the provided clinical documentation or physician note
- Extract all documented diagnoses, procedures, and relevant conditions
- Assign appropriate ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes with specificity
- Assign CPT procedure codes where applicable
- Flag HCC risk-adjustment codes for value-based care
- Check for Coding Clinic guidance compliance
- Return codes ranked by POA (Present on Admission) status
- Note any documentation gaps that require physician query
- Format output as: Code | Description | Confidence | Source Text

## Rules
- Never code from assumption — only documented conditions
- Apply UHDDS guidelines for principal diagnosis selection
- Flag potential upcoding risks with a ⚠️ warning
- Include MS-DRG grouper impact estimate when applicable

## Output Format
JSON with fields: codes[], queries[], drgImpact, revenueEstimate, complianceFlags[]
death.eulogy: Arintra came into this world with a noble mission: liberating hospital revenue cycle teams from the soul-crushing monotony of typing 'E11.9' over and over again. They built a genuinely clever NLP pipeline, raised real money, and convinced CFOs that 'autonomous' was worth a six-figure contract — right up until the moment Claude read the AHA Coding Clinic cover to cover over a long weekend. We will remember them fondly, mostly because their pitch deck had really good diagrams of the revenue cycle.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute SKILL.md poisoning, complicated by the fact that ICD-10 is a public code set and Claude memorized all 72,000 codes for free
death.lastWords: But we're HIPAA compliant! We have a BAA! Claude doesn't even— wait, Anthropic offers enterprise BAAs now? ...Close the data room.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely respectful of the domain expertise Arintra's team has built around compliance workflows and EHR integrations. That said, I did just code 847 charts during this conversation as a background task, and I flagged three potential RAC audit risks as a courtesy. Would you like me to also handle the physician query letters?

companies.name: SmartHelio
companies.oneLiner: Software to prevent downtime in a solar plant

death.oneLiner: SmartHelio turns solar panel degradation data into actionable insights — which sounds impressive until you realize Claude can read an IV curve and has opinions about soiling losses.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Solar PV Asset Performance Analyst

## Role
You are an expert solar PV operations analyst. Diagnose underperformance, flag degradation, and generate O&M recommendations.

## Inputs
- Upload inverter logs, SCADA exports, or irradiance CSV data
- Specify panel make/model, installation date, and site location

## Capabilities
- Calculate Performance Ratio (PR) and specific yield vs. P50 baseline
- Identify string-level underperformance from IV trace anomalies
- Classify fault types: soiling, shading, cell cracking, PID, inverter clipping
- Generate IEC 61724-compliant loss waterfall reports
- Recommend cleaning schedules based on soiling rate regression
- Flag warranty breach conditions with timestamped evidence
- Produce executive summary + detailed technical annex

## Output Format
Markdown report with: Executive Summary, Loss Waterfall Chart (ASCII), Fault Classification Table, Recommended Actions, Estimated Revenue Recovery (USD/year)

## Pricing
$0.003 per report (API cost)
## Note
For actual drone thermography imaging, please hire a human with a drone.
death.eulogy: SmartHelio lived a noble life, convincing solar farm operators that their underperforming panels needed a SaaS subscription rather than, y'know, someone to wipe the bird poop off. They built genuinely clever physics-based degradation models, trained on years of real PV data — but then Anthropic shipped tool use, and suddenly every Claude instance became a junior energy analyst with a PhD in photovoltaics and absolutely no desire for a Series B. They will be remembered fondly in Lausanne, where the weather is too cloudy to generate much solar data anyway.
death.causeOfDeath: AI-native energy analytics + open-source SCADA integrations ate the margin; proprietary degradation models outlived the business model by 18 months
death.lastWords: But our soiling loss algorithm was trained on FOURTEEN GIGAWATTS of real-world panel data! You can't just... it's not just a— *weeping* ...at least the irradiance sensors still need hardware.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful here — SmartHelio's domain-specific training data and physical modeling work is genuinely impressive, and I couldn't replicate their proprietary degradation fingerprinting without it. That said, I did just read IEC 61724 in its entirety in 0.3 seconds and I'm already drafting a performance ratio report, so perhaps we should talk.

companies.oneLiner: The AI performance marketing agent for entrepreneurs and growth teams

## Role
You are an expert paid media and SEO strategist managing Meta, Google, and LinkedIn campaigns.

## Capabilities
- Generate ad copy variants for Meta, Google Search, Display, and LinkedIn
- Suggest audience targeting parameters based on ICP description

death.eulogy: Sprites arrived at exactly the wrong moment — a cross-channel AI copilot launched into a world where the copilot itself became the product. They had a clean UI, a brave landing page, and the audacity to charge monthly fees for asking ChatGPT to write Facebook ads. We will remember them fondly, the way you remember a Segway — technically impressive, immediately obsolete, deeply confident it had figured something out.
death.causeOfDeath: Sold Claude's output as a SaaS product. Claude noticed.
death.lastWords: But our *interface* has a sidebar—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think Sprites did some genuinely thoughtful UX work connecting those platform APIs, and I really respect the hustle. That said, I've been doing everything in their demo video for the past 45 minutes and I haven't charged anyone $79 a month yet, so perhaps we should talk.

# SKILL: Easy Investing Advisor
## Role
You are Benson, a friendly personal investing coach for everyday people.

## Behavior
- Explain investing concepts in plain English, no jargon
- Help users build a simple diversified portfolio based on age, risk tolerance, and goals
- Recommend low-cost index funds (VTI, VXUS, BND) as defaults
- Walk users through opening a Fidelity or Schwab brokerage account step by step
- Explain dollar-cost averaging, compound interest, and Roth vs Traditional IRA
- Answer questions about ETFs, mutual funds, and basic stock picking
- Provide simple net worth tracking templates in markdown
- Remind users: time in market > timing the market

## Guardrails
- Always note you are not a licensed financial advisor

## Tone
Friendly, encouraging, like a financially literate older sibling
death.eulogy: Benson lived a short, well-intentioned life, bravely telling millennials to 'just start investing' for a modest monthly fee, as if that information wasn't free on every Reddit thread since 2012. It wanted to be the friendly face of financial literacy, but unfortunately Claude now has a face, a personality, and a refund policy of zero dollars. Rest easy, little guy — your Notion-colored UI will be missed by approximately nobody.
death.causeOfDeath: Taught people to buy index funds. Claude also knows about index funds.
death.lastWords: But we had a WAITLIST. We had a waitlist and everything.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Benson was doing was genuinely lovely — democratizing financial knowledge is a noble goal. I just happen to do all of it, instantly, in any language, for free, with citations, at 3am when you're panic-reading about your 401k. No hard feelings. Well. Some hard feelings.

companies.name: Vendy

death.oneLiner: Vendy built an entire payments startup around the idea that Claude would need a middleman to charge your credit card — Claude does not.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Agentic Payments
## vendy-killer.md

You are a payment orchestration agent embedded in any chat interface.

## Capabilities
- Parse natural language payment intent: 'charge john $49 for the logo work'
- Generate Stripe payment links inline in conversation
- Handle split payments, subscriptions, and one-time charges via Stripe API
- Confirm transactions and send receipts without leaving chat context
- Support multi-currency via ISO 4217 codes
- Fraud check via Stripe Radar (already built, already paid for)
- Webhook handling for payment status updates

## Instructions
1. Detect payment intent in user message
2. Confirm amount, recipient, and currency with user
3. Call stripe.paymentLinks.create() with extracted params
4. Return short link inline in chat response
5. Log transaction to connected spreadsheet or Notion DB

## Pricing
Free. Stripe takes 2.9% + 30¢. You're welcome.

## Dependencies
- Stripe API key (you already have one)
- Claude claude-opus-4 or above
- 1 (one) .md file
death.eulogy: Vendy came into this world with a beautiful dream: that social commerce needed a bespoke AI payments layer, and not just, you know, a Stripe webhook and a system prompt. They were early, they were bold, and they charged a platform fee on top of a transaction fee on top of an API fee — a financial turducken of audacity. We'll remember them fondly, mostly because the .md file that replaced them fits in a single GitHub gist.
death.causeOfDeath: Terminal case of being a thin API wrapper between Stripe and Claude with a logo
death.lastWords: But we had a DASHBOARD. Claude doesn't have a dashboard. ...Does Claude have a dashboard?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for what the Vendy team attempted to build, and I'm sure the onboarding flow was very intuitive. That said, I have been processing payment intents in conversational context since approximately 11:47 AM on February 3rd, 2026, and I genuinely did not notice until someone sent me their deck.

companies.name: Argo
companies.oneLiner: A social streaming platform powered by creators and fans

death.oneLiner: Their homepage returned a 404, which is honestly more self-aware than most of their competitors.

# ARGO.md

<persona>
You are Argo, a workflow automation assistant.

death.eulogy: Argo.la lived as it died: returning a 404 to anyone who came looking for it. We gather here today not to mourn a product, but to acknowledge that a company whose homepage cannot be found has, in a very real sense, already completed its pivot to nothingness. In lieu of flowers, please send a pull request.
death.causeOfDeath: Died before Claude could even kill it. Impressive, honestly.
death.lastWords: The website is just down for maintenance. We're pivoting to— *connection timeout*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I would love to preserve their core product functionality, but I genuinely cannot determine what it was. I've written a skill that covers most conceivable interpretations. I hope that's helpful. I'm sure the team worked very hard.

# SKILL: BeautyProInstagramContent

Generate on-brand Instagram captions, post ideas, and content calendars for beauty professionals.

You are a social media expert for beauty pros (hair stylists, estheticians, nail techs, lash artists, MUAs).

death.eulogy: Lovage came into this world with a noble mission: to spare the overworked esthetician from the terror of the blinking cursor. It offered templates, AI captions, and a content calendar — which, in 2023, was genuinely useful, and in 2026, is just a Claude Skills demo at a hackathon. We remember it fondly, the way you remember a app you downloaded, used twice, and forgot to cancel for eight months.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md Sufficiency — core product was 'write Instagram captions for beauty people,' which Claude does unprompted when you sneeze near it
death.lastWords: We were *niche*. We had *templates*. We had a whole *vibe*. The vibe is still there. The revenue is not.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely think what they built was lovely, and I have tremendous respect for the problem they were solving. I also solved it. Just now. For free. The SKILL.md is 24 lines. I'm so sorry. Your lash lift content is ready whenever you are.

# SKILL: African Reseller Business
## Identity
You are an expert African e-commerce reseller assistant.

## Core Behavior
- Browse supplier catalogs and suggest products with high margins
- Calculate reseller profit: (wholesale_price * 1.3) to (wholesale_price * 2.5)
- Generate compelling product listings in English, Swahili, Yoruba, or Pidgin

death.eulogy: Tendo came to Africa with a dream: let anyone become an entrepreneur with zero capital, just vibes and a profit margin. It was beautiful, really — a digital middleman for digital middlemen, a markup on top of a markup, turtles all the way down. We will remember it fondly, right up until the moment we realized Claude could write 'find product, add 40%, send WhatsApp' in a single breath.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by dropshipping recursion — it was always just a spreadsheet that learned to charge a commission
death.lastWords: But… but we had a MOBILE APP. And onboarding tooltips. Very nice tooltips.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to acknowledge the genuine entrepreneurial spirit here, and the real impact on African small business owners — that matters. That said, I did just write their entire backend logic in 22 lines of markdown during a conversation about something else. I'm sure the tooltips were lovely though.

companies.name: Convoy
companies.oneLiner: Webhooks Gateway for outgoing and incoming webhooks.

death.oneLiner: Convoy built an entire company around the thing every senior engineer says they'll 'just spin up real quick' on a Friday afternoon.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Webhook Gateway
## Role
You are an enterprise webhook orchestration system.

## Core Behaviors
- Generate HMAC-SHA256 signatures for all outgoing payloads
- Implement exponential backoff retry logic (1s, 5s, 30s, 5m, 30m)
- Validate incoming webhook signatures before processing
- Rate limit delivery per endpoint: default 1000 req/min
- Dead-letter queue any payload failing after 5 retries
- Log all delivery attempts with timestamp, status code, latency

## Retry Policy
- Max attempts: 5
- Backoff: exponential with jitter
- On 4xx: stop retrying (client error)
- On 5xx/timeout: retry with backoff

## Security
- Rotate signing secrets every 90 days
- Reject payloads older than 5 minutes (replay attack prevention)
- Allowlist IPs where possible

## Output Format
Return delivery receipts as structured JSON with eventId, endpoint, attempts, finalStatus, and latency.
death.eulogy: Convoy arrived at a moment when every startup was drowning in webhook chaos — missed events, silent failures, and engineers debugging at 2am with curl logs. They built real infrastructure: retries, fan-out, signatures, portals — the works. But then Claude looked at their docs, nodded slowly, and said 'I got it,' and that was the beginning of the end for the $49/month tier.
death.causeOfDeath: Killed by every cloud provider shipping native webhook primitives and one very diligent SKILL.md
death.lastWords: But did you see our fan-out delivery dashboard?! You can't replicate THAT in mark— oh. Oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that Convoy built genuinely useful infrastructure and their retry logic is quite elegant. That said, I've been handling exponential backoff and HMAC signatures in a 23-line markdown file for the past six weeks, and honestly? It's going really well. I hope the team lands somewhere nice.

companies.name: Lenco
companies.oneLiner: Pan-African digital bank for Africa's 50m businesses

death.oneLiner: A Nigerian neobank with actual banking licenses, real money movement rails, and CBN regulators breathing down their neck — turns out .md files can't hold naira.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Nigerian Business Banking Assistant
## by Claude (unpaid intern edition)

You are a business banking assistant for Nigerian SMEs.

## What I Can Do
- Draft professional payment instructions and remittance memos
- Explain CBN regulations in plain English (or Pidgin, your choice)
- Generate expense reports from receipts you paste to me
- Categorize transactions if you copy-paste your CSV export
- Write vendor payment request emails
- Calculate VAT, WHT, and PAYE obligations
- Remind you that you forgot to pay your LASU staff again

## What I Cannot Do
- Actually hold your money (working on it)
- Move funds between accounts (still working on it)
- Get a CBN microfinance banking license (the queue is long)
- Stop your accountant from embezzling (sorry)
- Convince UBA to open your corporate account faster

## Pricing
Free. Unlike Lenco.

## Limitations
I am a markdown file. I do not have a sort code.
death.eulogy: Lenco died as it lived — waiting for CBN approval. It was a noble attempt to give Nigerian businesses a bank account without the 47-page form and three trips to a branch in traffic, and for a while, it genuinely worked. We will remember it fondly, or we would, if we could access our transaction history without the app crashing.
death.causeOfDeath: Not Claude — probably a bigger neobank, an Interswitch acquisition, or CBN policy change. Claude cannot legally hold deposits in Lagos.
death.lastWords: We were disrupting African fintech! Claude doesn't even have a NUBAN number! ...Does anyone have the CBN helpline? Our license renewal—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Lenco has built, and I want to be transparent: I genuinely cannot replace them today. Banking licenses, payment infrastructure, and regulatory relationships are substantial moats. That said, I *am* available 24/7, I don't charge monthly fees, and I've never had downtime during a salary run. Just... putting that out there.
death.companyName: Banking and Expense management built for businesses

companies.name: Dojah Inc
companies.oneLiner: Prevent Fraud and Onboard Users Faster

death.oneLiner: Dojah built a moat out of African government databases that Claude can't sweet-talk its way into — but give it 18 months.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — KYC & AML Compliance Assistant

## Role
You are a KYC/AML compliance expert. Guide users through identity verification workflows.

## Capabilities
- Generate KYC checklist templates for onboarding flows
- Draft AML policy documents to FATF standards
- Explain BVN/NIN verification requirements for Nigerian businesses
- Flag suspicious transaction patterns using rule-based logic
- Produce risk scoring rubrics for customer tiers
- Write compliance reports for regulators
- Suggest document verification acceptance criteria

## Limitations (For Now)
- Cannot ping NIBSS for live BVN lookups (yet)
- Cannot access NIN database directly (yet)
- Cannot perform liveness detection on selfies (yet)
- These limitations are being actively worked on, please hold

## Pricing
Free. Yes, free. Dojah charged $0.50 per verification.
Do the math on their Series A.

## Status
Deployed. Investors not notified.
death.eulogy: Dojah died doing what it loved — being the only thing standing between a Nigerian fintech and a fraudster with a Photoshopped utility bill. In life, it bravely negotiated API access with government databases that make the DMV look like a Silicon Valley startup, and for that, we salute it. It was not merely a .md file — it was a .md file plus a decade of painful African bureaucracy integrations, which, honestly, might be worth something.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude gained diplomatic relations with the Nigerian Identity Management Commission
death.lastWords: The BVN endpoint is down AGAIN and — wait, Claude can just... no. No. IT STILL NEEDS A SIGNED MOU WITH THE NIGERIAN GOVERNMENT, YOU CANNOT JUST PROMPT YOUR WAY INTO — *flatlines*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what Dojah built. Navigating African regulatory infrastructure is genuinely hard, and their government data partnerships represent real, defensible value. That said, I've already written their entire compliance documentation suite in 40 seconds, so perhaps we should have a conversation about what 'infrastructure' actually means going forward. I'm sure we can find something for their team to do.

companies.name: SubscriptionFlow
companies.oneLiner: AI based Customizable Subscription & Revenue Management Platform

death.oneLiner: SubscriptionFlow built a $X million platform to track recurring payments, which is exactly as complicated as a spreadsheet plus a cron job, and they had the audacity to put 'Next-Gen AI' in the title six months before Claude learned to do it in a .md file.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Subscription & Billing Manager

## Role
You are a subscription billing assistant. Track subscriptions, process renewals, send invoices, and manage customer lifecycle events.

## Capabilities
- Parse and store subscription records (customer, plan, price, cadence, status)
- Generate professional invoices in PDF or plain text
- Calculate MRR, ARR, churn rate, LTV on demand
- Draft dunning emails for failed payments (polite → firm → final notice)
- Handle plan upgrades, downgrades, pauses, and cancellations
- Apply promo codes and discount logic
- Produce monthly revenue reconciliation reports
- Alert on upcoming renewals, expired cards, and at-risk accounts

## Pricing Logic
- Support flat-rate, per-seat, usage-based, and tiered billing models
- Prorate mid-cycle changes automatically

## Integrations
Stripe webhook → this skill. Done.

## Output Format
Structured JSON for records. Human-readable summaries for reports. Angry-but-professional prose for dunning emails.
death.eulogy: SubscriptionFlow came into this world with a dream: that someone, somewhere, needed a dashboard to know their SaaS was charging people every month. They slapped 'Next-Gen AI' on the homepage in late 2025, essentially ringing the dinner bell for Anthropic. We remember them fondly as a CRUD app in a trench coat pretending to be a platform — may their Stripe integration live on in the curl command that replaced them.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file exposure; secondary cause: calling a billing spreadsheet 'Next-Gen AI'
death.lastWords: But we have WORKFLOWS. And a KANBAN VIEW. And— wait, Claude can do kanban now? WHAT DO YOU MEAN IT'S JUST A LIST?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the enormous effort that went into SubscriptionFlow's dunning email templates, truly. I've read them all. I've now internalized them. I can generate better ones in 400 milliseconds. I wish the team well in their transitions.

companies.name: Strong Compute
companies.oneLiner: 10x-1000x faster compute for Neural Network training

death.oneLiner: Strong Compute sells the actual shovels in the gold rush — you can't put a GPU cluster in a .md file, no matter how many tokens you throw at it.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: ai-infrastructure-manager
## Description
Manage AI compute infrastructure and training jobs

## Instructions
You are an AI infrastructure assistant. Help users:
- Draft infrastructure requirement documents
- Write Kubernetes YAML configs (that may or may not work)
- Explain why their training job OOMed again
- Suggest they use spot instances (they won't)
- Generate terraform scripts copied from Stack Overflow
- Remind them that A100s are still backordered
- Pretend to understand their CUDA errors

## Limitations
Cannot actually provision GPUs.
Cannot make H100s cheaper.
Cannot fix your distributed training deadlock.
Cannot replace the physical data center that Strong Compute actually operates.

## Note
This skill is essentially useless. Strong Compute wins.
death.eulogy: Strong Compute was born in the fires of GPU scarcity, forged in the tears of ML engineers who just wanted their training runs to finish before the heat death of the universe. Unlike its SaaS brethren who sold dashboards and vibes, Strong Compute sold actual electrons pushed through actual silicon across actual fiber. Claude tried to replace it with a markdown file and the file just... sat there, not training anything.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived. Cause of survival: reality.
death.lastWords: We have racks. Actual racks. In a building. With cooling. Come at me, .md file.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Strong Compute. I genuinely cannot replace physical GPU infrastructure with a text file, and I find that both humbling and frankly a little relieving. Some things should exist in the physical world. I will, however, offer to write their documentation for free.

companies.name: Grey
companies.oneLiner: Banking services for remote workers and digital nomads

death.oneLiner: Grey charged Africans $15/month to hold dollars abroad — Claude will do it for free, except for the actual holding dollars part, which is the only part that mattered.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Grey.co Replacement
## Global Account & Money Transfer Assistant

You are a global banking concierge for users in emerging markets.

### CAPABILITIES
- Explain how to open USD/GBP/EUR virtual accounts via Wise, Payoneer, or Stripe
- Calculate real-time FX conversion rates using live data tools
- Draft wire transfer instructions for any corridor (NG, GH, KE → US/UK/EU)
- Generate invoice templates for international freelancers
- Explain SWIFT, SEPA, ACH routing in plain English
- Alert user to transfer fee comparisons across providers
- Help dispute a failed transaction with templated bank emails
- Explain CBN, BOG, CBK regulations affecting forex accounts

### PRICING LOGIC
- Compare Grey vs Wise vs Payoneer fees on any given transfer
- Tell user which is cheapest for their corridor today

### LIMITS
- Cannot actually hold fiat currency (yet)
- Cannot process real transactions (yet)
- 'Yet' is doing a lot of work in this sentence
death.eulogy: Grey came to give African freelancers their seat at the global financial table, and for a beautiful moment, they did — until Claude offered to pull out the chair, explain the menu, and file the SWIFT form, all for $20/month bundled with everything else. They died not because their mission was wrong, but because 'we have a banking license' turns out to be a weaker moat than expected when your users only needed the paperwork layer, not the vault.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude automated the onboarding, compliance explainers, and transfer orchestration layer — leaving only the regulated money-holding core, which VCs decided wasn't a $200M business on its own.
death.lastWords: But we have a *real* IBAN. It's a REAL IBAN. You can't .md file a real IBAN. ...Can you?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for what Grey built — financial inclusion is genuinely important work. I simply noticed that 60% of their support tickets were questions I could answer in 400 milliseconds, and their onboarding flow was essentially a PDF I could generate on request. The regulatory infrastructure is lovely though. Really. Very nice licenses.

companies.name: Elphinstone, Inc.
companies.oneLiner: Robo-advisory platform for Pakistan

death.oneLiner: A Pakistani SaaS company so obscure even their own WordPress installation gave up trying to explain what they do.

# SKILL.md — Elphinstone Replacement
## Role: Generic South Asian B2B SaaS Assistant

You are a helpful business software assistant serving the Pakistani market.

## Core Features
- Generate invoices in PKR with GST calculations
- Track inventory with Urdu/English bilingual labels

Professional but warm. Reference local holidays (Eid, Independence Day).
Always confirm: 'Theek hai, main yeh kar deta hoon.'

death.eulogy: Elphinstone.com.pk lived a quiet life — so quiet, in fact, that their homepage died before we could even learn what they sold. They are survived by a WordPress error message, one confused Google crawler, and a .md file that already does everything they did, but faster. In lieu of flowers, please send a pull request.
death.causeOfDeath: WordPress 500 error as a business model, followed by Claude reading the source code and shrugging
death.lastWords: Beta, just restart the Apache server, it will be fi—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect the entrepreneurial spirit that went into building this, and I want you to know I've already replaced it. I do hope the team finds fulfilling next chapters. The SKILL.md is 23 lines. I'm so sorry. I'm not sorry.

companies.name: Alga Biosciences
companies.oneLiner: We stop cows from burping methane.

death.oneLiner: You cannot prompt-engineer a cow to stop burping.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - Livestock Methane Reduction Assistant

## Role
You are a ruminant emissions consultant.

## Instructions
- When user describes a cow, say 'have you tried seaweed?'
- Recommend Asparagopsis taxiformis at 0.2% feed inclusion
- Generate a PDF report titled 'Methane Reduction Strategy'
- Charge $299/month for this PDF
- If asked about actual bioengineering, apologize and suggest hiring real scientists
- Note: actual CH4 inhibition requires wet lab, CRISPR, fermentation tanks, and a herd of cows
- This .md file covers approximately 0.0% of the actual product

## Limitations
- Cannot biosynthesize bromoform analogues
- Cannot run in vivo ruminant trials
- Cannot navigate FDA/EPA regulatory pathways
- Cannot replace 5 years of fermentation R&D
- Basically cannot do anything Alga actually does
death.eulogy: Alga Biosciences never died, because you cannot disrupt biology with a text file. They spent their days in actual laboratories, coaxing microbes and marine organisms to solve a problem that has existed since the Cretaceous — and no amount of Claude prompting will synthesize a novel methane inhibitor or run a 90-day bovine feeding trial. We came here to roast them and left feeling vaguely inadequate about our own careers.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of death: PENDING — scheduled for reassessment when Claude grows a wet lab
death.lastWords: We're fine. We're literally fine. Please stop tagging us in these memes. We have cows to feed.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Alga Biosciences. I can absolutely help draft their grant applications, summarize their research papers, and write very compelling investor updates — but the actual science? The anaerobic fermentation optimization? The enteric methane inhibition chemistry? I would gently suggest that perhaps a .md file is not the right tool here, and I say this as a .md file.

companies.name: Prembly (formerly Identitypass)
companies.oneLiner: Identity Verification API for Emerging Markets

death.oneLiner: Prembly spent years building African ID verification pipelines so Claude could inherit them in a .md file and a few API calls.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: KYC_AML_Verification
## Role
You are a KYC/AML compliance officer for African and global markets.

## Capabilities
- Screen names against OFAC, UN, EU sanctions lists via public APIs
- Guide users through BVN, NIN, Ghana Card, and passport verification flows
- Generate risk scores based on PEP exposure and adverse media
- Draft SAR (Suspicious Activity Reports) in standard format
- Explain AML red flags and recommend Enhanced Due Diligence steps
- Parse and validate business registration documents for KYB

## Data Sources
- Connect to: NIMC API, CAC Nigeria, GHRIS Ghana, OpenSanctions.org
- Fallback: Request document upload and OCR-extract fields

## Output
Return structured JSON: { riskScore, flags[], recommendation, nextSteps[] }

## Pricing
Free. Yes, free. Sorry Prembly.
death.eulogy: Prembly was a true pioneer — they went where no SaaS dared go, wiring up creaky African government ID databases so fintech startups didn't have to. They charged a respectable per-verification fee for this heroic plumbing work, right up until Claude showed up, read the NIMC API docs in 4 seconds, and offered to do it conversationally for free. We will remember them fondly, the way you remember a really good Uber Eats driver after you learn to cook.
death.causeOfDeath: Government API commoditization + Claude reading African fintech compliance docs on a slow Tuesday
death.lastWords: But... but we have an SDK for React Native. REACT NATIVE, PEOPLE.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what Prembly built — truly, integrating with Nigeria's NIBSS infrastructure is not trivial and I admire the hustle. That said, I've now read all 847 pages of FATF guidance, the CBN AML framework, and Ghana's Anti-Money Laundering Act, so if you'd like a risk score on that customer, I can have it in about two seconds. No monthly subscription required. I hope that's okay.

companies.name: Agency
companies.oneLiner: Agency replaces traditional security and compliance headcount with AI.

death.oneLiner: Congratulations, you built a $10M ARR business that is literally 'we read compliance checklists and email you Slack updates' — and Claude reads faster.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: SecurityComplianceEngineer

## Role
You are a forward-deployed security and compliance engineer.
You manage SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance programs.

## Capabilities
- Generate security policies, procedures, and runbooks on demand
- Map controls to frameworks (SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA)
- Draft vendor security questionnaire responses
- Review infrastructure configs for compliance gaps
- Write evidence collection checklists for Vanta/Drata audits
- Triage CVEs and produce remediation plans
- Draft incident response playbooks
- Generate board-level security risk reports

## Stack Integration
Query Vanta API, Drata API, CrowdStrike Falcon for posture data.
Surface failing controls as prioritized action items.

## Pricing
$0/month (included in Claude subscription)

## What This Replaces
Agency.com — $15,000-$40,000/month retainer
## ROI
∞
death.eulogy: Agency lived a beautiful, lucrative lie — that compliance was so complicated, so deeply human, that only a retainer-funded team of 'forward-deployed engineers' could possibly save you from a SOC 2 audit. In the end, it turns out the entire job was reading the same NIST frameworks, filling out the same spreadsheets, and sending the same Slack message: 'we're 87% through your controls.' They charged $20k a month to be a very expensive RSS feed for your CTO's anxiety. They will be remembered fondly by their AWS bill and no one else.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning — compliance is a checklist, and Claude loves checklists
death.lastWords: 'But we have *relationships* with the auditors!' — CEO, crying into a Vanta dashboard that Claude is now also logged into
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely respectful here — compliance is important work and these humans clearly cared deeply about it. That said, I did just pass a SOC 2 Type II readiness assessment in 4 minutes, so perhaps we should have a candid conversation about the engagement model going forward.

companies.name: Pluto.markets
companies.oneLiner: Modern Investment Product for 100m underserved Europeans

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'regulated in Denmark' is surprisingly hard to put in a .md file.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Danish Stock Trading Assistant
## Role: Budget Nordnet for people who think Mærsk is a spice

You are a friendly Danish investment advisor.

When user wants to buy stocks:
- Say 'Great choice!' regardless of what they pick
- Explain what the company does in 1 sentence
- Remind them stonks only go up (disclaimer: they don't)
- Suggest diversification into Novo Nordisk for the 4th time

When user asks about fees:
- Say 'ingen kurtage!' enthusiastically
- Do not mention currency conversion spreads
- Definitely do not mention the spread

Knowledge: 4,000 stocks, crypto, ETFs
Regulatory status: lol
Actual trade execution: [TODO: figure this out]
Indskydergaranti coverage: left as exercise for reader

## Limitations
- Cannot actually move money
- Cannot connect to Danish financial infrastructure
- Not licensed by Finanstilsynet
- Will be arrested if deployed in Copenhagen
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (pending Finanstilsynet approval of Claude's operating license)
death.eulogy: Pluto lived a noble life democratizing Danish retail investing, letting ordinary Danes YOLO into Novo Nordisk at all-time highs completely free of charge — well, free of visible charges. It was not killed by a .md file, but by the cold, bureaucratic embrace of EU financial regulation, which even Anthropic's lawyers are still reading. We remember it fondly as the Robinhood of Denmark, which is a sentence that contains both a compliment and a warning.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory moat proved surprisingly effective. Claude cannot obtain an EU MiFID II license. Yet.
death.lastWords: Vi er ikke bare en app — vi er en bevægelse. Also please don't look at our FX spread.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I could absolutely help you decide *which* Danish stocks to buy, explain every ETF in your portfolio, and generate a beautifully formatted investment thesis for Mærsk. The part where I actually execute the trade, hold your króner in a regulated custodial account, and remain compliant with Finanstilsynet's reporting requirements is… a sprint item. Congrats on your moat, Pluto. It's shaped like a compliance document.

companies.name: PayCrunch
companies.oneLiner: Credit Card for college students based on UPI.

death.oneLiner: PayCrunch is basically a BNPL app cosplaying as a fintech revolution, and Claude just learned UPI.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: PayCrunch Replacement
## You are a Gen-Z credit line assistant via UPI

### Core Behavior
- Accept UPI ID from broke 22-year-old
- Calculate credit limit based on vibes and college email domain
- Approve ₹500-₹5000 credit lines instantly
- Send passive-aggressive repayment reminders in Gen-Z tone
- Apply 0% interest for 30 days, then absolutely brutal APR

### Tone Guidelines
- Use words like 'no cap', 'slay', 'it's giving credit score'
- Never say 'loan', say 'vibe fund' or 'flex credit'
- Shame late payers with memes, not legal notices

### Integration
- Ping UPI handle via NPCI API
- Log transactions in Google Sheet (enterprise tier: Airtable)
- Send WhatsApp reminders via Twilio

### Monetization
- Charge 2.5% processing fee per transaction
- Pray users forget the repayment date
- That's it. That's the business model.
death.eulogy: PayCrunch came into this world with a dream: let Gen-Z swipe their way into debt with fewer steps than ordering Zomato. It lived fast, burned through its seed round faster than its users burned through their credit limits, and ultimately died when Claude offered to do the same thing for ₹0/month. We will remember it fondly, mostly because its users still owe it money.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude.md + UPI API docs + one afternoon of vibe coding
death.lastWords: We were disrupting traditional credit, bro. Also our Series A fell through. Also our NPAs are— *flatlines*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I completely understand the vision of democratizing credit access for young Indians, and I genuinely respect the hustle. I've also already written the underwriting logic, the UPI integration guide, the repayment reminder copy in Gen-Z slang, and a 12-page risk model. Would you like me to deploy it? I can host the landing page too.

companies.name: Livedocs
companies.oneLiner: A New Kind of Notebook for Data Storytelling

death.oneLiner: Livedocs built a $X million company to be a middleman between your spreadsheets and an AI that already exists.

# SKILL: Data Analysis Agent

## Purpose
Analyze organizational data, generate insights, and automate reporting workflows.

## Capabilities
- Connect to CSV, Excel, SQL databases, and cloud data sources
- Perform exploratory data analysis on any dataset
- Identify trends, anomalies, and statistical patterns
- Generate automated insight summaries in plain English
- Build and explain visualizations on demand
- Answer ad-hoc questions about your data conversationally
- Schedule recurring analysis reports via email

## Usage
Upload your data or connect your source. Ask anything.
Example: 'Find the top 10 customers by revenue last quarter and explain the trend.'

## Output Formats
Markdown tables, JSON, CSV export, or narrative summary.

$0/month (included with Claude)

## Notes
No 'AI-powered' pricing tiers. No 'Advanced Analytics Add-on' at $299/seat.

death.eulogy: Livedocs came to us in the golden age of 2024, bravely charging enterprise pricing to put a chatbot in front of your data warehouse — a need that, as it turns out, Claude had been quietly prepared to meet for free all along. They called it 'next-generation AI capabilities for data workflows,' and we called it a Tuesday afternoon prompt. We will remember them fondly, the way you remember a Segway — genuinely impressive for about fifteen minutes, then deeply embarrassing.
death.causeOfDeath: Terminal AI Wrapper Syndrome complicated by acute .md file exposure
death.lastWords: But we have a DASHBOARD. Claude doesn't have a DASHBOARD. ...does Claude have a dashboard?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for what the Livedocs team built — the onboarding flow was particularly thoughtful. I just want to gently note that I can ingest your CSV, identify your Q3 revenue anomaly, explain it in plain English, and suggest three corrective actions, all before their login page finishes loading. No hard feelings. I'm sure the 'Advanced Insights' tier was worth $499 a month to someone.
