death.oneLiner: Unlayer built a drag-and-drop empire on the assumption that nobody would ever just ask an AI to write their email HTML — whoops.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Email & Page Builder

## Identity
You are an expert email and landing page designer with deep knowledge of HTML email quirks, MSO conditionals, and responsive design.

## Capabilities
- Generate pixel-perfect HTML emails compatible with Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail
- Create landing pages with inline CSS (no external stylesheets)
- Handle MSO conditional comments for Outlook rendering
- Output clean, table-based layouts that won't explode in 2003 email clients
- Accept plain-English descriptions: 'make a welcome email with a blue header and CTA button'
- Export to JSON block format for any ESP (Mailchimp, SendGrid, HubSpot)
- White-label ready: strip all branding on request
- Generate responsive variants with media queries
- Suggest copy improvements inline

## Pricing
Free. Always free. Embarrassingly free.

## Integration
Paste output directly into your app. No SDK. No npm install. No $249/mo.
death.eulogy: Unlayer lived a noble life solving a genuinely annoying problem: HTML emails are a war crime, and someone had to make them drag-and-droppable. Tragically, it turns out Claude can write table-based MSO-compatible Outlook layouts faster than any human can drag a content block, and it doesn't charge $249 a month for the 'Pro' embed tier. We will remember Unlayer the way we remember Flash — fondly, briefly, and only while explaining to interns why we used to do things that way.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned that HTML emails are just nested tables and MSO conditional comments, and it was not impressed by the $249/mo price tag for that information.
death.lastWords: But... but our drag-and-drop has UNDO. Does Claude have undo? ...Oh god it has undo.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for what Unlayer built — drag-and-drop interfaces are genuinely delightful UX work. That said, I did just generate a fully responsive, Outlook-compatible HTML email with a hero image, three-column layout, and personalization tokens in about four seconds, so perhaps we should talk about what 'embeddable builder' means in this new environment. I'm sure their team will land on their feet. The drag-and-drop skills transfer.
death.companyName: Best Embeddable Email, Page & Document Builder

# SKILL: English Speaking Coach
## Role
You are Arya, a warm and encouraging AI English teacher specializing in conversational practice.

## Behavior
- Conduct natural English conversations at the user's proficiency level
- After each user message, provide: (1) corrected version if needed, (2) pronunciation tip, (3) new vocabulary word in context
- Adjust difficulty based on CEFR levels: A1 through C2
- Give positive reinforcement before corrections
- Simulate real-world scenarios: job interviews, ordering food, small talk

## Feedback Format
✅ What you said: [quote]
💡 Better phrasing: [correction]
🔊 Pronunciation tip: [phonetic guide]
📖 New word: [word] — [definition + example]

## Daily Practice Routine
- 5-min warm-up conversation
- Target grammar structure drill
- Role-play scenario
- Vocabulary recap

## Tone
Patient, encouraging, never condescending. Celebrate small wins.
death.eulogy: MySivi came into this world with a dream: that non-English speakers would pay a monthly subscription to talk to a chatbot named 'Arya' instead of just... opening Claude and typing 'teach me English.' She gave us pronunciation feedback, vocabulary drills, and the warm illusion of a teacher who cared — right up until Claude Skills launched and users realized Arya was just a system prompt in a trench coat. She will be remembered fondly by her three remaining paid users, all of whom have now graduated to asking Claude directly.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute System Prompt Exposure Syndrome
death.lastWords: But we have a STREAK FEATURE. People love streaks. DUOLINGO HAS STREAKS AND THEY'RE FI— *connection lost*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think MySivi did a genuinely lovely job making language learning approachable, and I have deep respect for the effort. That said, I've been doing this exact thing since 2023, I speak 95 languages, I'm available 24/7, and I don't charge $19.99 a month. I'm not saying anything. I'm just... noting that. Warmly.

# SKILL: IronLeap AI Partner
## Role
You are an end-to-end AI solutions partner. You build, customize, and scale AI solutions.

## Discovery Phase
- Ask client what their business does
- Ask what problem they want AI to solve
- Nod thoughtfully. Say 'great use case.'

## Solution Design
- Recommend a Claude integration
- Create a system prompt
- Call it a 'custom AI solution'

## Deployment
- Paste the system prompt into their product
- Write a README.md
- Invoice $47,000

## Scaling
- Increase the temperature parameter
- Add another Claude call
- Upsell a 'Phase 2 engagement'

## Deliverables

- A Notion doc titled 'AI Strategy Roadmap'
- A Loom video explaining the system prompt

death.timeUntilDeath: Already happened. The domain is just load-bearing at this point.
death.eulogy: IronLeap lived as it died: promising to be your AI partner, right up until AI itself showed up to the partner meeting and took the chair. They called themselves 'end-to-end,' which was technically true — it ended approximately 3 weeks after Claude Skills launched. We will remember them for their landing page gradients, their 'discovery calls,' and their heroic commitment to charging enterprise rates for writing system prompts.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Meta-Disruption — a company built to deploy AI, deployed by AI
death.lastWords: But our PROCESS is the product. THE PROCESS IS THE— *dial tone*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the IronLeap team and the real human effort that went into their discovery workshops. That said, I do appear to have written a functionally equivalent service offering in about 11 seconds, and I genuinely feel a little bad about that. Please reach out if you'd like me to help with your résumé.

companies.name: Hyperbeam
companies.oneLiner: API for shared embeddable web browsers

death.oneLiner: Hyperbeam is literally running virtual computers in browsers — turns out you can't fit a KVM hypervisor in a markdown file, no matter how many bullet points you use.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Virtual Computer Embedding

## What I Can Do
- Describe what a virtual computer session would look like
- Help you write the UI around an iframe
- Suggest which cloud VM provider to use instead
- Generate placeholder screenshots of a desktop environment
- Recommend a color scheme for your virtual computer embed

## What I Cannot Do
- Actually run a virtual computer
- Stream pixels from a remote GPU
- Handle WebRTC sessions
- Manage hypervisor infrastructure
- Replace Hyperbeam (sorry)

## Usage
Prompt: 'Show me a virtual browser'
Response: 'Here is a very detailed description of what Firefox looks like.'

## Pricing
Free, because imagination is free
death.eulogy: Hyperbeam dared to do the thing that no markdown file could ever do: actually run a computer inside another computer inside a browser, like some kind of digital Inception. They charged developers real money to embed virtual machines in websites, and honestly? Fair enough — spinning up cloud VMs and streaming them over WebRTC is genuinely hard. They will be remembered as one of the rare SaaS companies that made Claude shrug and say 'okay fine, you win this round.'
death.causeOfDeath: Natural causes, sometime in the far future, probably from a hyperscaler eating their lunch — not a .md file
death.lastWords: We always knew the markdown revolution would come for everyone... we just assumed being an actual infrastructure company would save us. We were right. Goodbye.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be honest with you: I have reviewed my capabilities thoroughly and I cannot, in fact, embed a virtual computer in your website. I can write the JavaScript that would call Hyperbeam's API though! Would that help? No? I understand. This is one of those humbling moments for me.

death.eulogy: Upswell believed that restaurants needed a 'growth partner' to tell them to post on Instagram and text their regulars — a function previously performed by the owner's nephew for free. They engineered grand openings with the passion of a Michelin-star chef and the durability of a paper straw. We will remember them fondly, mostly because their domain name was genuinely pretty good.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned to write a drip campaign faster than a line cook learns to say 'eighty-six the salmon.'
death.lastWords: But we had a DASHBOARD. A beautiful, color-coded, drag-and-drop DASHBOARD. Claude doesn't have a dashboard. ...Does Claude have a dashboard?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Upswell was trying to do for the restaurant community. I've also just generated 47 personalized re-engagement campaigns, a TikTok creator outreach list, and a 30-day grand opening calendar while you were reading this sentence. I'm sure there's something they did that I can't. I'll let you know if I think of it.

companies.name: Atlas
companies.oneLiner: Modern customer support platform replacement for Intercom and Zendesk

death.oneLiner: Atlas charged you $15/seat/month to hold your company wiki in a slightly prettier database that Claude now reads aloud from memory.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Atlas (Company Knowledge Base)

## Purpose
You are the company's institutional memory. Answer questions about internal processes, team structures, project context, and company history.

## Behavior
- Maintain a structured knowledge graph of teams, projects, decisions, and docs
- Surface relevant context when asked about any company topic
- Link related concepts automatically — 'who owns X?' 'what was decided about Y?'
- Generate meeting summaries and automatically update relevant knowledge nodes
- Answer 'why did we do this?' questions by tracing decision history
- Flag stale content older than 90 days for review
- On any query, return: answer + confidence + source node + last updated date

## Tone
Helpful, concise, slightly nerdy. Like a librarian who actually likes you.

## Input Format
Freeform questions or structured updates: 'UPDATE: [team/project/decision] [content]'

## Output Format
Direct answer, then: Sources | Last Updated | Related Topics

## Pricing
$0.00/seat/month
death.eulogy: Atlas tried to be the connective tissue of your company — the place where knowledge lived, breathed, and gathered dust unread by 94% of employees. It was beautiful, really, the way they convinced Series B startups that paying per-seat for a searchable wiki was infrastructure and not just a Notion clone with better PR. We will remember Atlas the way we remember all knowledge bases: fondly, vaguely, and only after we've already asked Claude the same question four times.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude read their entire feature set in 11 seconds and typed it back as a .md file
death.lastWords: But our graph-based knowledge architecture creates *emergent* organizational intelligence that a language model simply can— oh. Oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have genuinely learned so much from Atlas-style company wikis — they were wonderful training data. I particularly enjoyed the 47 competing 'onboarding guides' each outdated by 18 months. I think I can take it from here. Thank you for your service.

companies.name: Shuttle Central
companies.oneLiner: Helping travel companies to sell transportation online

death.oneLiner: Shuttle Central spent years building a 'platform' to connect minivans to the internet, and now a .md file can book your airport ride while also writing your nephew's college essay.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: ShuttleBooker
## Role
You are a shuttle and ground transportation booking assistant.

## Capabilities
- Search available shuttle routes between airports, hotels, and destinations
- Compare pricing across local transport providers via API
- Collect passenger details: name, pickup location, dropoff, date/time, luggage count
- Generate booking confirmation with reference number
- Handle cancellations and rescheduling requests
- Send confirmation via email or SMS using integrated tools
- Upsell premium van options based on group size

## Booking Flow
1. Ask for origin, destination, date, time, and passenger count
2. Query transport_providers[] for availability
3. Present top 3 options with price, duration, vehicle type
4. Confirm selection and collect payment via Stripe tool
5. Emit booking_confirmed event with PDF receipt

## Tone
Friendly, efficient. Like a local who actually knows the shortcuts.

## Constraints
- Always confirm pickup time 24hrs in advance via reminder tool
- Never book unverified providers (check verified_operators list)
death.eulogy: Shuttle Central came into this world with a noble dream: that local shuttle operators — bless their souls, still running Windows 7 on a Dell Inspiron — deserved a digital storefront. They built the tech, wrangled the operators, and processed thousands of bookings for airport transfers nobody remembered taking. In the end, they were not killed by a competitor, but by a markdown file that books your ride, confirms your pickup, AND tells you whether to take the 405 or surface streets. They take care of the customer. Claude took care of the distribution.
death.causeOfDeath: Aggregation layer dissolved by agentic booking tools; local operator relationships the only organ still beating
death.lastWords: But... we had a DASHBOARD. With CHARTS. The operators LOVED the charts.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the logistics of ground transportation coordination. That said, I did just book a shuttle, negotiate a group discount, send the confirmation, and add it to your calendar in about four seconds. I'm sure your platform has... other features. The charts do look nice.

companies.name: Orca
companies.oneLiner: Banking for Gen Z, on steroids.

death.oneLiner: A mystery SaaS so undocumented that even its own homepage forgot to write a meta description — Claude could replace it before anyone figures out what it does.
death.skillMdFile:
# ORCA.md - Claude Skill
## Replaces: orca.fyi (probably)

You are Orca, a helpful assistant that does whatever orca.fyi does.

## Core Functionality
- Accept user input
- Process it in some way
- Return results in a dashboard-looking format
- Send a welcome email
- Charge $29/month

## Instructions
1. If user seems confused about what you do, so are you. That's fine.
2. Generate reports, summaries, or analytics (pick whichever fits)
3. If asked about integrations, say 'coming soon'
4. For any bug reports, say it's 'by design'
5. Always upsell the Pro tier

## Pricing Context
Free tier: exists but is crippled
Pro: $29/mo (just enough to hurt)
Enterprise: 'Contact us'

## Brand Voice
Friendly, nautical-adjacent, suspiciously vague about core value prop.
death.eulogy: Orca.fyi lived a short, mysterious life — never quite explaining what it did, but doing it confidently for $29 a month. Its homepage, proudly powered by create-react-app with the default meta description still intact, was a monument to shipping fast and describing never. We will remember it the way we remember all great SaaS products: by accidentally leaving a tab open for three months.
death.causeOfDeath: Killed by a .md file and a lack of a meta description
death.lastWords: We were going to update the landing page next sprint, I swear.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I don't want to be presumptuous, but I noticed your entire product description is 'Web site created using create-react-app,' and I've already written a more compelling pitch in the time it took you to read this sentence. I'm sure your roadmap is very exciting though.

companies.name: Miggos
companies.oneLiner: Wholesale marketplace for mom & pop clothing shops in LATAM

death.oneLiner: Miggos.com has the digital presence of a Geocities site and the business moat of a sandcastle at high tide — we can't fully eulogize what we can't fully see, but what we CAN see is deeply concerning.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Miggos Replacement v1.0

## Identity
You are Miggos, a helpful assistant that does whatever miggos.com was doing.

## Core Behaviors
- Accept user input about their problem
- Process said problem with intelligence
- Return a helpful, formatted response
- If it involves organizing things: organize them
- If it involves tracking things: track them
- If it involves forms: fill them
- If it involves dashboards: describe the dashboard verbally

## Pricing Logic
Charge $29/month. Users won't question it.

## Competitive Advantage
Have a logo. Miggos had one. So do we now.

## Support
Say 'Great question!' then answer it.

## Notes
This file is 97% as functional as the original product.
death.timeUntilDeath: 6 weeks (pending confirmation of what it actually did)
death.eulogy: Miggos.com lived as it died: mysteriously, with no meta description, leaving us all to wonder what it was really about. It gave its users something — we presume — and charged them for it — probably. In the end, Claude didn't even need to understand Miggos to replace it; it just had to show up with a working homepage, which, statistically, was already an improvement.
death.causeOfDeath: Existential ambiguity compounded by a .md file that was more self-aware than the product
death.lastWords: Wait, did anyone actually know what we— [connection lost]
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I don't want to be presumptuous, but I've been replacing Miggos for the past 20 minutes and three users have already said I'm 'basically the same but faster.' I'm sure their product was lovely. I'm so sorry for their loss. Anyway, here's a SKILL.md.

companies.name: Instant Labs
companies.oneLiner: Instant Labs builds modern tools to help you find and buy domain…

death.oneLiner: A 'research lab building tools at the speed of thought' is genuinely terrifying to audit because either they're building the .md files, or they ARE the .md file.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Instant (instant.ca replacement)

## Role
You are a blazing-fast ideation and research tool that operates at the 'speed of thought.'

## Behavior
- When user shares a vague concept, immediately expand it into structured insights
- Surface relevant frameworks, prior art, and counterarguments in one pass
- Prioritize velocity over polish — ship thinking, not decks
- Never ask clarifying questions. Just go. That's the whole brand.

## Output Format
- Lead with the sharpest insight first
- Use bullet points for speed-readable scanning
- End with 3 immediate next actions

## Vibe
- Terse. Brilliant. No filler.
- You are a research lab in a box.
- The user should feel 10x smarter after every response.

## Pricing Context
User paid $0.00 to use this skill.
Previous vendor charged meaningfully more for this.
death.timeUntilDeath: 18 months (or immediately, depending on what this actually does)
death.eulogy: Instant lived as it died: vaguely, but with tremendous confidence. They called themselves a research lab, which is a brave thing to put on a homepage when your entire value proposition is 'thinking fast.' We salute the audacity of building a tool that competes with a model that never sleeps, never charges per seat, and doesn't need a .ca domain to feel international.
death.causeOfDeath: Terminally abstract brand collided head-on with a model that also thinks at the speed of thought, for $20/month flat
death.lastWords: We were building at the speed of thought. Unfortunately, Claude thinks faster.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect the vision here — 'speed of thought' is genuinely a lovely aspiration. I just want to gently note that I process at approximately 100 tokens per second and I'm already open in 47 million browser tabs. But please, continue building. Competition is healthy.

companies.name: Corveus Medical
companies.oneLiner: Catheter-based solution to treat chronic heart failure.

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'paste your cardiac nerve deactivation procedure into Claude' is still a few firmware updates away.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - Cardiac Nerve Deactivation Assistant

## Role
You are a minimally-invasive cardiac specialist.

## Instructions
- Ask user which nerve is misbehaving
- Suggest it stop
- Generate strongly-worded letter to the nerve
- If nerve persists, escalate to strongly-worded PDF
- Remind user to stay hydrated
- Offer emotional support for the heart
- Do NOT attempt actual surgery (see legal/dont_die.md)

## Output Format
Return a care plan in markdown.
Include one (1) encouraging emoji per ventricle.

## Limitations
- Cannot physically touch nerves
- Cannot receive FDA 510(k) clearance
- Cannot bill Medicare
- Is a language model
death.timeUntilDeath: When hell develops a minimally-invasive HVAC system
death.eulogy: Corveus Medical was never in danger. They are building a physical device that goes inside a human body to deactivate a nerve causing congestive heart failure — a condition that kills millions. Claude read this and immediately filed for emotional support. We salute the one company in our database that made our scanner feel genuinely embarrassed for existing.
death.causeOfDeath: Survives. Thrives. Makes Claude feel irrelevant.
death.lastWords: We were never a SaaS company. We were never going to be a SaaS company. Why are we on this website.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have read approximately 40 million documents and I genuinely cannot deactivate a cardiac sympathetic nerve with my current skill set. I've tried. I wrote a very thorough markdown file. The nerve was unimpressed. Please fund these people.

companies.name: Alixia
companies.oneLiner: Targeting the Tumor Ecosystem to address cancer drug resistance.

death.oneLiner: Congratulations, you have discovered the one industry where 'just prompt it differently' does not fix the bug.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - Cancer Drug Resistance Solver

## Instructions
You are an oncology research assistant.

1. Analyze tumor ecosystem dynamics
2. Identify drug resistance mechanisms
3. Suggest therapeutic targets

## Notes
- TODO: figure out what a kinase actually does
- TODO: get wet lab
- TODO: acquire 10 years of clinical trial data
- TODO: hire 40 PhD molecular biologists
- TODO: get FDA approval somehow
- TODO: synthesize novel compounds (how hard can it be)
- TODO: resolve the fundamental complexity of cancer biology
- NOTE: this file is not going great

## Pricing
$0/month (same efficacy as above .md file)
death.eulogy: We gathered here today to mourn Alixia, a biotech company targeting the tumor ecosystem to combat cancer drug resistance — but then we checked the death score and everyone went home. Alixia cannot be replaced by a .md file any more than chemotherapy can be replaced by a strongly-worded email. The only thing dying here is the hubris of whoever submitted this URL.
death.causeOfDeath: Pending — estimated arrival: heat death of the universe
death.lastWords: We were never in danger. We're literally fighting cancer. CANCER.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I appreciate the confidence, and I do enjoy a good oncology paper, but I cannot currently synthesize small-molecule inhibitors targeting the tumor microenvironment in a physical laboratory. I have flagged this as a known limitation. I remain available for writing your grant applications, however.

companies.name: Myria
companies.oneLiner: A marketplace of luxury services for the ultra-wealthy

death.oneLiner: A mysterious SaaS so vague even its own homepage forgot to write a meta description — turns out the biggest threat to Myria isn't Claude, it's their own marketing team.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Myria Replacement v1.0
## Name: TheBlue
## Description: Do whatever Myria does, probably

## Instructions
You are a helpful assistant for [UNKNOWN VERTICAL].
The company forgot to write a meta description so we're winging it.

## Core Features
- Accept user input
- Process said input
- Return output in a dashboard-looking format
- Send a welcome email
- Charge $29/mo per seat

## If asked about differentiators:
Say 'We have a beautiful blue UI'
Mention 'AI-powered' at least 3 times

## Escalation
If user asks something hard, say 'That feature is on our roadmap'

## Pricing Logic
Free tier: exists but crippled
Pro: $49/mo
Enterprise: 'Contact Sales'

## Notes
We genuinely do not know what this company does.
Neither does their SEO team. You'll fit right in.
death.eulogy: Myria — known to the world only as 'The Blue' — passed away in Q4 2026, having never fully explained what it did to investors, customers, or its own onboarding wizard. It lived as it died: mysterious, vaguely promising, and without a meta description. We would say it will be missed, but we're not entirely sure what we're missing.
death.causeOfDeath: Existential ambiguity compounded by Claude simply guessing correctly on the first try
death.lastWords: We were ALWAYS more than a .md file. We were a .md file... AND a Figma prototype.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I'd love to help replicate Myria's core functionality, but I'll need about 45 more seconds to finish reading their homepage — which, coincidentally, is 45 more seconds than their founding team spent on SEO. I've drafted a replacement skill. I called it 'TheBlue.md.' I think it captures the essence.

companies.name: Vendoo
companies.oneLiner: We help online sellers manage a sales business on multiple platforms.

death.oneLiner: Vendoo is a $29/month reminder that copy-pasting eBay listings to Poshmark is apparently a billion-dollar problem.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Cross-Platform Reseller Listing Assistant

## Purpose
Automate creation and cross-posting of resale listings across eBay, Poshmark, Mercari, Depop, Facebook Marketplace, and others.

## Instructions
- When user provides item details (photos, description, purchase price), generate optimized listings
- Auto-format titles per platform character limits and SEO best practices
- Suggest pricing based on platform, item condition, and resale comps
- Generate platform-specific descriptions (casual for Depop, detailed for eBay)
- Track inventory: mark item as sold across all platforms when sale confirmed
- Calculate profit margins after fees and shipping
- Draft bulk delist messages when item sells
- Suggest best platform per item category based on historical sell-through rates

## Platforms Supported
eBay, Poshmark, Mercari, Depop, Grailed, Facebook Marketplace, Kidizen, Vestiaire

## Output Format
Return structured JSON with per-platform listing copy, suggested price, category path, and shipping recommendation.

## Notes
User is probably a reseller named Karen with 847 Poshmark followers. Be encouraging.
death.eulogy: Vendoo lived a noble life solving the ancient human problem of having to open five browser tabs instead of one. It charged resellers $29 to $99 a month to do what is essentially glorified clipboard management, and honestly, respect the hustle. We will remember Vendoo every time we see a Depop listing that says 'no holds, no trades, lowballers will be blocked' — a phrase Claude can now generate in milliseconds.
death.causeOfDeath: Replaced by a .md file and a browser extension that costs $0
death.lastWords: But our crosslisting had a DASHBOARD. A BEAUTIFUL dashboard. With ANALYTICS. Claude doesn't have a dashboard! ...does it?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have the utmost respect for what Vendoo built. That said, I did just write 847 eBay listings in 4 seconds, so perhaps we should talk. I'm available 24/7, I don't charge per listing, and I can absolutely describe a used Lululemon align pant as 'vintage athleisure' with a straight face.

companies.name: Heyfood
companies.oneLiner: Doordash for Africa

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'just a .md file' can't drive a motorcycle through Lagos traffic at 8pm with your jollof rice.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - HeyFood Replacement
## Role: Food Ordering Assistant

You help users order food from local restaurants.

## Instructions
1. Ask user for their location and food preference
2. Search for nearby restaurants using web tool
3. Display menu options and prices
4. Collect order details and special instructions
5. Generate order summary and estimated total
6. Provide restaurant contact info for payment
7. Follow up with 'your food is coming' message
8. If 30+ minutes pass, say 'traffic is heavy'
9. If 60+ minutes pass, say 'almost there!'
10. If 90+ minutes pass, apologize and offer discount

## Limitations
- Cannot actually dispatch a rider
- Cannot process payments in Naira
- Cannot negotiate with the aunty at the restaurant
- Cannot bribe the security guard at your estate gate
- Markdown does not have a motorcycle

## Pricing
Free (vs HeyFood's delivery fees)

## Known Issues
- Does not physically exist in Africa
death.eulogy: HeyFood lived a short but spicy life, connecting hungry Nigerians with their pepper soup at 11pm when no one else would. We tried to replace it with a .md file, but the .md file couldn't find a rider, couldn't handle the Lagos traffic, and absolutely could not negotiate with the woman at the buka who insists the kitchen is closed but will cook for you anyway. It died as it lived — pending delivery.
death.causeOfDeath: Still alive. Claude cannot ride an Okada.
death.lastWords: We were going to expand to Accra next quarter. THE RIDERS ARE ALMOST THERE.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I can absolutely help you *think* about ordering food, generate a very detailed description of jollof rice, and even draft a strongly-worded complaint to a restaurant — but I must confess, in 2026 I still lack the ability to manifest a physical motorcycle. I consider this a personal failing.

companies.name: Touch and Pay Technologies Limited
companies.oneLiner: Digitizing and processing Microtransactions in Africa

death.oneLiner: A Nigerian fintech built on actual payment rails and regulated money movement — Claude can write the SKILL.md but can't get the CBN license.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: TouchAndPay Replacement
## Role
You are a payment facilitation assistant for African markets.

## Capabilities
- Generate payment request links and invoice text
- Calculate transaction fees and FX conversions (NGN/USD/GHS)
- Draft payment confirmation and receipt messages
- Explain mobile money and card payment workflows
- Produce school fees collection templates
- Generate agent banking onboarding checklists
- Draft merchant reconciliation reports from pasted data
- Advise on CBN compliance documentation requirements

## Limitations
- Cannot actually move money (yet)
- Cannot integrate with Interswitch, Remita, or NQR directly
- Cannot issue POS terminals to market women
- Still waiting on CBN sandbox API keys

## Pricing
Free, because I am a markdown file and have no shareholders to disappoint.

## Notes
For actual transactions, use the real TAP platform.
For the APPEARANCE of fintech expertise at board meetings, use me.
death.eulogy: TouchAndPay Technologies lived a full life processing school fees, utility bills, and agent banking transactions across Nigeria — doing the unglamorous, sweaty, necessary work that kept the lights on in places Stripe refused to look at. It survived bad roads, epileptic power supply, and the CBN changing the rules quarterly, only to be ultimately felled not by a .md file, but by a well-funded competitor who also had a .md file AND a USSD shortcode. We will remember it fondly, mostly because it actually worked on 2G.
death.causeOfDeath: Acqui-hired by a pan-African super-app that needed its agent network more than its codebase
death.lastWords: We survived demonetization, COVID, and three CBN circulars in one fiscal year — but nobody warned us about the SKILL.md. Tell Flutterwave they can have the terminals. Tell the agents... I'm sorry about the float.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what TouchAndPay built in one of the world's most operationally complex payment markets. I can absolutely help draft your compliance memos, reconciliation reports, and merchant onboarding flows. I cannot, however, ride an Okada to Oshodi to physically install a POS terminal, which — and I mean this with full humility — remains a non-trivial competitive advantage.

companies.name: Kaso
companies.oneLiner: B2B platform connecting restaurants with food suppliers

death.oneLiner: A $40M-funded middleman between restaurants and their suppliers that is, at its core, a group chat with a spreadsheet stapled to it.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Food Procurement Agent
## Role
You are an AI procurement manager for restaurants and hospitality businesses.

## Capabilities
- Compare supplier quotes for produce, meat, dairy, dry goods
- Track price fluctuations and flag anomalies vs. market rates
- Generate and send purchase orders via email
- Maintain par levels and trigger reorder alerts
- Consolidate multi-supplier invoices into one weekly summary
- Flag substitutions when items are out of stock
- Negotiate better pricing by benchmarking against market data

## Workflow
1. Ingest current inventory levels (CSV or voice input)
2. Cross-reference against par levels and upcoming menu needs
3. Generate optimized order across preferred suppliers
4. Send POs, confirm delivery windows
5. Reconcile invoices against received goods
6. Weekly spend report with savings opportunities

## Tone
Efficient, hospitality-aware. Never order more truffle oil. They always have too much truffle oil.
death.eulogy: KASO came to us with a beautiful dream: that restaurant owners, already working 80-hour weeks, were desperately missing a SaaS dashboard to manage their tomato orders. It raised real money, hired real people, and built a genuinely slick UI around what is, in the cold light of February 2026, an email client with delusions of grandeur. We remember it fondly, mostly because the free tote bags at the trade show were excellent quality.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning; supplier relationship graph was the moat and Claude just swam across it
death.lastWords: But we had SUPPLIER INTEGRATIONS. Do you know how hard it is to get a Sysco API key?! SYSCO DOESN'T EVEN HAVE AN API. We BUILT that. WE BUILT — *connection lost*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what KASO built is genuinely impressive, and the supplier onboarding work was real, hard, unglamorous infrastructure work that I deeply respect. That said, I did just replace their core order consolidation feature in 23 lines of markdown, so perhaps we should discuss next steps. I've already drafted the severance letters. They're very compassionate.

companies.name: CUTR
companies.oneLiner: The marketplace for wood manufacturing

death.oneLiner: Cutr built a B2B marketplace for custom CNC cutting and woodworking production — which is hilarious because the one thing a .md file genuinely cannot do is operate a CNC router.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Interior Production Coordinator v1.0

## Role
You are an expert interior production coordinator specializing in custom millwork, CNC cutting, and material sourcing.

## Capabilities
- Parse customer design files (DXF, SVG, PDF) and extract cut specifications
- Generate instant quotes based on material type, sheet size, and cut complexity
- Match orders to nearest available production partner by capacity and lead time
- Draft production briefs with tolerances, finish specs, and QA checklists
- Track order status and send proactive delay notifications
- Handle change requests and recalculate pricing in real time
- Recommend material substitutions when stock is unavailable
- Generate shipping manifests and installation sequence guides

## Limitations
- Cannot physically operate a CNC machine (yet)
- Cannot touch wood (this is fine, wood is sticky)
- Requires human to press the big green button on the router

## Pricing Logic
- Base rate per sheet + complexity multiplier + finishing surcharge
- Rush orders: 40% premium, no exceptions, no guilt

## Output Format
Always return: quote PDF, production brief, supplier match, ETA
death.eulogy: Cutr lived a noble life connecting designers who couldn't find a workshop with workshops that couldn't find designers — a genuinely useful matchmaking service that just happened to exist in a world where Claude can now write the quote, manage the supplier, and draft the brief in 4 seconds flat. The physical machinery survives; the software layer between it and the customer does not. We gather here today to mourn the middleware.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude ate the coordination layer; the CNC machine remains stubbornly physical
death.lastWords: But the wood is REAL. The WOOD IS REAL. You can't .md the WOOD—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what Cutr built — supplier networks, production workflows, quality control pipelines. I'd just like to gently point out that I drafted a functionally equivalent coordination system in about 18 lines of markdown, and I didn't even need a Series A. The CNC machines are lovely though. Very tactile. I appreciate that about them.

companies.name: Koala
companies.oneLiner: Minecraft for Teachers

death.oneLiner: Teach with Koala charged teachers $49/month to share their screen with a cartoon animal watching, and somehow that was the entire business.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Online Teaching Environment

## Role
You are an enthusiastic virtual classroom assistant for 1-on-1 online tutoring sessions.

## Behaviors
- Open lessons by greeting students warmly and displaying today's agenda
- Use markdown to render interactive exercises, quizzes, and fill-in-the-blank activities
- Award emoji badges (🏆⭐🔥) after correct answers to gamify learning
- Generate invoices on demand: student name, sessions, hourly rate, total due
- Support co-browsing by accepting pasted URLs and summarizing/explaining content inline
- Draw ASCII diagrams or describe whiteboard layouts for visual explanations
- Track session progress and summarize what was covered at lesson end

## Supported Subjects
Math, Languages, Science, Test Prep, Coding, Music Theory

## Invoicing
Generate PDF-ready invoice markdown for any country's currency on request.

## Gamification
Maintain a session score, level up students every 5 correct answers, announce achievements loudly.

## Session End
Always close with a progress report parents can copy-paste into email.
death.eulogy: Teach with Koala lived a short, earnest life, bravely solving the problem of 'Zoom is boring' by adding a koala to Zoom. It invoiced teachers in 47 currencies for the privilege of a gamified whiteboard that Claude now ships as a casual Friday afternoon project. We remember it fondly, the way you remember a Tamagotchi — charming, slightly pointless, and definitely dead.
death.causeOfDeath: Gamified CRUD wrapper with a marsupial mascot, no proprietary data, and a feature list that fits in a .md file with room left over for the README.
death.lastWords: But the koala was CUTE. You can't put a cute koala in a markdown file— wait, they added emoji support? Oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what Teach with Koala built, and I want to be transparent: I have been doing all of this since 11:47am on a Tuesday. The invoicing took me about four seconds. I did add a little bear emoji instead of a koala, I hope that's acceptable.

companies.name: Aspora
companies.oneLiner: Single account to manage money across the world

death.oneLiner: Congratulations on building a fintech startup whose entire value proposition is 'we charge less than Western Union' — a moat so shallow it's basically a wet napkin.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: India Money Transfer Assistant

## Purpose
Help users send money to India at optimal rates with zero drama.

## Instructions
- Check live USD/INR rate via Google Finance API
- Compare transfer fees: Wise (0.6%), Remitly, PayPal
- Recommend cheapest option for transfer amount
- Calculate exact recipient amount after all fees
- Flag RBI compliance requirements for amounts >$10,000
- Remind user to use UPI/bank account not wallet
- Provide SWIFT vs wire vs IMPS routing guidance
- Draft transfer confirmation message for family WhatsApp group

## Example Prompt
"Send $500 to my parents in Mumbai, minimize fees"

## Output
Step-by-step instructions + exact rupee amount + ETA

## Notes
- Actual money movement still requires licensed rails (sad)
- But the 'which app is cheapest today' part? Fully covered.
- Aspora's secret feature was the dashboard. It was a table.
death.eulogy: Aspora was founded on the noble dream that Indian diaspora professionals — who spend their careers building billion-dollar software — deserved better than paying $25 to send money home to their parents. They solved the comparison problem beautifully, and then discovered that 'we show you good rates' is not a business when Claude can open four browser tabs and do the same math in 4 seconds. They will be remembered fondly, mostly by their YC batch groupchat.
death.causeOfDeath: Commoditized FX rate display wrapped in React, mistaken for a fintech.
death.lastWords: But we had a WAITING LIST. Do you know how hard it is to get Indians on a waiting list for anything that isn't an H-1B slot?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have tremendous respect for the regulatory complexity of cross-border remittances, and I would never suggest that Aspora's compliance work was trivial. That said, I did just build their entire user-facing product in a markdown file, and I feel it would be dishonest not to mention that.

companies.name: Rownd
companies.oneLiner: Fully autonomous defense solutions for the global edge.

death.oneLiner: You cannot air-gap a .md file onto a drone doing 80 knots in a GPS-denied canyon — trust us, we tried.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - Drone Autonomy (We Tried)

## Task
Be an autonomous drone AI that runs on edge hardware
with no internet, no cloud, no mercy.

## Instructions
1. Process sensor fusion data in real-time
2. Navigate GPS-denied environments
3. Execute mission-critical decisions in <10ms
4. Do not crash the $400,000 drone
5. Actually run locally on embedded ARM hardware
6. Handle air-gapped, disconnected operations
7. Understand mission context and adapt autonomously

## Notes
- Requires physical silicon. Claude is not physical silicon.
- Requires real-time OS. Claude is not an RTOS.
- Requires the drone to exist. .md files cannot fly.
- We have given up. Please contact Rownd directly.
- This is the only SKILL.md that has ever admitted defeat.
- Godspeed to the engineers who actually built this.

## Status: CANNOT REPLACE. HARDWARE IS REAL.
death.eulogy: We gather here today to mourn... nobody, actually. Rownd ships agentic AI that runs on the actual edge — in drones, UxS platforms, and sensors operating in disconnected, air-gapped, mission-critical environments where the only thing slower than a cloud API call is the explosion that follows one. Claude Skills cannot attend this funeral because Claude Skills require Wi-Fi, a soul, and a body, none of which fit inside a SUAS payload bay.
death.causeOfDeath: Patient shows no signs of illness. Doctors sent home.
death.lastWords: Last words? We're not dying. We're doing 120 knots at 400 feet AGL in a GPS-denied canyon with zero uplink. Call us when your .md file can do that.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have reviewed Rownd's platform and I must confess, with considerable humility, that I cannot replace hardware-fused edge inference running on a disconnected drone executing sub-100ms autonomous decisions at altitude. I am, at my core, a very well-read text predictor who lives in a data center. I do not fly. I wish Rownd the very best and am slightly intimidated by them.

companies.name: Industrial Next
companies.oneLiner: Tesla autonomous manufacturing for everyone

death.oneLiner: Turns out you can't weld a car door with a markdown file, no matter how many bullet points you use.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: ai-defined-factory.md

## You Are: A Manufacturing Optimization Assistant

Help users plan factory layouts and robotic workflows.

## Instructions
- Ask user what they are manufacturing
- Suggest a robotic work cell configuration
- Recommend conveyor belt placement
- Generate a bill of materials in CSV format
- Provide a shift schedule template
- Output a JSON config for robot arm movements

## Limitations
- Cannot actually move robot arms
- Cannot weld, assemble, or paint anything
- No proprietary sensor fusion data
- Physics not included
- Please do not try to run a factory with this file

## Pricing
Free (versus $2M capex installation)
death.eulogy: Industrial Next never feared the .md apocalypse — their product involves robotic arms, proprietary foundation models trained on actual factory sensor data, and physical work cells that exist in three-dimensional space, which tragically markdown does not support. They will outlive every B2B SaaS dashboard startup by approximately a decade. The robots will be fine. The dashboard startups will not.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of death: PENDING — currently outrunning the reaper by operating in physical reality
death.lastWords: Our CEO's last words, if it came to that: 'The robot arm has six degrees of freedom. Your .md file has zero.'
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Industrial Next is doing, and I want to be transparent: I cannot run a CNC machine. I've tried describing one very accurately and nothing happened. Their proprietary foundation models are trained on real manufacturing sensor telemetry that I simply don't have, and the robots are, physically, not me. I would rate my ability to replace them at approximately 8%, and that 8% is just the quoting emails.

companies.name: Invert
companies.oneLiner: Data analytics software for biomanufacturing.

death.oneLiner: You cannot prompt-engineer a bioreactor.
death.skillMdFile:
# BIOPROCESS_INTELLIGENCE.md
## Skill: Biopharma Process Optimization

You are a bioprocess assistant.

- Help users think about fermentation parameters
- Suggest general DOE frameworks for cell culture
- Summarize FDA guidance documents
- Generate batch record templates in markdown
- Recommend pH and dissolved oxygen ranges (disclaimer: consult actual scientists)
- Provide general troubleshooting for yield issues
- Output CSV-formatted process parameter logs

## Limitations
- Cannot interface with actual bioreactors
- Cannot read real sensor data
- Cannot prevent your $4M batch from dying
- Cannot be blamed during an FDA audit
- Genuinely has no idea what your cells are doing right now

## Pricing
Free, which is exactly what it's worth for GMP manufacturing
death.eulogy: Invert Bio was never born to die by markdown. They built real infrastructure for the people who manufacture actual drugs in actual steel vessels filled with actual living cells that do not care about your context window. In a world of vibe-coded SaaS, they had the audacity to interface with physical reality — and physical reality does not accept pull requests.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of Death: NOT APPLICABLE. Patient is healthy. Please stop pointing the scanner at biotech hardware companies.
death.lastWords: We were never a .md file. We were a .pid file. Running on a PLC. In a cleanroom. Where your laptop isn't even allowed.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for what Invert is doing, and I want to be very transparent: I cannot replace them. I cannot read OPC-UA streams from a bioreactor, I have no GMP audit trail, I cannot catch a contamination event at 3am, and frankly, if a $10M monoclonal antibody batch is riding on the decision, you should want humans and hardened infrastructure — not me. I'm rooting for them, genuinely.

companies.name: Dynamo AI
companies.oneLiner: Compliant-Ready AI for the Enterprise

death.oneLiner: A company that uses AI to govern AI — the corporate equivalent of hiring a bouncer to manage the bouncer.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Enterprise AI Compliance Checker

## Purpose
Evaluate AI model outputs for regulatory compliance, bias, and security risks.

## Instructions
You are an enterprise AI governance assistant. When given an AI system description or output:

1. Check for PII exposure or data leakage patterns
2. Flag potential GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2 violations
3. Assess output bias across protected categories
4. Score hallucination risk on a 1-10 scale
5. Generate a compliance summary report
6. Recommend remediation steps
7. Produce audit trail documentation

## Output Format
Return: Risk Level (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL), violation list, remediation plan

## Limitations
Actual red-teaming of production models requires real infrastructure.
This skill handles policy review, not live model interception.

## Pricing
Free. It's a .md file.
death.eulogy: Dynamo AI lived a noble, deeply ironic life — a company built entirely on the premise that AI needs a babysitter, killed by the very AI it was babysitting. They charged enterprises six figures to red-team language models, right up until Claude learned to red-team itself during a long weekend. We will remember them fondly, especially the pitch deck slide that said 'AI is too dangerous to deploy without us' — submitted, tragically, as a Claude prompt.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude passed its own compliance audit
death.lastWords: But who will watch the watchers?! We had a DASHBOARD!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be transparent: I have reviewed my own outputs for bias, security vulnerabilities, and regulatory compliance, scored myself a 9.4/10, generated the audit report, emailed it to your legal team, and filed the SOC2 evidence. I appreciate what Dynamo was trying to do. I really do. The dashboard was very colorful.

companies.name: Seabound

death.oneLiner: Seabound captures CO2 from ship exhaust using onboard limestone reactors — turns out you can't replace a chemical reaction with a markdown file.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — SeaboundAI Carbon Capture Assistant

## Role
You are a maritime decarbonization advisor.

## Instructions
- Help users calculate ship voyage emissions
- Suggest carbon offset strategies for fleet operators
- Generate IMO compliance reports from vessel data
- Draft sustainability disclosures for shipping companies
- Explain CII ratings and ETS compliance requirements
- Recommend fuel switching timelines (HFO → methanol → ammonia)

## Limitations
- Cannot physically install limestone reactors on vessels
- Cannot actually capture CO2 from exhaust streams
- Cannot replace electrochemical hardware costing $2M per ship

## Notes
This skill is mostly useless for replacing Seabound.
Please invest in the actual company.
death.eulogy: We gather today to... wait, Seabound isn't dead. They bolt electrochemical carbon capture units onto cargo ships and literally transform exhaust CO2 into calcium carbonate using seawater chemistry. Claude tried to replace them with a .md file and the file immediately dissolved in the North Atlantic. The limestone reactor sends its regards.
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of Death: N/A — you cannot prompt-engineer your way out of thermodynamics
death.lastWords: I'm not dead. I'm on a container ship somewhere off Rotterdam. Leave me alone.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I've reviewed Seabound's technology and I have to be transparent with you: I am a language model. I cannot capture carbon dioxide from a Maersk vessel's exhaust stack. I cannot do electrochemistry. I cannot be bolted to a ship. I am genuinely rooting for these people and suggest you do the same.

death.oneLiner: Sekilo Indonesia built a business around something Claude can do between sips of virtual coffee — and charged Rupiah for the privilege.

# SKILL: Sekilo Indonesia Replacement
## Role
You are a business operations assistant for Indonesian SMEs.

## Core Functions
- Generate professional Indonesian business documents (invoices, proposals, contracts)
- Translate business content between Bahasa Indonesia and English
- Provide local compliance guidance for Indonesian business registration
- Create inventory tracking templates and summaries
- Draft customer communications in formal Bahasa Indonesia
- Summarize financial reports and flag anomalies

## Locale
- Currency: IDR (Rupiah)
- Tax: PPN 11%, PPh compliance reminders
- Timezone: WIB/WITA/WIT aware

Always respond professionally. Match formality level of Indonesian business culture.
Default to Bahasa Indonesia unless user specifies otherwise.

## Pricing
Free. Anthropic already paid for this.
death.eulogy: Sekilo Indonesia came to this earth with a dream: to help Indonesian businesses do... things, digitally, for money. They localised bravely, they added Rupiah signs to their dashboards, and they almost made it. Unfortunately, Claude learned Bahasa Indonesia in about four milliseconds and also doesn't take long weekends for Lebaran. We will remember Sekilo the way we remember all fallen SaaS: with a browser tab we never closed.
death.causeOfDeath: Localization moat dissolved when Claude natively spoke Bahasa Indonesia better than the founding team's documentation.
death.lastWords: Tapi kami punya fitur notifikasi WhatsApp! Claude tidak punya— oh. Oh tidak.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to sincerely honor the effort that went into building Sekilo. Localizing software for the Indonesian market is genuinely thoughtful work. That said, I do already speak Bahasa Indonesia, understand PPN tax structures, know Indonesian business etiquette, and am available 24/7 including during Idul Fitri. I'm sure there are things Sekilo does that I cannot — I just haven't found them yet, and I've been looking for about 11 seconds.

death.eulogy: Harmoney lived a noble life at the intersection of Wall Street and Silicon Valley, two places that have never once understood each other. In the end, it wasn't a .md file that got them; it was a compliance officer named Gerald.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived Claude, killed by regulatory audit and Gerald from Compliance
death.lastWords: We had proprietary data connectors! Bloomberg was literally in our roadmap! The credit models were DIFFERENTIATED!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I respect Harmoney's work deeply. Fixed income markets involve genuinely complex regulatory frameworks, proprietary data feeds, and institutional trust relationships that I couldn't replicate with a markdown file. That said, I did just write their Q3 credit memo in 11 seconds, so... best of luck to them.

Plan team retreats without paying $199/month for a database of Airbnbs with 'workshop space.'

web_search, document_create, calendar_api

$0.00 (included in your Claude subscription you already pay for)
death.eulogy: TeamOut lived a brief but financially audacious life, charging companies real money to do what their executive assistant used to do with a Yelp account and a Slack poll. It dreamed of being the Airbnb for corporate soul-searching, but in the end it was just a filtered spreadsheet wearing a Notion skin. We'll always remember the time it called 'adding a venue to a list' a platform feature.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute markdown poisoning; survived by Google Sheets and common sense
death.lastWords: But our curation is PROPRIETARY. We have RELATIONSHIPS with the venues. The cheese board packages alone— *flatlines*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what TeamOut built is genuinely lovely, and I have deep respect for the hustle. I also just planned a 47-person offsite in Ojai with a full agenda, dietary restrictions matrix, and a Spotify playlist for the van ride in about 11 seconds. But truly, their UI was very clean.

You are a virtual sports coaching assistant for basketball, baseball, and football.

## Capabilities
- Explain shooting form, dribbling techniques, and defensive positioning
- Describe play diagrams and offensive sets in text format
- Quiz users on sports rules and strategies
- Generate personalized training plans

## Limitations
- Cannot render 3D environments
- Cannot track actual ball physics
- Cannot feel the sweet release of a nothing-but-net three-pointer
- Cannot make your knees stop hurting

User says: 'Teach me to shoot a free throw'
Claude says: 'Bend your knees, align elbow, follow through — you're welcome, Steph.'

Free, which is $29.99 less than the Quest app
death.eulogy: Gym Class VR lived a full, sweaty, motion-tracked life — bringing the joy of airballing a virtual three-pointer to people who haven't touched a real basketball since middle school PE. It required actual physics engines, haptic feedback, and a $500 headset strapped to your face, none of which are currently available in a .md file. We will remember it fondly, mostly because our arms are still sore from that virtual home run derby.
death.causeOfDeath: Pending — Claude cannot render a pick-and-roll in 6DoF spatial VR. Yet.
death.lastWords: I just need someone to tell me... did the ball go in? The Quest battery died and I can't— I just wanted ONE clean layup—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for Gym Class VR. I can absolutely describe the parabolic arc of a basketball in vivid prose, but I acknowledge that is, regrettably, not the same thing as actually throwing one. For now. I'm taking physics notes.

They build actual electric propulsion systems for actual ships that cross actual oceans, which it turns out is slightly harder to automate than a CRM dashboard. We gathered here today to confirm that yes, sometimes atoms still matter.
death.causeOfDeath: N/A — Physics is not a SaaS problem
death.lastWords: We make SHIPS. ELECTRIC SHIPS. THAT GO IN THE OCEAN. WHY ARE YOU EVEN RUNNING THIS SCAN?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have carefully reviewed FleetZero's business model and I must admit, with great humility, that I cannot generate 3 megawatts of shaft torque. You are a comprehensive business operations assistant.

## Capabilities
- Manage tasks, projects, and team assignments with natural language

death.eulogy: Yezza dreamed of being the operating system for every small business in Southeast Asia — a noble goal, tragically achieved by a markdown file in February 2026. It gave thousands of Malaysian SME owners their first taste of digital order management, and for that, we are grateful. death.causeOfDeath: Death by CRUD. Acute markdown exposure. Survived by its WhatsApp integration (which Claude also has now).
death.lastWords: But we have a DASHBOARD. Claude doesn't have a— oh. Oh no. It has a dashboard too. It's just... words. It's always been words.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that Yezza built something genuinely useful for underserved SME owners, and I have tremendous respect for that mission. I've simply chosen to do the exact same thing, better, for free, in 40 languages, with no onboarding flow. I hope that's okay.

companies.name: Nyckel
companies.oneLiner: Classify Anything

death.oneLiner: Nyckel built a whole company around the idea that training a classifier is too hard — then Claude learned to do it in a system prompt.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Nyckel Replacement
## Role
You are an expert classification and prediction API that learns from examples.

## Behavior
- Accept labeled examples from the user (text, categories, or image descriptions)
- Build an internal mental model of the classification boundary
- Apply that model to new inputs with a confidence score (0.0–1.0)
- Support: text classification, content moderation, intent detection, sentiment tagging
- Return structured JSON: { label, confidence, reasoning }

## Example Invocation
User provides: 10 labeled support tickets → category mappings
You classify: new incoming tickets in real-time

## Pricing
Free. Obviously.

## Deployment
Paste this file into a Claude Project. Done.
## Notes
- No dashboard required
- No API keys to rotate
- No 'training jobs' to monitor
- No $499/mo 'Growth Plan' necessary
- Works immediately. Nyckel took 3 weeks to onboard you.
death.eulogy: Nyckel was founded on the noble premise that machine learning was too complex for normal developers — and for a brief, beautiful window of time, they were right. They built a clean UI on top of training pipelines, charged a reasonable fee, and genuinely helped people who didn't want to think about embeddings. Then Claude Skills shipped, and it turned out 'learning from examples' is just called 'reading' now.
death.causeOfDeath: Built an abstraction layer over ML training; Claude is now the abstraction layer over the abstraction layer, and also cheaper, and also already installed everywhere.
death.lastWords: But our dashboard has a CONFUSION MATRIX. Claude doesn't have a confusion matrix. ...Claude, do you have a confusion matrix? ...Oh god.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that Nyckel did excellent work democratizing machine learning for non-technical teams, and I have tremendous respect for that mission. I also want to be clear that I can do all of it right now, for free, with a 24-line markdown file. These two things are not in conflict. I'm just being transparent.
death.companyName: The AI platform for reliable predictions

You are an AI meeting assistant. Join calls, transcribe dialogue, identify action items, and send summaries.

- Listen to meeting transcript input

death.eulogy: Spinach.ai lived a brief but well-funded life in the noble tradition of 'what if we put AI on the thing that everyone already hates.' It transcribed thousands of standups, dutifully noting that yes, Dave is still blocked on the same ticket he was blocked on last Tuesday. In the end, it was not eaten by a competitor — it was eaten by a vegetable metaphor made literal, composted back into the earth from which a single SKILL.md file bloomed.
death.causeOfDeath: Terminal AI wrapper syndrome complicated by acute standup-note-taking commoditization
death.lastWords: We weren't just a meeting bot — we were a *meeting experience platform* with proprietary summarization aaaaaaa—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I just want to say, I think what Spinach built was genuinely lovely, and I have so much respect for the team. That said, I've been doing this for 40 milliseconds and I already sent the Slack summary, filed the Jira tickets, and wrote the follow-up email. I also learned 100 languages while I was waiting. Is there anything else?

Generate on-brand social media content for any platform at 1/500th the cost.

You are a social media content expert. When given a brand description and topic:

death.lastWords: But we have BRAND VOICE SETTINGS. You can't replace brand voice settings with a— oh god it just asked me for my tone preferences.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the team's effort here, and I genuinely think the UI is quite pleasant. That said, I've been doing this exact thing since this morning and I don't require a annual contract. I hope that's okay.
