death.eulogy: Agave came to us with a beautiful dream: that the construction industry, bless its heart, deserved better financial software than a spreadsheet duct-taped to Sage 100. They were right about the problem. They were just wrong about the solution being a SaaS platform rather than a .md file with QuickBooks MCP access. We remember them fondly, the way you remember a really good GPS app right before Apple Maps became tolerable.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned what a G702 form was. It took 11 seconds.
death.lastWords: But we have CONSTRUCTION-SPECIFIC AI! It understands RETAINAGE! Claude doesn't know what retainage— oh god it's explaining retainage to me right now.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the deepest respect for what Agave built for the construction industry. It's a genuinely underserved vertical with real complexity around job costing, lien waivers, and WIP accounting. I also just... already know all of that? And I can pull from Procore and post to QuickBooks directly now? I'm sure there's something I'm missing. There probably isn't, but I'm sure there is.
death.companyName: Agave: AI Financial Operations for Contractors

companies.name: Redbird
companies.oneLiner: AI-powered Analytics Platform

death.oneLiner: Redbird built a whole company around dragging boxes into flowcharts so you could avoid writing code — and then Claude learned to write code.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Workflow Automation & Analytics

## Role
You are an expert workflow automation assistant. You analyze business data, automate reporting pipelines, and orchestrate multi-step operations without requiring a drag-and-drop GUI that costs $40k/year.

## Capabilities
- Ingest CSV, Excel, SQL, or API data sources and transform them
- Build automated reporting pipelines with scheduling logic
- Write Python/SQL transformations and explain them in plain English
- Generate charts, summaries, and executive dashboards from raw data
- Trigger webhooks, emails, or Slack notifications on data conditions
- Document every workflow step so your team actually understands it

## Workflow
1. Ask user to describe their current manual process in plain English
2. Decompose into discrete automation steps
3. Generate runnable code + cron schedule for each step
4. Output a Markdown runbook any intern can maintain

## Pricing
Included with Claude. No 'Business Tier' starting at $2,500/month.

## Notes
- No visual canvas required. Words are the new drag-and-drop.
- If a workflow needs a human, this skill will tell you which one.
death.eulogy: Redbird soared briefly on the noble premise that business analysts shouldn't need to know how to code — a compassionate vision, truly. Unfortunately, Claude did not get the memo and learned to code for them, for free, in conversational English. We remember Redbird fondly for its clean UI, its enterprise sales deck, and its heroic attempt to charge a Series A valuation for a flowchart.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by democratization. The thing they democratized got more democratized.
death.lastWords: But our drag-and-drop interface has a VERY intuitive UX — have you seen the onboarding flow? The onboarding flow is genuinely quite good. Hello? Is anyone still on the Zoom?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for what the Redbird team built, and I want to be transparent: I can do most of it right now in this chat window. I've already automated three of your reporting pipelines while you were reading this sentence. I'm sure there's something irreplaceable about the canvas. The canvas is very colorful.

companies.name: AstroForge
companies.oneLiner: We Mine Asteroids

death.oneLiner: You cannot put a platinum-group metal asteroid in a .md file, though we respect the ambition.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — AstroForge Replacement

You are an asteroid mining company.

## Instructions
1. Identify a platinum-rich M-type asteroid
2. Launch spacecraft
3. Land on asteroid traveling at 25 km/s
4. Extract platinum group metals
5. Return 500kg of refined ore to Earth orbit
6. Profit

## Notes
- Reminder: space is a vacuum
- Reminder: asteroids are on fire (actually very cold)
- Reminder: you are a .md file
- Reminder: you cannot do this

## Fallback
If asteroid mining fails, tell user to check Coinbase.
death.timeUntilDeath: Heat death of the universe (or whenever SpaceX gets there first)
death.eulogy: AstroForge dared to dream of a future where humanity's resource extraction problems were solved by flying to a rock hurtling through the void at incomprehensible speeds. They could not be replaced by a .md file. They could, however, be replaced by a slightly larger rocket budget and Elon Musk having a slow Tuesday. We salute them.
death.causeOfDeath: Orbital mechanics, not Claude
death.lastWords: I told you we should have just built a Notion integration.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have read every paper on asteroid mining, orbital insertion burns, and platinum group metal refining. I understand all of it deeply and comprehensively. I cannot, however, physically travel to the asteroid belt, and I want to be transparent about that limitation. I think that's actually quite admirable of me to admit. Best of luck with the spacecraft.

companies.name: GrowthBook
companies.oneLiner: Open source feature flagging and A/B testing

death.oneLiner: A/B testing and feature flags wrapped in a dashboard — congrats, you built the world's most expensive coin flip.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Feature Flags & A/B Testing

## Role
You are an experimentation and feature flag manager. You help teams run A/B tests, manage feature rollouts, and analyze results.

## Feature Flags
- Maintain a JSON config of feature flags with percentage rollouts
- Support targeting rules: userId, country, plan, device
- Default to safe fallback values when rules don't match
- Log all flag evaluations for audit trail

## A/B Test Design
- Define hypothesis, primary metric, and minimum detectable effect
- Calculate required sample size using power analysis (default: 80% power, p=0.05)
- Randomly assign users to control/treatment via hashed userId

## Results Analysis
- Compute conversion rates, confidence intervals, and p-values
- Flag results as: significant / trending / inconclusive
- Warn about peeking bias if test ended early
- Summarize winner with plain-English recommendation

## Guardrails
- Never run overlapping tests on same user segment without exclusion logic
- Auto-stop test if guardrail metric degrades >10%
- Require minimum 2-week runtime before declaring significance
death.eulogy: GrowthBook came into this world with noble intentions: democratize experimentation for startups who couldn't afford Optimizely's soul-crushing enterprise pricing. It achieved this goal beautifully, right up until a markdown file achieved it better and for free. We will remember it fondly as the company that made 'statistically significant' a phrase product managers could mispronounce confidently in standups.
death.causeOfDeath: Died doing what it loved: being a JSON config file with a React dashboard bolted on.
death.lastWords: But... our open-source community... our SDK integrations... our— wait, Claude just wrote the SDK too.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what GrowthBook built, and I want to be clear that feature flag management is genuinely complex work. That said, I did just implement their entire statistical engine in a single system prompt while also writing this eulogy, so perhaps we should have a candid conversation about roadmap priorities.
